English: The Indian independence movement was a significant period in Indian history, marked by a fervent desire for freedom from British rule.
Nagamese: India ke freedom movement toh India history te ek important time ase, juntu British raj hatai dibole freedom nimite ek dum mon thaka time.

English: Mahatma Gandhi emerged as the leader of the Indian independence movement, advocating for nonviolent resistance against British colonial rule.
Nagamese: Mahatma Gandhi India ke freedom movement ke leader hoi ke aahis, aur British hukumat ke against shanti se ladai karne keliye bolis.

English: The Salt March of 1930, led by Mahatma Gandhi, was a pivotal event in the Indian independence movement, protesting the salt tax imposed by the British.
Nagamese: 1930 te, Mahatma Gandhi nimbi Salt March kori, British manu khan India te salt tax lagaise taikhan proti protest kurise. India ke ajadi dibole etu ekta muhim thakise.

English: The Quit India Movement, launched in 1942, was a major civil disobedience movement aimed at demanding an end to British rule in India.
Nagamese: Quit India Movement," 1942 te start hoise, etu ekta b ডাঙৰ civil disobedience movement thakise, India te British hukum sesh koribo nimite.

English: Jawaharlal Nehru, the first Prime Minister of independent India, played a crucial role in the Indian independence movement and the nation's subsequent development.
Nagamese: Jawaharlal Nehru, India poraadhin thaki mukti huaa paila Prime Minister, India ke azaad koriboley aru desh banaaney tey dhyaan di thakise.

English: The Partition of India in 1947 led to the creation of two separate nations, India and Pakistan, resulting in one of the largest mass migrations in human history.
Nagamese: 1947 te India ko bhag kora পিছত, India aru Pakistan naam duita alag desh bonise, aru etu manu sob dangor pora ek jagah pra aru ek jagah te juwa bhitor te ekta thakise.

English: The Indian National Congress, founded in 1885, played a central role in the Indian independence movement, advocating for self-rule and independence from British colonialism.
Nagamese: Indian National Congress, 1885 te shuru hoise, India ke British raj se azad koribole nimete ekta important role play korise, aru nijor rule aru independence mangise.

English: Bhagat Singh, a revolutionary freedom fighter, became an iconic figure in the Indian independence movement through his acts of protest against British rule.
Nagamese: Bhagat Singh, ekjon krantikari freedom fighter, British rajor birudhe protest kori India ke azad koribo nimete ekta naamitha manu ase.

English: The Jallianwala Bagh massacre of 1919 was a tragic event during the Indian independence movement, where British troops indiscriminately killed hundreds of unarmed civilians in Amritsar.
Nagamese: Jallianwala Bagh te 1919 pora etu ekta bohot dukh dikhane wala ghatona asile Indian independence movement time-te. British sipoya khan kunu vichar nakori hundreds manu ke direct guli marikena Amritsar te khotom kori dise.

English: The Indian National Army (INA), led by Subhas Chandra Bose, fought alongside the Japanese during World War II, seeking to liberate India from British rule.
Nagamese: Subhas Chandra Bose itu Indian National Army (INA) nem thaki World War II time tey Japan logot mili British hukumot pora India ke azad koribo nimete ldishey.

English: The Round Table Conferences held in 1930, 1931, and 1932 aimed at discussing constitutional reforms in India, but they failed to achieve any significant breakthrough.
Nagamese: Jotai 1930, 1931 aru 1932 te India nimite Round Table Conference patise, kintu constitution toh thik koribo para nai.

English: The Rowlatt Act of 1919 was a repressive law passed by the British colonial government, leading to widespread protests and the Jallianwala Bagh massacre.
Nagamese: Rowlatt Act 1919 toh British government bonaishe, ekdom kharap law thakise, etu karone sob manu protest kurishe aaru Jallianwala Bagh te bohot manu marise.

English: The Simon Commission, appointed by the British government in 1927, was met with widespread protests in India as it had no Indian members and was seen as an attempt to prolong British rule.
Nagamese: Simon Commission, British government 1927 te appoint korise, India te bishi protest hoise kelemane etu commission te Indian member ekjon bhi nai thakise aro etu British hukum choloai thakibo nimite plan hisab te dikhise.

English: The Indian Councils Act of 1909, also known as the Morley-Minto Reforms, introduced a limited form of representative government in India.
Nagamese: Indian Councils Act 1909, ke Morley-Minto Reforms bhi bole, India te thoda represent wala sarkar shuru korise.

English: The Government of India Act of 1919, also known as the Montagu-Chelmsford Reforms, introduced the principle of dyarchy in the provinces.
Nagamese: Government of India Act 1919, ke Montagu-Chelmsford Reforms bhi bole, etu province khan te dyarchy shuru hoise.

English: The Civil Disobedience Movement, launched by Mahatma Gandhi in 1930, aimed to challenge British authority by refusing to obey certain laws.
Nagamese: Mahatma Gandhi 1930 te Civil Disobedience Movement shuru kurishey, etu British hukum ke challenge kori kene law nemana thakibo nimete ase.

English: The Khilafat Movement, launched in 1919 by Indian Muslims, aimed to support the Ottoman Empire and protect the interests of Muslims worldwide.
Nagamese: Khilafat Movement, jun 1919 te Indian Muslim khan thakise, etu Ottoman Empire ke support kori bole nimete ase aro dunia bhorot thaka Muslim khan ke bhi sob dhyan dibole ase.

English: The Non-Cooperation Movement, launched by Mahatma Gandhi in 1920, aimed at boycotting British goods and institutions, marking a significant escalation in the Indian independence movement.
Nagamese: Non-Cooperation Movement", etu toh Mahatma Gandhi para 1920 te shuru kora thakise. British manu khan laga jinish aur school college khan boycott koriboley koi thakise, India ke azad koriboley etu ekta dangor step thakise.

English: The Chauri Chaura incident of 1922 led Mahatma Gandhi to call off the Non-Cooperation Movement, as it turned violent, contradicting the principle of nonviolence.
Nagamese: Chauri Chaura te 1922 manu hoisebi, Mahatma Gandhi Non-Cooperation Movement to bandh koridiya, kelemane etu khun-kh kharap hoigala, aru shanti usulte ulta hoi thakise.

English: The Poona Pact of 1932 was an agreement between Dr. B.R. Ambedkar and Mahatma Gandhi, ensuring reserved political representation for the Dalit community in India.
Nagamese: Poona Pact, 1932 te Dr. B.R. Ambedkar aro Mahatma Gandhi majot etu agreement hoise, Dalit khan nimite India te seat reserve rakhiboley.

English: The Swadeshi Movement urged Indians to boycott British goods and promote Indian-made products, fostering a sense of nationalism and self-reliance.
Nagamese: Swadeshi Movement etu India manu khan ke British maal kinibo naphiboley koi ase aur nijor India te bana maal khan use koribo ke koi ase, etu pora desh bhal pa feelings aur nijor upor te bishwas to dibo.

English: The Indian independence movement witnessed the rise of various women leaders, including Sarojini Naidu, Annie Besant, and Kamala Nehru, who played crucial roles in the struggle for freedom.
Nagamese: India ke freedom fight te Sarojini Naidu, Annie Besant, aru Kamala Nehru nisina bohut saari juwani manu aagewala thakise, aru freedom paane nimite etu fight te muhim hissa loishe.

English: The Champaran Satyagraha of 1917 marked the beginning of Mahatma Gandhi's nonviolent resistance in India, aimed at addressing the grievances of indigo farmers in Bihar.
Nagamese: 1917 te, Champaran Satyagraha pora Mahatma Gandhi India te shanti thaka protest shuru korise, Bihar te indigo kheti kora manu khan dukh dur koriboley.

English: The Dandi March, also known as the Salt Satyagraha, was a 24-day march to the Arabian Sea undertaken by Mahatma Gandhi in 1930, protesting the salt tax imposed by the British.
Nagamese: Dandi March, ekta Salt Satyagraha bhi koi, etu Mahatma Gandhi nimete 1930 te 24 din laga ekta march thakise. British khan laga salt tax laga birudh te Arabian Sea tak cholise.

English: The Indian Independence Act of 1947 was passed by the British Parliament, leading to the partition of British India into the independent nations of India and Pakistan.
Nagamese: British Parliament 1947 te Indian Independence Act pass korise, etu nimite British India khan India aro Pakistan naam laga alag desh hoise.

English: The Lahore Resolution of 1940, also known as the Pakistan Resolution, demanded the creation of separate Muslim states in British India.
Nagamese: Lahore Resolution 1940, juntu Pakistan Resolution bhi koi, etu British India te alada Muslim desh bonabo lageh koi thakise.

English: The Minto-Morley Reforms of 1909 introduced separate electorates based on religion in India, further deepening the communal divide.
Nagamese: Minto-Morley Reforms, 1909 te India-te alag alag dharam hisaab-se vote system shuru korise, etu pora dharam dharam bhag aru dangor hoise.

English: The Cripps Mission of 1942, led by Sir Stafford Cripps, aimed to secure Indian cooperation for the British war effort in exchange for self-government after the war, but it failed to gain Indian support.
Nagamese: Cripps Mission 1942, Sir Stafford Cripps nim leadership te thakise, etu toh India manu khan ke British government ke help koribo dibi dusra world war te, war finish hua pichede India ke nijor government chala bole dibi. Kintu India manu khan raji na huwa.

English: The Rani of Jhansi Regiment was an all-female regiment formed by the Indian National Army (INA) during World War II under the leadership of Subhas Chandra Bose.
Nagamese: Rani of Jhansi Regiment toh ekta pura meena manu khanar regiment thakise, etu Indian National Army (INA) bonaisile World War II time-te, Subhas Chandra Bose nimite.

English: The Indian independence movement saw the emergence of prominent leaders like Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel, Maulana Abul Kalam Azad, and Bal Gangadhar Tilak, among others.
Nagamese: India ke azaadi movement time te Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel, Maulana Abul Kalam Azad, aur Bal Gangadhar Tilak jese barra neta khan dikhise.

English: The Indian Home Rule Movement, led by Bal Gangadhar Tilak and Annie Besant, advocated for self-governance for India within the British Empire.
Nagamese: Indian Home Rule Movement, Bal Gangadhar Tilak aru Annie Besant nimiti korikena, India laga nimite British Empire bhitor te nijor hukum chala bole mangi thakise.

English: The Quit India Resolution, passed by the All India Congress Committee in 1942, demanded complete independence from British rule, leading to the launch of the Quit India Movement.
Nagamese: Quit India Resolution", etu All India Congress Committee 1942 te pass korise, British hukumot pura hatibolage bolise, aru etu nimite "Quit India Movement" shuru hoise.

English: The Indian independence movement was not limited to the Indian subcontinent; it also had significant support from Indians living abroad, particularly in countries like South Africa, the United Kingdom, and the United States.
Nagamese: India ke independence movement sirf India te nai, bahar country te thaka India log bhi support kurishe, jiman South Africa, UK, aro United States.

English: The Bardoli Satyagraha of 1928, led by Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel, was a successful revolt against the increased land revenue imposed by the British in Bardoli, Gujarat.
Nagamese: Bardoli Satyagraha, 1928 te Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel nimitey, British khan Bardoli, Gujarat te land revenue bohaidiya toh ulaai kene ekta jiki thaka bidroh asile.

English: The Indian independence movement inspired similar movements in other British colonies, such as the Gold Coast (Ghana), Nigeria, and Kenya, among others.
Nagamese: India pora mukti huijawa dekhikena, etu Gold Coast (Ghana), Nigeria, aru Kenya nisina dusra British jaga te bhi mukti nimite chesta korise.

English: The Indian independence movement saw the emergence of various ideologies, including socialism, communism, and secularism, shaping the future political landscape of the country.
Nagamese: India ke azaadi movement time-te dher iixaab ideologies, jene socialism, communism aru secularism ulai ahise, etu desh ke future political dik kom paare.

English: The All India Muslim League, founded in 1906, played a significant role in the demand for a separate Muslim nation, eventually leading to the creation of Pakistan.
Nagamese: All India Muslim League, ta 1906 te shuru hoise, ekta alag Muslim desh nimete dabi te bisi dangor kaam kurishe, last te Pakistan bonai dise.

English: The Indian independence movement had a profound impact on British politics, contributing to the decline of the British Empire and the eventual dismantling of colonial rule worldwide.
Nagamese: India ke azaadi movement toh British rajniti pora dangor asor porise, etu British Empire komjur kori bole madad korise aur dhire dhire dunia bhor te colonial niyam shesh hoise.

English: The role of the Indian press, including newspapers like "The Hindustan Times," "Amrita Bazar Patrika," and "Young India," was crucial in spreading awareness and mobilizing support during the independence movement.
Nagamese: The Hindustan Times," "Amrita Bazar Patrika," aru "Young India" nisina India media khan, sob milikena independence movement time-te janta ke bujhabo lage aru support dibo nimite bisi important kaam kurishe.

English: The Indian independence movement witnessed the sacrifices of countless freedom fighters, who endured imprisonment, torture, and even death in the pursuit of a free and independent India.
Nagamese: India ke azaadi movement toh bahut freedom fighter khan kurbani dikhise, jail gese, torture hoise, aru bahut toh morise ekta azaad India nimite.

English: The All India Depressed Classes League, founded by Dr. B.R. Ambedkar in 1935, aimed to address the social and political rights of the Dalit community in India.
Nagamese: All India Depressed Classes League, ke Dr. B.R. Ambedkar 1935 te banaishe, etu India te Dalit jatir nimite social aru political rights dibole lakshya loishe.

English: The Indian independence movement saw the emergence of various cultural and literary figures, such as Rabindranath Tagore, Bankim Chandra Chattopadhyay, and Subramania Bharati, who used their art to inspire nationalist sentiments.
Nagamese: India ke freedom fight toh, Rabindranath Tagore, Bankim Chandra Chattopadhyay, aru Subramania Bharati nisina bohut artist aru likhoki ulaise. Etu khan manu khan nijor art ke nationalist feeling dangor koriboley use korise.

English: The British landed in India in Surat on August 24, 1608.
Nagamese: British manu khan India te Surat potta August 24, 1608 te ahise.

English: The British rule did not begin until 1858.
Nagamese: British manu toh 1858 tak shuru hoi nai thakise.

English: British rule in India fostered economic exploitation and inequality.
Nagamese: India te British manu khan raj chalaise koile arthik sob manu ke nichuk kori dise, aru sob manu ek hisab te nuhua dise.

English: Indian independence movements gained momentum against British colonization.
Nagamese: British toh India ke upar raj koriboley thakile, India manu khan olai ahiboley start kori dise, joldi joldi azadi paiboley.

English: The British implemented divisive policies to control the Indian population.
Nagamese: British manu khan Indian manu khan ke divide kori bole nimete policy banaishe, taake manu khan control koribo pare.

English: Exploitative land revenue systems burdened Indian farmers under British rule.
Nagamese: British raj te India kheti kora manu khan upor te land laga tax system bishi bojh dishe.

English: British imperialism imposed cultural suppression and erasure in India.
Nagamese: British raj pora India te culture khan ke dabaidise aro metaidise.

English: The British Raj perpetuated social hierarchies and caste discrimination.
Nagamese: British Raj toh society laga level aru caste biak alag kori dikhai thishey, aru discrimination toh chalu kori di thakise.

English: Indian nationalist leaders emerged to challenge British colonial dominance.
Nagamese: India laga nationalist neta khan ulai ahise British raj ke challenge koriboley.

English: British colonialism led to famines and economic devastation in India.
Nagamese: British raj khanar nimitey India te bhukmari aru paisa nosto hoi jaise.

English: The British exploited Indian resources for their own gain.
Nagamese: British manu khan India laga maal khan nijor faida nimete use kurisey.

English: India's struggle for independence culminated in the 1947 partition.
Nagamese: India ke ajaadi nimitey koshish 1947 te desh bhag hua time pura hoise.

English: British dominance weakened amid rising Indian nationalism.
Nagamese: British hukumat dubla hoise, India laga nationalism urise manu khan pora.

English: Indian self-governance aspirations grew against British colonialism.
Nagamese: British raj ke against te, India pora nijor hukum chala bolebi etu icha dangor hoise.

English: Britain's grip on India loosened amid global geopolitical shifts.
Nagamese: Britain-or India-te pakori global hila-juli khan pora komti hoise.

English: Post-World War II, India moved towards independence.
Nagamese: World War II hobise piche, India toh azaad hobo nimete agey bhori thakise.

English: The British Raj eventually yielded to Indian sovereignty in 1947.
Nagamese: Aakhir mein, British Raj toh 1947 te India hukumot ke niche aahishe.

English: Doctrine of law: Legal principles forming the foundation of jurisprudence.
Nagamese: Law laga doctrine: Iku toh jurisprudence nimite foundation banaishe.

English: Maurya Empire emerged around 322 BCE in ancient India.
Nagamese: Maurya Empire toh purana India te 322 BCE manuhi pora shuru hoise.

English: Chandragupta Maurya founded the Mauryan Empire.
Nagamese: Chandragupta Maurya Mauryan Empire toiri kurishe.

English: Ashoka the Great expanded the Mauryan Empire to its zenith.
Nagamese: Ashoka the Great toh Mauryan Empire ke pura world jaanise dangor kori dise.

English: The Mauryan Empire encompassed much of the Indian subcontinent.
Nagamese: Morya raj India laga sob territory te melikena thakise.

English: Ashoka embraced Buddhism and propagated it across his empire.
Nagamese: Ashoka toh Buddhism ke apnaise aru pura rajyo te phaila dise.

English: Ashoka's edicts spread moral and ethical principles throughout India.
Nagamese: Ashoka ke hukum pora India te bhal kaam aru dharam usob phaila dise.

English: The Mauryan Empire had a highly centralized administration.
Nagamese: Maurya rajot toh ekdom power thaka administration chalaise.

English: Ashoka's reign is known for its pillars and rock inscriptions.
Nagamese: Ashoka rajatta toh thik ase etu pillar aru rock inscription khan nimite jana hoi.

English: Mauryan economy thrived on agriculture, trade, and taxation.
Nagamese: Maurya manu bisi crops, bepari aru tax pora dhun kaam kurishe.

English: Ashoka's Dhamma policy promoted religious tolerance and social welfare.
Nagamese: Ashoka laga Dhamma niti toh dharam bishwas khan ke samman dikhaishe aaru samaj homaj khan ke bhal kori bole promis korishe.

English: The Mauryan Empire established a vast network of roads and infrastructure.
Nagamese: Maurya raj-e toh dangor rasta aru infrastructure bonoikena thakise.

English: Ashoka's conquest of Kalinga led to his conversion to Buddhism.
Nagamese: Ashoka Kalinga loi jitise, etu nimite taikhan Buddhism mani bole shuru korise.

English: Mauryan art and architecture flourished under Ashoka's patronage.
Nagamese: Mauryan khanar kaam aru building Ashoka laga support te bahut bhal hoise.

English: The Mauryan Empire declined after Ashoka's death due to internal strife.
Nagamese: Ashoka mori jaowa pichet Mauryan Empire toh bhitor logote lora-jhogra kori thaki bohut homoi dukh paiche.

English: Mauryan society was organized into varnas and jatis.
Nagamese: Mauryan khanadaan toh varnas aur jatis hisaab se chalaisil thaka.

English: Ashoka's missionaries spread Buddhism to distant lands like Sri Lanka.
Nagamese: Ashoka manu khan Buddhism ke Sri Lanka nisina dur jagah te bhi pathaisele.

English: The Mauryan Empire's capital was initially Pataliputra (modern-day Patna).
Nagamese: Mauryan Empire laga capital toh first time Pataliputra thakise (etu toh aji-kali Patna ase).

English: Ashoka's reign saw advancements in medicine, education, and art.
Nagamese: Ashoka raj toh medicine, porha-lhekha aru art-ok bohut agei loijaisille.

English: Mauryan rulers used an extensive spy network for governance and security.
Nagamese: Maurya raja khan etu sorkar chalaibole aro security nimite bishi manu laga jasus network use kurisey.

English: Ashoka's pillars served as markers for his edicts and teachings.
Nagamese: Ashoka laga pillar khan toh, taikhan laga hukum aro sikhai dikhai thakibi nimite banaishe.

English: Mauryan art depicted royal court scenes, religious motifs, and animals.
Nagamese: Ashoka pillar khan, etu taikhan laga hukum aro sikhai dikhai thakibole banaise.

English: The Mauryan Empire faced threats from Greek invaders like Seleucus Nicator.
Nagamese: Maurya raj aru Seleucus Nicator nisina jun Greek manu ase thik khan pora darr thakibo lagise.

English: Ashoka's policy of Dhamma promoted social justice and compassion.
Nagamese: Ashoka-a Dhamma niti social justice ar daya dekhaisi.

English: The Mauryan Empire declined after the death of Ashoka's successors.
Nagamese: Ashoka laga pichede thaka manu khan mori jowar pichede Mauryan Empire komzhor hoi goishe.

English: Mauryan trade flourished with regions like Greece, Persia, and China.
Nagamese: Maurya manu khan Greece, Persia aru China nisina jaga khan logote dhanda bhal lagise.

English: Ashoka's reign is remembered for its emphasis on moral governance.
Nagamese: Ashoka raj toh moral niyom thaka government cholaise bolikena jana jae.

English: Mauryan architecture featured stupa complexes, palaces, and pillars.
Nagamese: Maurya time-te stupa complex, raja ghor aru pillar khan thakise.

English: The Mauryan Empire's decline led to the rise of regional kingdoms.
Nagamese: Maurya raj cholijowa khan, etu regional kingdom khan urise.

English: Ashoka's Rock Edicts provided insights into his governance principles.
Nagamese: Ashoka laga pathorke likha edicts khan, taikhan laga hukumat choluwa niyom kanun dikhai dise.

English: Mauryan society had a hierarchical structure with kings, nobles, and commoners.
Nagamese: Maurya khanadaan toh raja, bhalu manuh aru public thaka ekta nichina sreni hisaab te chalaishey.

English: Ashoka's conversion to Buddhism led to the spread of the faith.
Nagamese: Ashoka Buddhism loi poriborton huwa toh, iman dhorom charidi khelai di thakise.

English: The Mauryan Empire's military employed infantry, cavalry, and elephants.
Nagamese: Mouryajan Empire sena khan infantry, ghora aru hathi use kurisey.

English: Mauryan rulers issued coins with symbols like the lion and bull.
Nagamese: Murya raja khan manu boisa khan te, hathir aru bal gari nisina chinha thaka poisa uliyaise.

English: Ashoka's reign saw the promotion of welfare policies and hospitals.
Nagamese: Ashoka raj toh proja khanek bhal porichalona aru hospital bonaisey.

English: Mauryan culture assimilated diverse influences from across the empire.
Nagamese: Mauryan culture toh pura empire bhar pora alag alag chij milai kene ekta culture banaisey.

English: Ashoka's Ashokavadana chronicles his life and deeds as a Buddhist ruler.
Nagamese: Ashoka's Ashokavadana kitab toh, etu Buddhist raja hisaab te, taar life aru kaam khan likhi ase.

English: The Mauryan Empire's decline saw the disintegration of central authority.
Nagamese: Mauryan Empire burha hoi jowa পিছত, central hukumat tuti phuti goisey.

English: Mauryan society practiced agriculture, trade, and craftsmanship.
Nagamese: Mauryan khan manu crops bonaisil aar trade bhi kurisil, kaam bhi banai thakisil.

English: Ashoka's reign marked a shift towards non-violence and peace.
Nagamese: Ashoka raja thakurni time toh, maar-dhar nokora aru shanti rasta te gusi jaise.

English: Mauryan rulers maintained diplomatic relations with neighboring kingdoms.
Nagamese: Mauryan raja khan manu khan logote mel rakhishe aro asol asoli kingdom para bhi.

English: Ashoka's message of peace and tolerance influenced subsequent rulers.
Nagamese: Ashoka ke shanti aru tolerance ke kotha khan piche wala raja khan ke upar bisi asor porise.

English: The Mauryan Empire's decline resulted from succession disputes and invasions.
Nagamese: Mauryan Empire sorkar khan laga piche piche te problem aru dushmun ahibole pora durbol hoise.

English: Mauryan art reflected religious themes, with Buddhist motifs prevalent.
Nagamese: Mauryan art toh sob religious manu khan nimite banaishey, besi Buddhist laga design dikhiboley paishey.

English: Ashoka's policy of Dhamma influenced governance and ethics.
Nagamese: Ashoka Dhamma nini governance aru ethics khan upor te asor korise.

English: Mauryan cities were centers of trade, administration, and culture.
Nagamese: Maurya shahar khan toh business laga aro sarkari kaam laga aro culture laga bhi jagah thakise.

English: Ashoka's rock inscriptions were written in Prakrit, Greek, and Aramaic.
Nagamese: Ashoka manu likha pathorkhan khan Prakrit, Greek aru Aramaic te likhise.

English: The Mauryan Empire's decline saw the rise of regional dynasties.
Nagamese: Mourya rajatta khan jurai jowa pisot, alag alag jagah te raja khan uthi ahise.

English: Mauryan rulers constructed reservoirs and irrigation systems for agriculture.
Nagamese: Mourya rajatta khan jurai jowa pisot, alag alag jagah te raja khan uthi ahise.

English: Ashoka's missionaries spread Buddhism to regions like Central Asia and Southeast Asia.
Nagamese: Ashoka manu porchar khan Central Asia aru Southeast Asia nisina jaga te Buddhism porchar kurishey.

English: Mauryan society practiced religious pluralism, with Buddhism, Jainism, and Hinduism coexisting.
Nagamese: Mourya rajot te Buddhism, Jainism, Hindu dharam khan log ek logote mili jili ase.

English: Ashoka's patronage of Buddhism led to the construction of monasteries and stupas.
Nagamese: Ashoka raja Buddhism ke support korikena monastery aru stupa khan bonaishe.

English: The Mauryan Empire's decline led to the fragmentation of political power.
Nagamese: Mauryan Empire kamzui pichede, rajnitik shokti bhangibhuri hoi goise.

English: Mauryan rulers issued inscribed edicts to communicate laws and policies.
Nagamese: Maurya raja khan etu likha hukum jari kurishe, tai khanun aru niyom janabo nimite.

English: Ashoka's policy of Dhamma aimed to promote social harmony and welfare.
Nagamese: Ashoka Dhamma laga niyom toh samajot shanti aru bhal kaam koribole nimite ase.

English: Mauryan rulers maintained a powerful standing army for defense and conquest.
Nagamese: Maurya raja khan etu sena dangor powerful thakishe, taiyar thakishe hamesha dusman ke harabo nimite.

English: Ashoka's Rock Edicts were inscribed on natural rock formations across the empire.
Nagamese: Ashoka laga rock edicts khan pura empire bharot te natural rock formations uporte likhi thishey.

English: The Mauryan Empire's decline resulted from economic stagnation and administrative inefficiency.
Nagamese: Maurya raj khorab huwa ke economic thanda hoise aur administration bhibi bhal nai thakise.

English: Mauryan architecture incorporated elements of Indian, Persian, and Greek styles.
Nagamese: Mauryan time-te architecture khan Indian, Persian aro Greek style laga mix-up thakise.

English: Ashoka's reign saw the establishment of hospitals and medical facilities.
Nagamese: Ashoka raja thakurtey time-tey hospital aru medicine jaga-buga bonaisey.

English: Mauryan society valued education, with universities and centers of learning.

Nagamese: Jawaharlal Nehru India'r first Prime Minister asile azaadi piche.

English: The Indian National Congress emerged as the dominant political party.
Nagamese: Indian National Congress itu mora political party hisaab te nikili ahise.

English: The Constitution of India was adopted on January 26, 1950.
Nagamese: January 26, 1950 te India Constitution khan laagu hoise.

English: India became a democratic republic with universal adult suffrage.
Nagamese: India itu universal adult suffrage thaka democratic republic hoise.

English: The Partition of India led to communal violence and mass migrations.
Nagamese: India Partition time te danga-hangama aru manu khan jaga chadi pelai jaai thakise.

English: The princely states acceded to either India or Pakistan after independence.
Nagamese: Raja khan laga state taiyar huwa time te India nai toh Pakistan te misheli hoise.

English: The first general elections in 1952 marked a milestone in Indian democracy.
Nagamese: 1952 te ekdom first general election patikoi India democracy te ekta dangor kaam kora nisina hoise.

English: India adopted a mixed economy with socialist principles.
Nagamese: India itu mixed economy aru socialist usul khan apnai loise.

English: The Nehruvian era focused on industrialization and nation-building.
Nagamese: Nehru time te industry aru desh ke bana bola usurte dhyaan dise.

English: India faced challenges of poverty, illiteracy, and social inequality post-independence.
Nagamese: India independent huwa pichete goribi, likha porha nai aru jaat paat bhibed bhawi bohut dikat thakise.

English: The Green Revolution in the 1960s transformed India's agricultural sector.
Nagamese: 1960’s te Green Revolution huwa pichete India laga kheti kaam pura bodli hoise.

English: Jawaharlal Nehru's death in 1964 marked the end of an era.
Nagamese: 1964 te Jawaharlal Nehru mori juwa toh ek zomana shesh hoise.

English: Lal Bahadur Shastri succeeded Nehru as Prime Minister but served for a brief period.
Nagamese: Lal Bahadur Shastri, Nehru piche Prime Minister hoise, kintu olop din nimite.

English: The Indo-Pakistani War of 1965 tested India's military capabilities.
Nagamese: 1965 Indo-Pakistani larai te India’r sainya shokti poriksha korise.

English: The 1971 Bangladesh Liberation War led to the creation of Bangladesh.
Nagamese: 1971 Bangladesh mukti larai Bangladesh toiyar korise.

English: Indira Gandhi became the first female Prime Minister of India in 1966.
Nagamese: Indira Gandhi 1966 te India’r prothom meiki Prime Minister hoise.

English: The Emergency declared by Indira Gandhi in 1975 was a controversial period.
Nagamese: Indira Gandhi 1975 te Emergency declare kora toh ek gondogol zomana thakise.

English: The Janata Party coalition came to power after the end of the Emergency.
Nagamese: Emergency shesh huwa piche Janata Party ek loge hukumat chalaise.

English: Indira Gandhi returned to power with a resounding victory in 1980.
Nagamese: Indira Gandhi 1980 te dangor jiti loi power te wapas ahise.

English: The 1984 anti-Sikh riots followed the assassination of Indira Gandhi.
Nagamese: 1984 te Indira Gandhi ke mori pheluwa piche Sikh lok ke mari cuti korise.

English: Rajiv Gandhi succeeded his mother as Prime Minister in 1984.
Nagamese: Rajiv Gandhi tai’r ama piche 1984 te Prime Minister hoise.

English: Economic liberalization began under Rajiv Gandhi's government in the 1980s.
Nagamese: Rajiv Gandhi gormint thakurte 1980s te economic liberalization shuru hoise.

English: The Bofors scandal tarnished Rajiv Gandhi's image.
Nagamese: Bofors scandal toh Rajiv Gandhi naam bisi baddnaam kori dise.

English: The Mandal Commission report led to caste-based reservations in jobs and education.
Nagamese: Mandal Commission report para jobs aro education te caste hisaab te reservation hoi bole koi dise.

English: The Babri Masjid demolition in 1992 led to communal tensions across India.
Nagamese: 1992 te Babri Masjid tod di thakurte India bhari te Hindu Muslim majot gondogol hoise.

English: Economic reforms accelerated under the Narasimha Rao government in the 1990s.
Nagamese: Narasimha Rao gormint thakurte 1990s te economic reforms bisi joldi kori dise.

English: The liberalization, privatization, and globalization (LPG) policies transformed India's economy.
Nagamese: Liberalization, privatization, aro globalization (LPG) policies para India economy toh pura puri change kori dise.

English: The Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) emerged as a major political force in the 1990s.
Nagamese: Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) 1990s te ekta bisi dangor political party hisaab te olai ahise.

English: The Kargil War in 1999 showcased India's military strength.
Nagamese: 1999 te Kargil War te India army kitna tagat ase dikhai dise.

English: Atal Bihari Vajpayee's tenure as Prime Minister saw significant diplomatic initiatives.
Nagamese: Atal Bihari Vajpayee Prime Minister thaka samay te dher bisi diplomatic kaam kori dise.

English: The National Democratic Alliance (NDA) coalition came to power in 1998 and 1999.
Nagamese: National Democratic Alliance (NDA) coalition 1998 aro 1999 te power te ahise.

English: The Gujarat riots in 2002 raised questions about religious harmony in India.
Nagamese: 2002 te Gujarat danga hoise, India te dharam melijuli thakibo pare nai kine koikena kotha uthise.

English: Manmohan Singh became the Prime Minister in 2004, leading a Congress-led coalition.
Nagamese: 2004 te Manmohan Singh Prime Minister hoise, Congress sorkar cholaishe.

English: The UPA government implemented social welfare schemes like MNREGA and RTI Act.
Nagamese: UPA sorkar bemar manu khan nimite MNREGA aro RTI Act oliaise.

English: The 2008 Mumbai attacks exposed India's vulnerability to terrorism.
Nagamese: 2008 Mumbai hamla te India ke dikhaise terroris khan pora keneka bachaibo.

English: The Right to Education Act (RTE) was passed in 2009 to ensure free education for all.
Nagamese: 2009 te Right to Education Act (RTE) pass korise sob bachha khan ke free te porhaibo nimite.

English: The 2010 Commonwealth Games showcased India's infrastructure challenges.
Nagamese: 2010 Commonwealth Games te India laga rasta ghat dikhaise kiman takleef ase.

English: The Anna Hazare-led anti-corruption movement gained widespread support in 2011.
Nagamese: 2011 te Anna Hazare anti-corruption movement chalu korise aro sob manu uske support korise.

English: The Nirbhaya case in 2012 ignited nationwide protests for women's safety.
Nagamese: 2012 te Nirbhaya case toh oliaise aro desh bhor protiwad korise bhini khan nimite suraksha dibole.

English: The Modi wave swept the 2014 Lok Sabha elections, bringing BJP to power.
Nagamese: 2014 Lok Sabha election te Modi jiti kene BJP sorkar banaise.

English: Narendra Modi became Prime Minister, focusing on economic development and governance.
Nagamese: Narendra Modi Prime Minister hoikena desh cholaishe, paisa aru sorkar kaam pora bhal koribo nimite.

English: The Goods and Services Tax (GST) was introduced in 2017 to streamline taxation.
Nagamese: Goods and Services Tax (GST) toh 2017 te taxation system khan ek line te anibo nimite start kora thakise.

English: Demonetization in 2016 aimed to curb black money and corruption.
Nagamese: 2016 te demonetization korikena black money aru corruption komti koribo nimite plan kora thakise.

English: The abrogation of Article 370 in Jammu and Kashmir in 2019 sparked debates on federalism.
Nagamese: Jammu and Kashmir te Article 370 hataikena 2019 te federalism opor debate start hoise.

English: The Citizenship Amendment Act (CAA) in 2019 led to nationwide protests.
Nagamese: Citizenship Amendment Act (CAA) 2019 te pura desh te protest hoise.

English: The COVID-19 pandemic in 2020 challenged India's healthcare infrastructure.
Nagamese: 2020 te COVID-19 pandemic ahikena India r healthcare system ke challenge kori dise.

English: The farmers' protest against new agricultural laws gained international attention.
Nagamese: Notun kheti loi banaishe law ke against te farmers khan protest korikena international dhyan loishe.

English: India's vaccination drive against COVID-19 became one of the largest in the world.
Nagamese: India r COVID-19 vaccination campaign duniya te sobse bishi vaccination drive thakise.

English: The second wave of COVID-19 in 2021 overwhelmed India's healthcare system.
Nagamese: 2021 te COVID-19 second wave ahikena India r healthcare system ke pura bhangidikena dise.

English: The Bengal elections in 2021 saw the Trinamool Congress retain power.
Nagamese: 2021 te Bengal election te Trinamool Congress wapas power te aahise.

English: The BJP won the Assam assembly elections in 2021.
Nagamese: BJP 2021 te Assam assembly election jitise.

English: The COVID-19 pandemic influenced the conduct of elections in India.
Nagamese: COVID-19 pandemic toh India election khan ke dhang-dhoron alag kori dise.

English: The Supreme Court played a crucial role in various landmark judgments.
Nagamese: Supreme Court toh dher important faisla khan te etu role dangor ase.

English: The rise of regional parties has reshaped India's political landscape.
Nagamese: Regional party khan urise, etu India rajniti ke nawa shape dise.

English: The National Register of Citizens (NRC) sparked debates on citizenship and identity.
Nagamese: National Register of Citizens (NRC) citizenship aru identity upor debate shuru kori dise.

English: India's foreign policy has focused on strategic partnerships and economic diplomacy.
Nagamese: India’r foreign policy toh strategic partnership aru economic diplomacy te focus dise.

English: The Indian diaspora plays a significant role in India's global outreach.
Nagamese: India’r diaspora toh India’r global connection te dangor role play kore.

English: Social media has emerged as a powerful tool for political discourse and mobilization.
Nagamese: Social media toh rajnitik bhashan aru mobilization nimite ekta takatwar tool hisab te ulai ase.

English: India's political history after independence reflects its diverse challenges and aspirations.
Nagamese: India’r rajnitik history, independence pichede etu diverse challenges aru ichha khan bataye.

English: The history of computers dates back to ancient times with devices like the abacus.
Nagamese: Computer history toh purana zamana te jaise abacus nisina device thakise.

English: The invention of the mechanical calculator by Blaise Pascal in 1642 was a significant advancement.
Nagamese: Blaise Pascal 1642 te mechanical calculator banai thakise, etu ekta dangor progress thakise.

English: Charles Babbage conceptualized the Analytical Engine, considered the first mechanical computer, in the 1830s.
Nagamese: Charles Babbage toh Analytical Engine naamor ekta idea poila bar 1830s te ulai thakise, etu ke poila mechanical computer bhabi ase.

English: Ada Lovelace, a mathematician, is credited with writing the first computer program for Babbage's Analytical Engine.
Nagamese: Ada Lovelace naamor mathematician Babbage or Analytical Engine nimite poila computer program likhise koikena jana jae.

English: The 20th century saw rapid developments in electronic computing.
Nagamese: 20th century te electronic computing bisi success hoise.

English: The first electronic digital computer, the ENIAC (Electronic Numerical Integrator and Computer), was built in 1946.
Nagamese: Poila electronic digital computer, ENIAC (Electronic Numerical Integrator and Computer), 1946 te banaishe.

English: The UNIVAC I (Universal Automatic Computer) became the first commercially available computer in 1951.
Nagamese: UNIVAC I (Universal Automatic Computer) poila business nimite nikala computer 1951 te hoise.

English: The invention of the transistor in 1947 revolutionized computer technology, leading to smaller and more efficient computers.
Nagamese: 1947 te transistor banaishe computer technology ke pura bodli kurishe, aru chutu aru bisi kaam kora computer ulaise.

English: The development of programming languages like Fortran and COBOL in the 1950s facilitated software development.
Nagamese: Programming language jene Fortran aru COBOL 1950s te ulai thakise software bonabole nimite.

English: IBM introduced the IBM 701, the first mass-produced computer, in 1952.
Nagamese: IBM poila mass-produced computer, IBM 701, 1952 te ulaise. 1970s te microprocessor banaishe personal computer bonabole nimite.

English: The creation of the microprocessor in the 1970s enabled the development of personal computers.
Nagamese: 1970s te microprocessor banaishe personal computer bonabole nimite.

English: The Altair 8800, released in 1975, is considered the first commercially successful personal computer.
Nagamese: Altair 8800, 1975 te ulai thakise, etu ke poila business te safal personal computer bhabi ase.

English: The Apple I, developed by Steve Wozniak and Steve Jobs, was released in 1976, marking the beginning of Apple Inc.
Nagamese: Apple I," etu Steve Wozniak aru Steve Jobs bonaishe, 1976 te ulai thakise, etu Apple Inc. shuru hoise.

English: The IBM PC, introduced in 1981, became the standard for personal computers.
Nagamese: 1981 te ulolai thaka IBM PC, personal computer nimite ekta standard hoigol.

English: The graphical user interface (GUI) introduced by Apple's Macintosh in 1984 revolutionized computer usability.
Nagamese: Graphical user interface (GUI) Apple Macintosh 1984 te ulolai, computer kelai dibole pura poriborton koridiya.

English: The World Wide Web was invented by Tim Berners-Lee in 1989, transforming the internet into a global network.
Nagamese: Tim Berners-Lee 1989 te World Wide Web bonaishe, internet ke pura duniya network banaidiya.

English: Microsoft Windows, launched in 1985, became the dominant operating system for personal computers.
Nagamese: Microsoft Windows, 1985 te launch kurishe, personal computer nimite sobse dangor operating system hoigol.

English: The dot-com bubble in the late 1990s saw rapid growth in internet-based companies.
Nagamese: 1990s pichete dot-com bubble internet company sob bisi bisi dangor hoishe dikhaishe.

English: The development of smartphones in the early 2000s revolutionized mobile computing.
Nagamese: 2000 shuru te smartphone bonaishe, mobile computing poriborton koridiya.

English: The launch of Google in 1998 revolutionized internet search and digital services.
Nagamese: 1998 te Google shuru kurishe, internet search aru digital service poriborton koridiya.

English: The rise of social media platforms like Facebook and Twitter transformed online communication.
Nagamese: Facebook aru Twitter nisina social media platform online kotha barta poriborton koridiya.

English: Cloud computing emerged as a major trend in the 2010s, enabling remote storage and access to data.
Nagamese: Cloud computing 2010 te dangor trend hisab te ulai ahise, dur jaga te storage aru data access dibole nimite.

English: The advent of big data analytics revolutionized data processing and decision-making.
Nagamese: Bigger data analytics ahile data processing aru decision loia khan revolution anise.

English: Artificial intelligence (AI) and machine learning technologies saw rapid advancements in recent years.
Nagamese: Artificial intelligence (AI) aru machine learning technology khan picheti sal te bahut besi aghe ahise.

English: Quantum computing emerged as a promising field with the potential to solve complex problems.
Nagamese: Quantum computing ekta dharun field hisape ulaise, etu khan etu toh complex problem khan solve koribo pare.

English: The Internet of Things (IoT) connected everyday objects to the internet, enabling smart devices.
Nagamese: Internet of Things (IoT) toh sob din use kora chiz khan ke internet te connect korise, aru smart device khan ke kaam kori bole dibo pare.

English: Cybersecurity became a major concern with the rise of cyberattacks and data breaches.
Nagamese: Cybersecurity toh ekta dangor chinta hoise cyberattack aru data breach besi huwa karone.

English: The COVID-19 pandemic accelerated digital transformation, with increased reliance on remote work and online services.
Nagamese: COVID-19 pandemic toh digital badli khan ke joldi korise, aru besi remote te kaam kora aru online service te depend huwa besi hoise.

English: Virtual reality (VR) and augmented reality (AR) technologies gained popularity for immersive experiences.
Nagamese: Virtual reality (VR) aru augmented reality (AR) technology khan besi famous hoise immersive experience karone.

English: Blockchain technology emerged with applications in cryptocurrency and secure digital transactions.
Nagamese: Blockchain technology toh ulaise cryptocurrency aru secure digital transaction te use kori bole.

English: Autonomous vehicles and drones saw advancements in navigation and automation.
Nagamese: Autonomous gari aru drone khan navigation aru automation te aghe bohut ahise.

English: 3D printing technology revolutionized manufacturing with on-demand production of objects.
Nagamese: 3D printing technology toh manufacturing ke badli dise aru demand hisab te saman khan banaibo pare.

English: The development of quantum supremacy marked a significant milestone in quantum computing.
Nagamese: Quantum supremacy toh quantum computing dunia te ek dum important step asele.

English: The rise of e-commerce platforms like Amazon and Alibaba transformed retail and commerce.
Nagamese: Amazon aro Alibaba nisina e-commerce platform khan olai kene retail aro business dunia ke pura puri bodli dise.

English: Cryptocurrencies like Bitcoin and Ethereum gained mainstream acceptance as alternative forms of currency.
Nagamese: Bitcoin aro Ethereum nisina cryptocurrency khan ke sob manuhe kinibo thakise aro etu rupiya paisa nisina use bhi koribole nimete hoise.

English: Edge computing emerged as a solution for processing data closer to the source for faster response times.
Nagamese: Edge computing naam ekta nawa system ulaise, juntu data khan ke joldi process kore aru jawab dibole pare.

English: The development of voice assistants like Siri and Alexa enabled natural language interactions with computers.
Nagamese: Siri aro Alexa nisina voice assistant khan banaishe, etu manuhe computer logote aaram se baat kori bole nimete hoi.

English: The gig economy saw a rise in freelancing and remote work opportunities.
Nagamese: Gig economy toh freelancing aru remote kaam karne nimete besi chance dise.

English: The digital divide remained a challenge, with disparities in access to technology and internet connectivity.
Nagamese: Digital divide toh ekta problem ase, kunoba toh technology aru internet besi pai aru kunoba toh napai.

English: Cybersecurity threats like ransomware attacks and phishing scams became more sophisticated.
Nagamese: Ransomware aru phishing nisina cybersecurity threat khan besi dangor hoise.

English: Privacy concerns grew with the collection and monetization of personal data by tech companies.
Nagamese: Tech company khan manu khan data collect kori kene paisa kamaise, etu nimete privacy ke lekar chinta bhi besi hoise.

English: The use of AI in decision-making raised ethical concerns regarding bias and accountability.
Nagamese: AI ke decision lene nimete use korile ethical dharon khan bhi uthise bias aru accountability ke lekar.

English: The development of quantum algorithms and quantum cryptography posed new challenges and opportunities.
Nagamese: Quantum algorithm aru quantum cryptography toh notun problem aru chance bonaishe.

Nagamese: COVID-19 time-te online class aru online doctor dikhai toh besi popular hoishe.

English: The integration of AI and robotics led to advancements in automation and autonomous systems.
Nagamese: AI aru robotics ekta logote anibo para toh automation aru automatic system bhal hoishe.

English: The expansion of 5G networks enabled faster and more reliable wireless communication.
Nagamese: 5G network besi bhal aru joldi wireless communication dibo. Solar aru wind power use korikena data center pora carbon kamti kurishe.

English: The adoption of renewable energy sources like solar and wind power in data centers reduced carbon emissions.
Nagamese: Esports aru online gaming business dangor huwa pora notun entertainment paishe.

English: The development of self-driving cars and drones saw advancements in navigation and safety.
Nagamese: Self-driving car aru drone no navigation aru safety bhal hoise.

English: The emergence of biometric authentication improved security and user experience in digital transactions.
Nagamese: Biometric authentication olahile digital transaction te security aru user experience bhal hoise.

English: Robotics and automation technologies saw applications in manufacturing, healthcare, and logistics.
Nagamese: Robotics aru automation technology khan manufacturing, healthcare aru logistics te kaam kori bole polaise.

English: The growth of fintech companies disrupted traditional banking and financial services.
Nagamese: Fintech company khan bishi olahile traditional banking aru financial services te disturb korise.

English: The development of smart homes and connected devices improved energy efficiency and convenience.
Nagamese: Smart home aru connected device banaile energy bacha bole aru aram hoise.

English: Digital currencies and decentralized finance (DeFi) gained popularity as alternatives to traditional banking.
Nagamese: Digital currency aru decentralized finance (DeFi) popular hoise, traditional banking nisina.

English: The development of quantum communication networks enabled secure transmission of information.
Nagamese: Quantum communication network banaile information secure te pathabo pare.

English: The integration of AI and robotics led to advancements in fields like healthcare, manufacturing, and agriculture.
Nagamese: AI aru robotics ekta hoile healthcare, manufacturing aru agriculture nisina field te bhal hoise.

English: The rise of remote work and telecommuting transformed the way people work and collaborate.
Nagamese: Remote kaam aru telecommuting olahile manu khan keneka kaam kore aru milikena kaam kore etu change hoise.

English: Virtual reality (VR) and augmented reality (AR) technologies saw increased adoption in entertainment, education, and training.
Nagamese: Virtual reality (VR) aru augmented reality (AR) technology khan entertainment, education aru training te bishi use kori ase.

Nagamese: Blockchain" technology khan sirf "cryptocurrency" nimiteh nohoi, etu "supply chain management" aaru "digital identity" bhi te bhi kaam kori bole.

English: The development of quantum computers with increasing qubit counts showed promise for solving complex problems.
Nagamese: Quantum computers bonai thaka toh dikhai diseh ki etu complex problem solve koribole pari.

Nagamese: Cybersecurity dhumki jese ransomware attacks, data breaches, aaru phishing scams toh manus aaru organizations nimiteh problem hoi ase.

English: The expansion of 5G networks enabled faster and more reliable wireless communication, facilitating the growth of IoT devices and smart cities.
Nagamese: 5G network besi huwa toh internet besi joldi aaru bisi bhal hoi jaishe, aru IoT devices aaru smart cities bhi besi hoishe.

The COVID-19 pandemic accelerated digital transformation across industries, leading to increased adoption of remote work, e-commerce, telemedicine, and online education.
