English: Indian poetry has a rich history dating back to ancient times.
Nagamese: Quantum communication networks bonai thaka toh information safe te long distance te pathai dibole pari, aru quantum internet nimiteh rasta bonai diseh.

English: The Rigveda, composed around 1500 BCE, contains some of the earliest Indian poetry.
Nagamese: Rabindranath Tagore, jitu Nobel inam paishe, etu kobita khan India ruhaniyat aro universal insaniyat ke dikhai dise.

English: Sanskrit poetry flourished during the Vedic and Classical periods.
Nagamese: Sanskrit kobita khan Vedic aru Classical shomoy te joti uthise.

English: Kalidasa, often regarded as the greatest poet in Sanskrit literature, wrote masterpieces like "Shakuntala" and "Meghaduta."
Nagamese: Kalidasa, juntu Sanskrit sahitya te sobse dangor kabi koi, "Shakuntala" aro "Meghaduta" nisina kitaab likhise.

English: Tamil Sangam poetry, dating back to the classical period, celebrated love, nature, and devotion.
Nagamese: Tamil Sangam kobita, juntu classical shomoy te shuru hoise, pyar, nature, aro bhagwaan ke puja koribole koi.

English: Bhakti poetry emerged in medieval India, expressing devotion to Hindu deities through vernacular languages.
Nagamese: Bhakti kobita medieval India te ulaise, Hindu bhagwaan khan ke bhakti dikhai bole local bhasha use kori.

English: Kabir, Mirabai, and Tulsidas were prominent Bhakti poets who influenced Indian literature.
Nagamese: Kabir, Mirabai, aro Tulsidas famous Bhakti kabi ase, jun khan India sahitya ke probhab dikhaise.

English: Sufi poetry, with its mystical themes, transcended religious boundaries in medieval India.
Nagamese: Sufi kobita, juntu mystical bhabona dikhai, medieval India te dharam ke pare kori di se.

English: The Mughal era saw the patronage of Persian poetry by rulers like Akbar and Shah Jahan.
Nagamese: Mughal shomoy te Persian kobita ke Akbar aro Shah Jahan nisina raja khan support korise.

English: The Bhakti and Sufi movements contributed to the democratization of poetry, reaching the masses.
Nagamese: Bhakti aro Sufi movement kobita ke aam janta tak pohchai dise.

English: The colonial period witnessed the emergence of Indian poets writing in English, like Rabindranath Tagore and Sarojini Naidu.
Nagamese: Colonial shomoy te Rabindranath Tagore aro Sarojini Naidu nisina Indian kabi khan English te likhibole shuru korise.

English: Rabindranath Tagore, a Nobel laureate, composed poems that captured the essence of Indian spirituality and universal humanism.
Nagamese: Rabindranath Tagore, jitu Nobel inam paishe, etu kobita khan India ruhaniyat aro universal insaniyat ke dikhai dise.

English: The Indian independence movement inspired patriotic poetry, expressing aspirations for freedom and social justice.
Nagamese: India ke swadhinta aandolan deshpremik kobita khan ke jonom dise, juntu swatantrata aru samajik insaf khan dikhai dise.

English: Subramania Bharati, a Tamil poet, infused his works with nationalist fervor during India's struggle for independence.
Nagamese: Subramania Bharati, ekjon Tamil kobi, India ke swadhinta nimite luta-puti kori thaka time-te puro josh bhordisile.

English: The Progressive Writers' Movement in the 20th century addressed social issues through poetry, advocating for change.
Nagamese: 20th (bisi) century te, Progressive Writers' Movement kobita dwara samajik dukh-dard ke bataisile, aru poriborton anibo nimite dabi kurisile.

English: Faiz Ahmed Faiz and Sahir Ludhianvi were influential Urdu poets who voiced dissent against oppression and inequality.
Nagamese: Faiz Ahmed Faiz aru Sahir Ludhianvi dangor Urdu kobi asile jun khan julumi aru bhedbhav ke virodh kurisile.

English: Post-independence Indian poetry reflected the complexities of a newly liberated nation, grappling with identity and modernity.
Nagamese: Swadhin pichete India ke kobita khan nawa azad desh ke bhawna khan ke dikhai dise, aru nijorke pahachan aru aadhunikta ke bare-te bhabisile.

English: Kamala Das and A.K. Ramanujan were notable poets who explored themes of gender, identity, and cultural heritage.
Nagamese: Kamala Das aru A.K. Ramanujan jana-jayi kobi khan asile jun khan aurat, pahachan aru culture ke uporte kobita kurisile.

English: The Navya movement in Kannada poetry introduced modernist techniques, breaking away from traditional forms.
Nagamese: Kannada kobita'te Navya movement toh convensional breaking kori modernist techniques aniselese.

English: Dalit poets like Namdeo Dhasal and Meena Kandasamy use poetry as a tool for social activism and empowerment.
Nagamese: Namdeo Dhasal aro Meena Kandasamy nisina Dalit kobi khan poem ke social kaam aru takat dibole nimete use kore.

English: Indian poetry festivals, such as the Jaipur Literature Festival, provide platforms for poets to showcase their work and engage with audiences.
Nagamese: Indian poetry festival khan, jiman Jaipur Literature Festival ase, etu poets khan ke platform diye nijor kaam dekhabo nimite aru audience logote baat koribo nimite.

English: Digital platforms have democratized poetry, allowing poets to reach global audiences through blogs, social media, and online publications.
Nagamese: Digital platform besi bhal hoi jaise poetry nimite, poets khan ke duniya bhar manu khan tak pohchibo nimite blogs, social media aru online publications dwara. Indian poets khan alag alag jagaa pora prerna loi, jiman purana kotha khan, nature, aru din din zindagi pora.

English: Indian poets draw inspiration from a myriad of sources, including mythology, folklore, nature, and everyday life.
Nagamese: Poetry reading aru spoken word event popular hoi ase, India te performance poetry culture bisi bhal hoi ase.

English: Poetry readings and spoken word events have gained popularity, fostering a vibrant culture of performance poetry in India.
Nagamese: Aaj kal poets khan jiman Javed Akhtar, Gulzar, aru Arundhathi Subramaniam manu khan ke aaji bhi nijor kobita dwara mohit kore.

English: Contemporary poets like Javed Akhtar, Gulzar, and Arundhathi Subramaniam continue to captivate audiences with their lyrical verses.
Nagamese: Indian poetry alag alag bhasha aar region ke paar kore, desh ke alag alag culture ke dikhaye.

English: Indian poetry transcends linguistic and regional boundaries, reflecting the diverse cultural tapestry of the nation.
Nagamese: Poetry anthologies aru literary magazines nawa poets khan ke nijor talent dekhabo nimite aru manu khan logote milibo nimite platform diye.

English: Poetry anthologies and literary magazines provide platforms for emerging poets to showcase their talent and connect with readers.
Nagamese: Indian poets khan alag alag chij ke explore kare, jiman pyaar aru dukho pora politics aru zindagi tak.

English: Indian poets explore a wide range of themes, from love and longing to politics and existentialism.
Nagamese: Poetry slams aru open mic nights jagaa diye poets khan ke nijor kaam share koribo nimite aru creative baat cheet koribo nimite.

English: Poetry slams and open mic nights provide spaces for poets to share their work and engage in creative exchange.
Nagamese: Poetry slam aru open mic night khan poet khan ke nijor kaam share kori bole aru creative exchange te bhag lobi bole jagah diye.

English: Indian poets often experiment with form and language, blending traditional and contemporary elements to create innovative works.
Nagamese: Indian poet khan prai form aru language ke loi kheli, purana aru notun kura mix kori kene notun kaam banae.

English: Poetry has the power to provoke thought, evoke emotions, and foster empathy, making it a potent tool for social change.
Nagamese: Poetry te takat ase ki etu bhabona ulae, emotion anibo, aru ek dusre ke bujibo, etu ekdum dangor hathiyar ase etu society ke bodli bole.

English: The legacy of Indian poetry is celebrated through academic studies, literary festivals, and cultural institutions dedicated to preserving literary heritage.
Nagamese: Indian poetry ke school college aru festival manai kene ijjot diye, aru culture jagah khan ase etu sob purana likha khan ke bachai kene rakhe.

English: Indian poetry reflects the complexities of modern life, grappling with issues of identity, belonging, and human connection.
Nagamese: Indian poetry te ajikal life ki ase etu dikhae, nijor pahchan, kuntu jagah te ase aru insan khan ke jura kene rakhe etu sob dikhae.

English: Poetry has played a pivotal role in shaping Indian cultural identity, preserving traditions, and inspiring future generations of writers.
Nagamese: Poetry ek important kaam kori ase Indian culture ke bana bole, purana dharon khan ke bachai rakhibole aru future te likhibole khan ke hosila diye.

English: Indian poetry encompasses a diverse range of voices, from classical masters to contemporary wordsmiths, each contributing to the rich tapestry of literary expression.
Nagamese: Indian poetry te alag alag awaz ase, purana ustads khan pora suru kori kene ajikali likha wala khan tak, sob mili kene ek dangor kitaab banae.

English: Poetry serves as a vehicle for self-expression, allowing individuals to articulate their thoughts, feelings, and experiences in verse.
Nagamese: Poetry ek rasta ase nijor bhabona khan ke dikha bole, nijor dil ki bole etu aru life ki ase etu sob line line likhi bole.

English: The beauty of Indian poetry lies in its ability to transcend language barriers, touching the hearts and minds of readers across the globe.
Nagamese: Indian poetry to itna sundor ase ki etu language ke bi paar kori, dunia ke luk khan ke dil aru dimag ke chui jae.

English: Indian poetry celebrates the human experience in all its complexity, offering solace, insight, and inspiration to those who engage with it.
Nagamese: Indian poetry to insan life ke ijjot diye, dukh ke kamti kore, gyaan battae, aru hosila diye etu ke jun khan pare.

English: From ancient epics to modern free verse, Indian poetry continues to evolve, reflecting the ever-changing landscape of human existence.
Nagamese: Purana kahani pora suru kori kene ajikali notun style tak, Indian poetry to hamesha bodli thake aru etu insan life ke dikhae ki etu hamesha kene bodli ase.

English: The Bhakti and Sufi movements enriched Indian poetry with their spiritual and philosophical insights, fostering a deep sense of introspection and devotion.
Nagamese: Bhakti aru Sufi movement khan Indian poetry ke spiritual aru philosophical gyaan diye, aru dil se bhabona aru bhakti ke hosila diye.

English: The colonial era brought new influences to Indian poetry, as poets grappled with the challenges of cultural assimilation and resistance.
Nagamese: Colonial time khan India poem te nawa nawa asor anishe, etu time te poet khan cultural assimilation aru resistance dikhai thishe.

English: Rabindranath Tagore's lyrical poetry resonated with audiences worldwide, earning him acclaim as a literary giant and a voice of universal wisdom.
Nagamese: Rabindranath Tagore etu time te world wide famous hoishe, tai ekjon wisdom poet ase.

English: The Progressive Writers' Movement gave voice to the marginalized and oppressed, using poetry as a means of social critique and political resistance.
Nagamese: Progressive Writers' Movement pora oppressed aru marginalized manu khan ke awaj dise, poem ke social aru political resistance hesab te use korishe.

English: Modernist poets like Nissim Ezekiel and A.K. Ramanujan experimented with form and language, pushing the boundaries of poetic expression in India.
Nagamese: Modernist poet khan jese Nissim Ezekiel aru A.K. Ramanujan khan poem aru language ke dangi loishe, India te poem ke nawa style te banai thishe.

English: Aryabhata introduced trigonometry and astronomy in ancient Indian mathematics.
Nagamese: Aryabhata pora trigonometry aru astronomy ancient India math te shuru korishe.

English: Ramanujan made substantial contributions to number theory and infinite series.
Nagamese: Ramanujan pora number theory aru infinite series te help kurishe.

English: Brahmagupta formulated rules for arithmetic operations and introduced zero.
Nagamese: Brahmagupta pora arithmetic operations nimite rules banai thishe aru zero bhi shuru kurishe.

English: Bhaskara II pioneered differential calculus and solved quadratic equations.
Nagamese: Bhaskara II pora differential calculus shuru korishe aru quadratic equations bhi solve kurishe.

English: Srinivasa Ramanujan had an intuitive grasp of complex mathematical concepts.
Nagamese: Srinivasa Ramanujan complex mathematical concepts bhi janishe.

English: Aryabhata's work influenced Islamic and European mathematics.
Nagamese: Aryabhata kaam pora Islamic aru European mathematics te bhi asor porishe.

English: Ramanujan's notebooks contain remarkable mathematical discoveries.
Nagamese: Ramanujan-a kitaab khan math dikhaikena dangor dangor kura ase.

English: Indian mathematicians made significant strides in algebra and geometry.
Nagamese: India manu khan algebra aru geometry te bhut agey ahibole para.

English: Madhava of Sangamagrama developed infinite series for trigonometric functions.
Nagamese: Sangamagrama-a Madhava-e trigonometric function nimite ekdam lamba series banaishe.

English: Baudhayana authored the earliest known text on geometry, the Baudhayana Sulba Sutra.
Nagamese: Baudhayana-e geometry uporte likha kitaab, Baudhayana Sulba Sutra, sobse agey ase.

English: Aryabhata proposed the heliocentric model centuries before Copernicus.
Nagamese: Aryabhata-e Copernicus agey centuries heliocentric model dikhaishe.

English: The Kerala school of mathematics refined calculus concepts centuries ago.
Nagamese: Kerala school khan calculus concepts agey centuries te aur dhori loile.

English: Ramanujan's mathematical insights continue to inspire modern research.
Nagamese: Ramanujan-a math khan ajikalkar research nimite bhi kaam te lage.

English: Indian mathematics flourished during the Gupta period.
Nagamese: Gupta period time India-a math bhut besi bhal hoishe.

English: Bhaskara II's works include treatises on arithmetic, algebra, and astronomy.
Nagamese: Bhaskara II-a kitaab khan hisaab, algebra, aru astronomy uporte ase.

English: Brahmagupta's Brahmasphutasiddhanta contributed to algebraic and geometric progress.
Nagamese: Brahmagupta-a Brahmasphutasiddhanta khan algebra aru geometric agey nimite bhut madad korise.

English: Kerala mathematicians developed techniques for calculus and infinite series.
Nagamese: Kerala math wala khanelog toh calculus aru infinite series nimitey techniques bonaishey.

English: Indian mathematicians laid foundations for modern calculus and algebra.
Nagamese: India math wala khanelog toh modern calculus aru algebra nimitey buthbuniyad banai thishey.

English: Ramanujan's conjectures have led to significant mathematical breakthroughs.
Nagamese: Ramanujan's hunman laga dharona khan majboot math laga aguvai dikhaishey.

English: Ancient Indian mathematics influenced global mathematical thought.
Nagamese: Purana India laga math toh duniya math biswas ke probhab korishey.

English: Nilakantha Somayaji made contributions to calculus and astronomy.
Nagamese: Nilakantha Somayaji toh calculus aru astronomy te bhi madad korishey.

English: Indian mathematicians made early advancements in numerical analysis and algorithms.
Nagamese: India laga math wala khanelog toh number hisab kitab aru algorithms te bhi agey barhishey.

English: Aryabhata's Aryabhatiya is a seminal work in Indian mathematics.
Nagamese: Aryabhata's Aryabhatiya toh India laga math te ekta dangor kitab hisab se jana jaishey.

English: Ramanujan's collaboration with Hardy led to profound mathematical discoveries.
Nagamese: Ramanujan aru Hardy ekta logot kam karikena math te dhyaan dene wala chij dikhaishey.

English: Indian mathematics emphasized practical applications in architecture, astronomy, and commerce.
Nagamese: India laga math toh architecture, astronomy aru bepar te bhi kaam kurishey.

English: Space is vast and seemingly endless.
Nagamese: Aakash toh bhut bada ase aru koto dur ase koi nai sakey.

English: The universe contains billions of galaxies.
Nagamese: Universe toh bahut galaxy ase.

English: Galaxies are vast systems of stars, gas, and dust bound together by gravity.
Nagamese: Galaxy manu stars, gas, aru dust ekta gravity te bandhi hua system.

English: Our galaxy is called the Milky Way.
Nagamese: Amaar galaxy naam Milky Way ase.

English: The Milky Way is home to our solar system.
Nagamese: Milky Way te amaar solar system bhi ase.

English: The solar system consists of the sun and all the objects that orbit around it.
Nagamese: Solar system manu sun aru etu sarpakhe ghumai thaka bostugila.

English: The sun is a star, a massive ball of hydrogen and helium.
Nagamese: Sun ekta star ase, etu hydrogen aru helium bhara ball.

English: Earth is the third planet from the sun.
Nagamese: Earth toh sun pora tin number planet ase.

English: Earth is unique in our solar system because it has liquid water on its surface.
Nagamese: Amaar solar system te Earth ekela planet juntu te pani ase.

English: The study of space is called astronomy.
Nagamese: Space ke study koribole astronomy bole.

English: Telescopes are used to observe objects in space.
Nagamese: Telescope manu space te thaka bostugulake dekhibole use kore.

English: The Hubble Space Telescope has provided some of the most detailed images of distant galaxies.
Nagamese: Hubble Space Telescope pora bohut door galaxy khan-ni ke dikhai diye ase.

English: The speed of light is the fastest speed in the universe.
Nagamese: Phor light toh universe te sobse joldi jae.

English: Light from the sun takes about 8 minutes to reach Earth.
Nagamese: Light, jun toh suraj pora ase, etu dharti te ahibo nimete 8 minutes lage.

English: The closest star to Earth, other than the sun, is Proxima Centauri.
Nagamese: Dharti laga sobse najdik star, suraj ke bood dile, etu Proxima Centauri ase.

English: The moon is Earth's natural satellite.
Nagamese: Chand toh dharti laga natural satellite ase.

English: It takes about 27.3 days for the moon to orbit Earth.
Nagamese: Chand ke dharti charifale ghuribo nimete prai 27.3 din lage.

English: The moon's surface is covered in craters, mountains, and plains.
Nagamese: Chand laga upar toh crater, pahar aru samtal jagah pora bhara hua ase.

English: The moon has no atmosphere.
Nagamese: Chand te hawa nai.

English: The first human landing on the moon was Apollo 11 in 1969.
Nagamese: Sobse phaila insan jun chand te gorai thise, etu Apollo 11, 1969 te thise.

English: Astronauts Neil Armstrong and Buzz Aldrin were the first humans to walk on the moon.
Nagamese: Astronaut Neil Armstrong aru Buzz Aldrin phaila insan thakise jun chand te khuj thise.

English: Space travel is achieved using rockets.
Nagamese: Rocket use kori space te jabo pare.

English: The first artificial satellite launched into space was Sputnik 1 by the Soviet Union in 1957.
Nagamese: Pola artificial satellite, Sputnik 1 Soviet Union para 1957 te space te pathaise.

English: The International Space Station (ISS) is a habitable space station orbiting Earth.
Nagamese: International Space Station (ISS) ekta thakibo para station ase, etu Earth charifale ghuri ase.

English: The ISS serves as a research laboratory for scientific experiments in microgravity.
Nagamese: ISS te gravity komti thaka pora scientific experiment khan koribo nimite ekta laboratory hisape use kore.

English: Gravity is weaker in space than on Earth.
Nagamese: Earth tula te space te gravity komti ase.

English: Astronauts experience weightlessness in space.
Nagamese: Astronaut khan space te weightless feel kore.

English: Space is filled with radiation from stars and other cosmic phenomena.
Nagamese: Space ta star aro dusra cosmic chiz pora radiation bhorti ase.

English: Black holes are regions in space where gravity is so strong that nothing, not even light, can escape.
Nagamese: Black hole khan space te etu dangor jagah ase juntu gravity pora light bhi nikalibo napare.

English: The center of most galaxies contains a supermassive black hole.
Nagamese: Joto galaxy ase taikhan laga center te ekta supermassive black hole thake.

English: Stars are born from clouds of gas and dust in space.
Nagamese: Star khan space te gas aro dust laga cloud pora toiri hoi.

English: The life cycle of a star depends on its mass.
Nagamese: Tarasokol jibon kotha ta, taikhanibur weight uporte depend kore.

English: Stars like our sun will eventually become red giants and then white dwarfs.
Nagamese: Ama khanir junka tarasokol, eku somoite etu dangor aru lalsina hoi jabo, pichete ta white dwarf hoijabo.

English: Some stars end their lives in explosive events called supernovae.
Nagamese: Kichu tarasokol toh dhum-dhamaka hoi kene sesh hoi, etu ke supernovae bole.

English: Neutron stars are incredibly dense remnants of supernova explosions.
Nagamese: Neutron tarasokol toh etu dangor dhum-dhamaka pichete pura kachikuchi hoi kene ase.

English: Some neutron stars emit beams of radiation and are known as pulsars.
Nagamese: Kichu neutron tarasokol radiation dibo pare, taikhanike pulsars bole.

English: The study of the origin, structure, and evolution of the universe is called cosmology.
Nagamese: Universe kenekoi ahise, ki ase aru kenekoi change hoise etu bisoy porha ke cosmology bole.

English: The Big Bang theory is the prevailing cosmological model for the observable universe's origin.
Nagamese: Big Bang theory toh sobse popular theory ase universe start hua bisoyte.

English: The universe is expanding, with galaxies moving away from each other over time.
Nagamese: Universe toh dhire dhire dango hoikene ase, galaxies ek dusra ke durlai jaikene ase.

English: Dark matter and dark energy are mysterious components that make up most of the universe.
Nagamese: Dark matter aru dark energy naam ke duita jinis ase, taikhanibur pora universe besi hissa bona ase.

English: Nebulae are vast clouds of gas and dust in space.
Nagamese: Nebulae toh gas aru dhuli mati laga clouds ase space te.

English: Planets are large bodies that orbit stars.
Nagamese: Planets toh dangor bodies ase, etu star charifale ghuri thake.

English: There are eight planets in our solar system: Mercury, Venus, Earth, Mars, Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, and Neptune.
Nagamese: Amar solar system te aat ta planets ase: Mercury, Venus, Earth, Mars, Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, aru Neptune.

English: Dwarf planets are smaller bodies that orbit the sun and have not cleared their orbits of other debris.
Nagamese: Dwarf planets toh chutu bodies ase jun solar charifale ghuri thake lekin nijor orbit te thaka debris poriskar kori diya nai.

English: Pluto was reclassified as a dwarf planet in 2006.
Nagamese: Pluto ke 2006 te dwarf planet hisap te class paltai diya hol.

English: Comets are icy bodies that orbit the sun.
Nagamese: Comets toh baraf thaka bodies ase jun solar chari fale ghuri thake.

English: Asteroids are rocky bodies that orbit the sun, mostly found in the asteroid belt between Mars and Jupiter.
Nagamese: Asteroids toh pathor thaka bodies ase jun solar charifale ghuri thake, besi kori Mars aru Jupiter majot thaka asteroid beltot poa jai.

English: Meteoroids are small rocky or metallic objects that travel through space.
Nagamese: Meteoroids toh chutu pathor or metal bostu ase jun space te ghumi thake

English: Meteors are meteoroids that enter Earth's atmosphere and burn up, producing a streak of light.
Nagamese: Meteors toh meteoroids ase jun Earthor atmosphereot ghumi jai aru joli jai, aru ekta line nisina light dekhai.

English: Meteorites are meteoroids that survive the passage through Earth's atmosphere and land on the surface.
Nagamese: Meteorites toh meteoroids ase jun Earthor atmosphereor para basi jai arr matit pori jai.

English: The Oort Cloud is a hypothesized region of icy bodies surrounding the solar system.
Nagamese: The Oort Cloud toh baraf thaka bodiesor ekta area buli bhaba hoi jun solar systen chari fale ase.

English: The Kuiper Belt is a region of the solar system beyond the orbit of Neptune, containing many dwarf planets and other small bodies.
Nagamese: Kuiper Belt toh Neptune orbit par hoi thaka solar system laga ekta jaga ase, etu jaga te dwarf planet aro chutu chutu object bhut ase.

English: Space missions have explored other planets, moons, and celestial bodies in our solar system.
Nagamese: Space mission bur te amikhan solar system laga dusra planet, moon aro celestial body bur ke sai ase. "Curiosity" aro "Perseverance" naam laga Mars rover bur toh Mars surface ke sai ase.

English: The Mars rovers, including Curiosity and Perseverance, have explored the surface of Mars.
Nagamese: Voyager probe bur toh solar system chadi kene interstellar space ke sai ase.

English: The Voyager probes have left the solar system and are exploring interstellar space.
Nagamese: Space exploration pora technology te bhi improvement dikhai dise, jese satellite communication aro medical imaging.

English: Space exploration has led to many technological advancements, including satellite communication and medical imaging.
Nagamese: Space debris, jeme defunct satellite aro rocket laga hissa bur, space te orbit karuthaka spacecraft nimite khatra hoi sakhe.

English: Space debris, including defunct satellites and spent rocket stages, poses a risk to spacecraft in orbit.
Nagamese: Space laga environment toh bhut bisi khorab ase, etu te temperature bhut bisi high aro low ase, vacuum ase, aro radiation bhi ase.

English: The space environment is harsh, with extreme temperatures, vacuum, and radiation.
Nagamese: Spacesuit bur toh astronaut ke space te khatra bur pora bachai bole banaise.

English: Spacesuits are designed to protect astronauts from the hazards of space.
Nagamese: Dusra grah te jindagi ase ki nai etu khoji ulua toh space exploration laga main kaam ase.

English: The search for extraterrestrial life is a major focus of space exploration.
Nagamese: SETI (Search for Extraterrestrial Intelligence) pora radio telescope use kori kene dusra civilization pora signal sunibo.

English: SETI (Search for Extraterrestrial Intelligence) uses radio telescopes to listen for signals from other civilizations.
Nagamese: SETI (Search for Extraterrestrial Intelligence) pora radio telescope use kori dusra civilization khan signal hunibo nimite ase.

English: The Fermi paradox asks why we have not yet detected signs of extraterrestrial life, given the vastness of the universe.
Nagamese: Fermi paradox etu kotha hudi ase ki universe itna dangi hoikena bhi, kile amikhan etiya tak extraterrestrial life thaka sign dikha nai.

English: The Drake Equation is a probabilistic argument used to estimate the number of active, communicative extraterrestrial civilizations in the Milky Way galaxy.
Nagamese: Drake Equation para Milky Way galaxy te kitna active, baat-chit koribo parawala extraterrestrial civilization ase bole estimate kora nimite use kore.

English: Exoplanets are planets that orbit stars outside our solar system.
Nagamese: Exoplanets khan etu planet ase jun amikhan solar system bahar star charifale ghure.

English: Thousands of exoplanets have been discovered, some of which may have conditions suitable for life.
Nagamese: Hazaron exoplanets pawa jaise, etu bhitar pora kunibakhan life nimite thik ase.

English: The habitable zone is the region around a star where conditions may be right for liquid water to exist on a planet's surface.
Nagamese: Habitable zone etu star charifale thaka ekta jaga ase junte liquid pani thakibo para conditions ase.

English: The study of exoplanets helps scientists understand the prevalence and diversity of planetary systems in the universe.
Nagamese: Exoplanets khan study korile scientist khan universe te kitna planetary systems ase bole jani bole madad kore.

English: The James Webb Space Telescope (JWST) is an upcoming space observatory that will study the formation of stars, galaxies, and planetary systems.
Nagamese: James Webb Space Telescope (JWST) ekta space observatory ase jun star, galaxies aro planetary systems keneka bonaise etu study koribo.

English: Space agencies, including NASA, ESA, Roscosmos, and others, collaborate on space missions and research.
Nagamese: Space agencies, jineka NASA, ESA, Roscosmos aro dusra khan, eklogote space missions aro research te kam kore.

English: Space tourism is a growing industry, with companies planning to offer trips to space for private individuals.
Nagamese: Space tourism ekta business ase junte company khan private manuh khan ke space te juwa nimite plan kori ase.

English: The Space Race was a competition between the United States and the Soviet Union to achieve milestones in space exploration.
Nagamese: Space Race United States aro Soviet Union majot space exploration te aagey jabo nimite competition thakise.

English: The Apollo program landed astronauts on the moon and brought them safely back to Earth.
Nagamese: Apollo program toh astronaut khan ke moon te utharise aro safe pora Earth te wapas anise.

English: The Space Shuttle was a reusable spacecraft used by NASA for space missions from 1981 to 2011.
Nagamese: Space Shuttle ekta reusable spacecraft thakise, etu NASA use korishe space mission khan nimite 1981 pora 2011 tak.

English: Space telescopes, such as the Chandra X-ray Observatory and the Spitzer Space Telescope, study the universe across different wavelengths of light.
Nagamese: Space telescope khan jese Chandra X-ray Observatory aro Spitzer Space Telescope, universe ke different light waves pora study kore.

English: The study of gravitational waves, predicted by Albert Einstein's theory of general relativity, has opened a new window on the universe.
Nagamese: Gravitational waves ke study kora, Albert Einstein’s general relativity theory pora janaishe, etu universe dikhibole ekta nawa window khulise.

English: The Event Horizon Telescope captured the first image of a black hole in 2019.
Nagamese: Event Horizon Telescope first time black hole ke 2019 te photo loishe.

English: The study of exoplanets has revealed a wide range of planetary types, including hot Jupiters, super-Earths, and mini-Neptunes.
Nagamese: xoplanets ke study korikena planetary types khan dikhaishe, jese hot Jupiters, super-Earths, aro mini-Neptunes.

English: The search for Earth-like exoplanets focuses on finding planets with similar size, temperature, and composition to our own.
Nagamese: Earth-like exoplanets biharibole hoile planet khan size, temperature, aro composition amikhan laga nisina thakibo lage.

English: The concept of terraforming involves transforming a planet or moon to make it habitable for human life.
Nagamese: Terraforming naam ke ekta idea hisab pora planet ba moon ke human life thakibole banabole pare.

English: The International Astronomical Union (IAU) is responsible for naming celestial objects and defining the rules for classification.
Nagamese: International Astronomical Union (IAU) celestial objects ke naam dibole aro classification nimite rules banaibo nimite responsible ase.

English: The study of astrobiology seeks to understand the origin, evolution, and distribution of life in the universe
Nagamese: Astrobiology etu origin, evolution, aro universe te life kidhori felaise etu bujhibole nimite study kore.

English: The study of astrochemistry investigates the chemical processes occurring in space, including the formation of complex molecules.
Nagamese: Astrochemistry toh space te kimia kaam keneka hoi etu janibo nimite study kora, juntu complex molecules bonaishe.

English: The study of astrogeology examines the geological processes and features of celestial bodies, such as planets, moons, and asteroids.
Nagamese: Astrogeology toh heavenly bodies manuhe najana kenebi ase, jineka planets aro moons etu janibo nimite study kora.

English: Space weather refers to the environmental conditions in space, including solar flares, cosmic rays, and geomagnetic storms.
Nagamese: Space weather mane space poribesh kenebi ase, jineka solar flares, cosmic rays, aro geomagnetic storms.

English: Solar eclipses occur when the moon passes between the sun and Earth, casting a shadow on the Earth's surface.
Nagamese: Solar eclipses toh jun toh suraj aro earth majot ahile earth upor te saya dibo.

English: Lunar eclipses occur when Earth passes between the sun and the moon, causing Earth's shadow to fall on the moon.
Nagamese: Lunar eclipses toh earth toh suraj aro jun majot ahile jun upor te saya dibo.

English: The search for dark matter is one of the most significant challenges in modern astrophysics.
Nagamese: Dark matter khojibo toh etu modern astrophysics te sobse dangor muskil ase.

English: The study of the cosmic microwave background radiation provides insights into the early universe and the conditions shortly after the Big Bang.
Nagamese: Cosmic microwave background radiation pora universe keneka start hoishe jani paribo.

English: Gravitational lensing occurs when the gravitational field of a massive object bends light from a more distant source.
Nagamese: Gravitational lensing toh etu gravitational field dangor chij pora aru dur chij pora light bend kore.

English: Space archaeology uses satellite imagery and remote sensing techniques to study archaeological sites on Earth.
Nagamese: Space archaeology toh satellite image use kori earth te purana jaga ase etu study kore.

English: The study of space law addresses legal issues related to space exploration, including property rights and international cooperation.
Nagamese: Space law toh space sob thik thakibo nimite law ase, jineka property kisu aru international milijuli koribo nimite.

English: The study of space medicine focuses on understanding and mitigating the health effects of space travel on astronauts.
Nagamese: Space medicine toh study kore astronauts khan uporte space travel pora ki health problem hoi asey aru taikene kemiti komti koribo pare.

English: Space colonies and habitats are envisioned as future settlements for human beings beyond Earth.
Nagamese: Space colony aru habitat khan toh future-te manu jatis Earth para bahar-te thakibo nimite banaishe.

English: The study of space propulsion aims to develop faster and more efficient methods of space travel.
Nagamese: Space propulsion khan study kore kemiti joldi aru asaan tareeka-te space-te jabo pare.

English: The study of space weather forecasting is crucial for protecting satellites, spacecraft, and astronauts from harmful space conditions.
Nagamese: Space weather study important ase satellite, spacecraft aru astronauts khan ke space-te thaka danger khan pora bachabo nimite.

English: The study of space debris mitigation seeks to reduce the risk of collisions in Earth's orbit.
Nagamese: Space debris study kore Earth orbit-te collision huwa chance kemiti komti koribo pare.

English: The study of space elevators explores the possibility of building structures that could transport people and cargo from Earth to space without rockets.
Nagamese: Space elevator study kori thake ki pora manu aru saman khan ke lift hisab-te Earth pora space-te loisakibo pare without rocket use nakori kene.

English: The study of space mining investigates the potential extraction of resources from asteroids, moons, and other celestial bodies.
Nagamese: Space mining toh etu biswas ase ki asteroids, moons aru dusra celestial bodies pora valuable chiz kemiti nikalibo pare.

English: The study of space psychology examines the psychological challenges faced by astronauts during long-duration space missions.
Nagamese: Space psychology toh dikhai ki astronauts khan ke space mission-te ki dukh aru tension hoi.

English: The study of space ethics considers the moral implications of human activities in space, including environmental protection and equitable access to resources.
Nagamese: Space ethics toh manu khan space-te ki kaam koribo pare aru ki napare, environment bachai kene aru sob manu khan ke moka di kene.

English: Geography is the study of the Earth's landscapes, environments, and the relationships between people and their surroundings.
Nagamese: Geography toh study ase Earth kaisa dikhai diye, environment kaisa ase, aru manu aru surroundings bhitor-te ki connection ase.

English: It encompasses physical features such as mountains, rivers, and deserts.
Nagamese: Ito phyzikal chiz bishi ase jine ki pahar, nodi, aru desert.

English: It also includes human elements like cities, cultures, and economies.
Nagamese: Ito manu bishi elements bhi ase jine ki shahar, culture, aru paisa sob.

English: The Earth's surface is divided into continents, which are large landmasses surrounded by water.
Nagamese: Earth uporte continents bishi ase, etu mati dam bishi ase aru charifale pani ase.

English: There are seven continents: Africa, Antarctica, Asia, Europe, North America, South America, and Australia.
Nagamese: Saat ta continents ase: Africa, Antarctica, Asia, Europe, North America, South America, aru Australia.

English: The largest ocean on Earth is the Pacific Ocean, followed by the Atlantic, Indian, Southern, and Arctic Oceans.
Nagamese: Earth te sabse dangor ocean Pacific Ocean ase, aru pichete Atlantic, Indian, Southern, aru Arctic Oceans ase.

English: Mountains are landforms with high elevation and steep slopes.
Nagamese: Pahar itu mati dam ase aru dhala bishi ase.

English: The highest mountain in the world is Mount Everest, located in the Himalayas on the border between Nepal and China.
Nagamese: Duniya te sabse uncha pahar Mount Everest ase, itu Himalayas te Nepal aru China border te ase.

English: Rivers are natural watercourses that flow towards larger bodies of water, such as seas or oceans.
Nagamese: Nodi itu pani rasta ase jun mati dam pani laga jage te jai jine ki sea aru ocean.

English: The longest river in the world is the Nile River, located in northeastern Africa.
Nagamese: Duniya te sabse lamba nodi Nile River ase, itu northeastern Africa te ase.

English: Deserts are arid regions with little rainfall and sparse vegetation.
Nagamese: Desert itu shukha jaga ase jaha te kamti barik pani hoi aru gas bhi kamti ase.

English: The Sahara Desert in Africa is the largest hot desert in the world.
Nagamese: Africa te thaka Sahara Desert dunia ta sobse bishi garam desert ase.

English: Forests are areas with dense tree coverage and abundant biodiversity.
Nagamese: Jungle khan sobse bishi gach aru alag alag jibi jontu thaka jaga ase.

English: The Amazon Rainforest in South America is the largest tropical rainforest in the world.
Nagamese: South America te thaka Amazon Rainforest dunia ta sobse bishi dangor tropical jungle ase.

English: Islands are landmasses surrounded by water.
Nagamese: Island khan pani majot thaka jamin bur ase.

English: The largest island in the world is Greenland, located in the North Atlantic Ocean.
Nagamese: Dunia ta sobse dangor island Greenland ase, etu North Atlantic Ocean te ase.

English: Plate tectonics is the theory that Earth's outer shell is divided into several large plates that float on the semi-fluid asthenosphere below.
Nagamese: Plate tectonics etu theory ase juntu Earth outer shell khan dangor dangor plate hisab te divide hoikena thake aru etu khan asthenosphere upar te bahi thake.

English: Earthquakes, volcanoes, and mountain formation are all related to plate tectonics.
Nagamese: Earthquake, volcano, aru pahar bona khan sob plate tectonics logote connected ase.

English: Weathering and erosion are geological processes that shape the Earth's surface over time.
Nagamese: Weathering aru erosion khan geological kaam ase juntu Earth surface to change kore time hisab se.

English: Glaciers are large masses of ice that move slowly over land.
Nagamese: Glacier khan bishi dangor ice bur ase juntu slowly jamin upar te move kore.

English: Climate refers to the long-term patterns of temperature, precipitation, and other atmospheric conditions in a particular region.
Nagamese: Climate etu long time tak temperature, borokha, aru dusra hawa conditions ekta particular jaga te keman thake etu to bole.

English: The Earth's climate zones include polar, temperate, and tropical regions.
Nagamese: Earth toh climate zone te polar, temperate aro tropical region ase.

English: Latitude and altitude are factors that influence climate.
Nagamese: Latitude aro altitude manu climate ke influence kore. Hurricane, tornado aro tsunami nisina natural disaster khan landscape aro community uporte dangor asor pore.

English: Natural disasters such as hurricanes, tornadoes, and tsunamis can have significant impacts on landscapes and communities.
Geography plays a crucial role in shaping human history and culture.
Nagamese: Silk Road purana trade route thakise, jun East Asia ke Europe aro Africa logote connect korise.

English: The Silk Road was an ancient network of trade routes that connected East Asia with Europe and Africa.
Nagamese: Geography manu itihas aro culture bonabote important role play kore.

English: The Nile River Valley was the cradle of one of the world's earliest civilizations, Ancient Egypt.
Nagamese: Nile River Valley dunia purana civilization, Ancient Egypt nimite cradle nisina thakise.

English: Geography affects economic activities such as agriculture, mining, and tourism.
Nagamese: Geography economic kaam, jene agriculture, mining aro tourism ke asor kore.

English: The Industrial Revolution transformed cities and landscapes through the development of factories and infrastructure.
Nagamese: Industrial Revolution factory aro infrastructure bonai kene city aro landscape purapuri change kori dise.

English: Urbanization is the process of population migration from rural areas to cities.
Nagamese: Urbanization hoile manu rural area pora city te migrate huah process.

English: Megacities are urban areas with populations exceeding ten million people.
Nagamese: Megacity hoile jun city te dush million pora bhi besi manu thake.

English: Geographical information systems (GIS) are tools used to capture, analyze, and display spatial data.
Nagamese: Geographical information systems (GIS) manu data collect, analyze aro spatial data dekhawabote use kore.

English: Cartography is the science and art of mapmaking.
Nagamese: Cartography hoile map bonabote science aro art.

English: Topographic maps show the elevation and physical features of an area.
Nagamese: Topographic map ekta area elevation aro physical features dekhai.

English: Political maps display boundaries between countries, states, and cities.
Nagamese: Political map country, state aro city majote boundary dekhai.

English: Population density maps illustrate the distribution of people in a particular area.
Nagamese: Population density map ekta particular area te manu distribution kochu ase etu dekhai.

English: Remote sensing involves collecting data about Earth's surface from satellites and aircraft.
Nagamese: Remote sensing manu Earth surface pora satellite aro aircraft pora data collect kore.

English: Geographic information is used in fields such as urban planning, environmental management, and disaster response.
Nagamese: Geographic information khan urban planning, environmental management aro disaster response nisina field te use kore.

English: The study of biogeography examines the distribution of plant and animal species across different habitats.
Nagamese: Biogeography study te different habitat uporte plant aro animal species distribution dekhe.

English: Cultural geography explores how human societies interact with their environments and each other.
Nagamese: Cultural geography manu society environment aro ek dusre ke nisina interact kore etu dekhe.

English: Human geography investigates population demographics, migration patterns, and urbanization trends.
Nagamese: Human geography population demographics, migration pattern aro urbanization trend khan investigate kore.

English: Physical geography focuses on natural processes such as erosion, weathering, and climatology.
Nagamese: Physical geography toh natural kaam khan pora focus kore, jineka mati khan khoya, weather keneka ase, aro climate sob.

English: Geopolitics examines the relationships between geography, politics, and international affairs.
Nagamese: Geopolitics toh geography, politics, aro international kaam khan majot keneka rista ase etu dikhai.

English: Environmental geography studies the impact of human activities on the natural environment.
Nagamese: Environmental geography pora manuhe environment uporte ki asor kore etu jane.

English: Geographic literacy is the ability to understand and interpret geographical concepts and information.
Nagamese: Geographic literacy hoile geographic concept aro information bhal se bujhi bole pare.

English: Geographic disparities refer to inequalities in access to resources, opportunities, and quality of life across different regions.
Nagamese: Geographic disparities mane alag alag jagate resources, chance, aro jineka jibon to chola luk khan thik nehi thake.

English: Geographic features such as mountains and oceans can act as barriers or conduits for human movement and trade.
Nagamese: Geographic features jineka pahar aro doria manu khan jua aru business koribole rasta dikhai nai toh bandh bhi kore.

English: The study of geographic patterns helps us understand spatial relationships and make informed decisions about resource management and land use.
Nagamese: Geographic pattern pora ami jane kun jaga keneka ase aru etu pora ami kheti bari aru itu khan bhal se koribo pare.

English: Geographic knowledge is essential for addressing global challenges such as climate change, biodiversity loss, and natural disasters.
Nagamese: Geographic knowledge toh duniate climate change, biodiversity komti howa, aro natural disaster khan samna koribole important ase.

English: Geography is a dynamic field that continues to evolve as we explore new technologies and confront emerging environmental and social issues.
Nagamese: Geography toh eku dynamic field ase, etu toh notun technology aru environmental aro social problem samna koribole karne dhori ase.

English: Environmental study is the interdisciplinary field of science that examines the interactions between humans and their environment.
Nagamese: Environmental study toh science pora ase etu pora manu aro environment majot keneka rista ase dekhe.

English: It encompasses the study of natural resources, ecosystems, pollution, climate change, and sustainable development.
Nagamese: Etu pora natural resources, ecosystems, pollution, climate change, aro sustainable development sob study kore.

English: Environmental scientists work to understand the impact of human activities on the environment and develop strategies for conservation and preservation.
Nagamese: Environmental scientist khan manuhe environment uporte ki asor kore etu jane aru etu bachabo lagi bole plan banae.

English: The study of ecology focuses on the relationships between organisms and their environment.
Nagamese: Ecology toh organisms aru environment majot keneka rista ase etu uporte focus kore.

English: Ecosystems are complex networks of plants, animals, and microorganisms interacting with each other and their physical surroundings.
Nagamese: Ecosystems toh plants, animals, aru microorganisms ekta junte thaki bohut complicated kaam kore.

English: Biodiversity refers to the variety of life forms found in a particular habitat or ecosystem.
Nagamese: Biodiversity mane eku jaga te koto different type life ase.

English: Conservation biology is the branch of environmental science that aims to protect and preserve biodiversity.
Nagamese: Conservation biology toh environmental science branch ase, etu biodiversity bachabole help kore.

English: Habitat destruction, pollution, and climate change are major threats to global biodiversity.
Nagamese: Habitat dukan howa, pollution, aro climate change pora biodiversity komti hoi ase.

English: Environmental degradation occurs when natural resources are depleted or ecosystems are damaged.
Nagamese: Environmental degradation toh natural resources komti hoile nai toh ecosystems damage hoile hoi.

English: Deforestation, overfishing, and urbanization are examples of human activities that contribute to environmental degradation.
Nagamese: Jungle sapha korile, besi machi marile, aro city dangor korile pora environmental degradation hoi."

English: Climate change is the long-term alteration of temperature and weather patterns in a region.
Nagamese: Climate change mane temperature aro weather pattern besi din tak change hoile.

English: Global warming refers to the increase in Earth's average temperature due to the release of greenhouse gases such as carbon dioxide and methane.
Nagamese: Global warming koile earth (prithvi) nem aaverage (sadharan) temperature (tapman) bishi huwa ke koi, etu greenhouse gases nisina carbon dioxide aro methane oluwa karone hoi.

English: The greenhouse effect is the natural process by which greenhouse gases trap heat in the Earth's atmosphere, warming the planet.
Nagamese: Greenhouse effect etu natural (prakritik) process ase juntu dwara greenhouse gases buri earth (prithvi) atmosphere (vatavaran) te heat (garmi) dhori rakhe, aru planet (graha) ke garam kore.

English: Anthropogenic activities, such as burning fossil fuels and deforestation, have intensified the greenhouse effect, leading to accelerated climate change.
Nagamese: Manuh dwara kora kam, juntu nisina jala fossil fuels aro jungle katikena, "greenhouse effect" ke bishi kori deise, etu karone climate change bohut joldi hoikena ase.

English: The consequences of climate change include rising sea levels, more frequent extreme weather events, and shifts in ecosystems and habitats.
Nagamese: Climate change pora sea level (samudra level) bishi hoi, baru baru kharap bator huwa, aro ecosystem aro habitat te change hoi.

English: Environmental pollution is the release of harmful substances into the air, water, or soil.
Nagamese: Environmental pollution etu harmful (nuksan deh) jinish buri air, water, ya soil te oluwa ke koi.

English: Air pollution is caused by emissions from vehicles, industrial processes, and power plants.
Nagamese: Air pollution gari, industrial (udyogik) process aro power plants pora nikla emissions karone hoi.

English: Water pollution results from the discharge of chemicals, sewage, and waste into rivers, lakes, and oceans.
Nagamese: Water pollution chemicals, sewage aro waste nadi, pukhuri aro samudra te oluwa pora hoi.

English: Soil pollution occurs when contaminants, such as heavy metals and pesticides, accumulate in the soil, affecting plant growth and water quality.
Nagamese: Soil pollution etu contaminants nisina heavy metals aro pesticides soil te jama huwa pora hoi, etu pora gas buri dhik pora nadikhe aru water quality ke bi kharap kore.

English: Plastic pollution is a growing environmental concern, with plastic waste contaminating waterways and harming marine life.
Nagamese: Plastic pollution ekta dangor pareshani ase, plastic waste buri jol te jai aru marine life (samundar jiwan) ke nuksan kore.

English: The study of environmental toxicology examines the effects of pollutants on human health and ecosystems.
Nagamese: Environmental toxicology pollution pora manuh body aro ecosystem te ki asar pare etu bisoy te pore.

English: Acid rain is a type of precipitation that contains high levels of sulfuric and nitric acids, resulting from air pollution.
Nagamese: Acid rain etu ek rakam precipitation ase juntu te sulfuric aro nitric acids bishi hoi, etu air pollution karone hoi.

English: Ozone depletion refers to the thinning of the ozone layer in the Earth's stratosphere, caused by the release of ozone-depleting substances such as chlorofluorocarbons (CFCs).
Nagamese: Ozone depletion etu ozone layer patla huwa ke koi, etu chlorofluorocarbons (CFCs) nisina jinish oluwa karone hoi.

English: The ozone layer protects the Earth from harmful ultraviolet (UV) radiation from the sun.
Nagamese: Ozone layer earth (prithvi) ke harmful (nuksan deh) ultraviolet (UV) radiation pora bachai.

English: The Montreal Protocol is an international treaty aimed at phasing out the production and use of ozone-depleting substances.
Nagamese: Montreal Protocol ekta international treaty ase juntu ozone kamti kora jinish buri bandh karne banai.

English: Sustainable development is the concept of meeting the needs of the present without compromising the ability of future generations to meet their own needs.
Nagamese: Sustainable development etu ajikali manuh ke dorkar pura kora ke koi future (anewala) generation ke taklif nadikhe.

English: The study of environmental economics examines the relationship between economic activity and environmental sustainability.
Nagamese: Environmental economics economic kaam aru paryavaran ke sathi te chalawa bisoy te koi.

English: Renewable energy sources, such as solar, wind, and hydroelectric power, offer alternatives to fossil fuels and reduce greenhouse gas emissions.
Nagamese: Renewable energy sources, nisina solar, wind, aro hydroelectric power, fossil fuels ke badli hoi aru greenhouse gas emissions ke kamti kore.

English: The transition to a low-carbon economy requires investment in clean energy technologies and energy efficiency measures.
Nagamese: Low-carbon economy te jana pora clean energy technologies aro energy bacha wala kaam ke karna pare.

English: Environmental policy refers to laws, regulations, and initiatives aimed at protecting the environment and promoting sustainable practices.
Nagamese: Environmental policy etu kanun aro niyam ase juntu environment bachawa aro sustainable kaam ke support kare.

English: Environmental impact assessments (EIAs) are used to evaluate the potential environmental effects of proposed projects or developments.
Nagamese: Environmental impact assessments (EIAs) etu paryavaran te asar check karna ke koi project ke banawa ya start kara age.

English: Environmental education promotes awareness and understanding of environmental issues and encourages individuals to take action to protect the environment.
Nagamese: Environmental education mane okole poribesh ke bhujibo lage aro environmental problems khan ke buji kene poribesh to bachabo nimete kaam koribo lage.

English: Ecotourism is a form of tourism that promotes responsible travel to natural areas, supporting conservation efforts and local communities.
Nagamese: Ecotourism ekta tourism form ase, etu natural jaga te responsible hoi kene travel koribo lage, conservation kaam aro local community ke madad koribo lage.

English: Marine conservation aims to protect and preserve marine ecosystems and biodiversity.
Nagamese: Marine conservation mane doriya ke aro doriya bhitor te thaka jibon khan ke bachabo lage.

English: Coral reefs are among the most diverse and productive ecosystems on Earth, providing habitat for a wide range of marine species.
Nagamese: Coral reefs ekta dangor jaga ase juntu te dher dhoronor machli khan thake.

English: Overfishing, pollution, and climate change threaten the health of coral reefs worldwide.
Nagamese: Overfishing, pollution aro climate change nimete coral reefs khan nosto hoi ase.

English: Marine protected areas (MPAs) are designated areas of ocean set aside for conservation purposes, where fishing and other activities may be restricted.
Nagamese: Marine protected areas (MPAs) mane doriya ke safe rakhibo nimete jaga banaise, juntu jaga te fishing aro dusra kaam koribo nimete rule ase.

English: The study of oceanography explores the physical, chemical, biological, and geological processes that occur in the ocean.
Nagamese: Oceanography study mane physical, chemical, biological aro geological processes khan doriya te ki hoi ase etu janibo nimete ase.

English: The Great Pacific Garbage Patch is a large area of marine debris in the North Pacific Ocean, consisting mostly of plastic waste.
Nagamese: The Great Pacific Garbage Patch ekta doriya te plastic kachra dangor jaga ase juntu North Pacific Ocean te ase.

English: Environmental justice is the principle that all people, regardless of race, ethnicity, or socioeconomic status, have the right to a clean and healthy environment.
Nagamese: Environmental justice mane sob manu, race, jaati, dhan thakuk wa nathakuk, sobke saf aro bhal poribesh paabo nimete hak ase.

English: Environmental racism refers to the disproportionate burden of environmental pollution and hazards borne by marginalized communities.
Nagamese: Environmental racism mane pollution aro khrab environment khan minority community manu khan dukh pae.

English: Environmental monitoring involves the collection and analysis of data to assess the state of the environment and track changes over time.
Nagamese: Environmental monitoring mane data collect koribo lage environment kaisa ase aro time hisab te ki change hoi ase etu jannibo nimete.

English: Remote sensing technologies, such as satellites and drones, are used to monitor environmental changes from a distance.
Nagamese: Satellite aro drone jaishe technology use kori kene environment change khan dur pora nazar rakhibo pare.

English: Citizen science projects engage members of the public in environmental monitoring and data collection efforts.
Nagamese: Citizen science project te public manu khan ke milai kene environment ke nazar rakhibo aro data collect koribo pare.

English: Restoration ecology is the scientific discipline that focuses on restoring degraded ecosystems to a more natural or functional state.
Nagamese: Restoration ecology ekta science ase, etu nosto hoi jowa ecosystem ke phirse bhal banaibo nimete kaam kore.

English: Reforestation and afforestation efforts aim to replant trees in areas that have been deforested or degraded.
Nagamese: Reforestation aro "afforestation" mane juntu jaga te gas khan katise etu jaga te phirse gas ropibo lage.

English: Wetland restoration projects help to preserve and restore critical habitats for birds, fish, and other wildlife.
Nagamese: Wetland restoration project nimete birds, fish, aro dusra jungle janwar khan thakibo nimete help hobo.

English: Sustainable agriculture practices minimize the environmental impact of farming while maintaining productivity and profitability.
Nagamese: Sustainable agriculture mane farming to environment ke khrab nakori kene koribo lage aro profit bhi hobo lage.

English: Organic farming avoids the use of synthetic pesticides and fertilizers, relying instead on natural methods to control pests and improve soil fertility.
Nagamese: Organic farming te synthetic pesticide aro fertilizer use nakori kene natural chiz use koribo lage.

English: Agroforestry integrates trees and shrubs into agricultural landscapes, providing benefits such as shade, erosion control, and habitat for wildlife.
Nagamese: Agroforestry mane gas aro jhup khan lagaikene farming koribo lage, etu dhoop kamti hobo nimete aro mati bachabo nimete madad kore.

English: The study of agroecology examines the ecological processes that occur in agricultural systems and seeks to promote sustainable farming practices.
Nagamese: Agroecology study mane farming system te ki hoi ase etu janibo nimete aro sustainable farming koribo nimete help kore.

English: Soil conservation measures, such as contour plowing and terracing, help to prevent soil erosion and degradation.
Nagamese: Mati bachabo lagi kaam koribo lage, jiman ki contour plowing aru terracing, etu mati dhongkho hoikena najabo nimite aru mati laga quality to thik rakhibo nimite madad kore.

English: Desertification is the process by which fertile land becomes desert as a result of deforestation, overgrazing, and unsustainable agricultural practices.
Nagamese: Desertification etu eku process ase, juntu fertile mati desert bonoikena jai, jungle katikena, besi goru charaikena, aru thik nathaka kheti kaam usor pora hoi.

English: The study of hydrology explores the distribution, movement, and quality of water resources on Earth.
Nagamese: Hydrology to etu bisai ase pani kineko distribute hoi, kineko move kore, aru kineko pani besi bhal ase etu prithibi te.

English: Groundwater depletion occurs when the rate of extraction exceeds the rate of recharge, leading to declining water levels and aquifer depletion.
Nagamese: Groundwater komti hobo to etu time te hoi jitia pani ulitai kene besi hoi jai aru pani bhari bole komti hoi, etu usor pora pani level komti hoijai aru aquifer komti hoi jai.

English: Water scarcity is a growing global challenge, exacerbated by population growth, urbanization, and climate change.
Nagamese: Pani nadharibo to dunia te dukh ase, population besi hoi usor pora, shohor bona usor pora, aru climate change usor pora.

English: Water conservation measures, such as rainwater harvesting and drip irrigation, help to reduce water waste and ensure sustainable water management.
Nagamese: Pani bachibo nimite, jiman ki borokha pani to rakhi kene aru drip irrigation, etu madad kore pani waste nokoribo nimite aru pani thik usor pora use koribo nimite.

English: The study of climatology examines long-term patterns and variations in climate, including temperature, precipitation, and atmospheric conditions.
Nagamese: Climatology to etu bisai ase kineko climate thake, temperature, borokha, aru hawa kineko ase.

English: Paleoclimatology uses proxy data, such as tree rings and ice cores, to reconstruct past climates and understand climate change over geological time scales.
Nagamese: Paleoclimatology data use kore, jiman ki gas usor pora pata laga bole past climate aru climate change kineko thakise age te.

English: The Kyoto Protocol is an international treaty aimed at reducing greenhouse gas emissions and mitigating climate change.
Nagamese: Kyoto Protocol etu international treaty ase environment bacha bole aru climate change komti koribo nimite.

English: The Paris Agreement is a global accord that seeks to limit global warming to well below 2 degrees Celsius above pre-industrial levels.
Nagamese: Paris Agreement etu dunia laga accord ase climate change komti koribo nimite 2 degrees Celsius usor pora.

English: Carbon capture and storage (CCS) technologies capture carbon dioxide emissions from power plants and industrial facilities and store them underground.
Nagamese: Carbon capture aru storage (CCS) technologies carbon dioxide ulai power plant aru industrial usor pora aru etu mati niche te rakhi dei.

English: Afforestation and reforestation projects help to sequester carbon dioxide from the atmosphere and mitigate climate change.
Nagamese: Afforestation aru reforestation project madad kore carbon dioxide hawa pora loikena aru climate change komti kore.

English: The study of paleontology examines the history of life on Earth, including the fossil record and evolutionary processes.
Nagamese: Paleontology to etu bisai ase life kineko thakise prithibi te, fossil record aru evolution kineko hoi ase.

English: Mass extinctions have occurred throughout Earth's history, often due to catastrophic events such as asteroid impacts or volcanic eruptions.
Nagamese: Bisi dukh pora extinct hoibo to prithibi history te hoise, etu time te asteoid impacts aru volcanic eruption hoi.

English: The sixth mass extinction is currently underway, driven by human activities such as habitat destruction, pollution, and climate change.
Nagamese: Atha number extinct hoibo to hoikena ase, manu usor pora habitat dhongkho kora, pollution usor pora, aru climate change usor pora.

English: The study of conservation genetics examines the genetic diversity of populations and species and seeks to develop strategies for preserving genetic resources.
Nagamese: Conservation genetics to etu bisai ase population kineko ase aru species aru strategy banaibo nimite genetic resources bachabo lagi.

English: Invasive species are non-native organisms that can have harmful effects on native ecosystems, biodiversity, and human health.

English: Green building design incorporates sustainable materials, energy-efficient systems, and green spaces to minimize environmental impact and promote human health and well-being.
Nagamese: Green building design toh etu building bona time-te environment-ke bacha bole saman use korikena, energy kamti lagibo systems lagikena, aru ghas-bon bhi lagai kena environment-ke bhal rakhibole aru manu khan ke bhi swasth rakhibole nimite ase.

Nagamese: Environmental psychology manu aru jun khan bona huwa environment ase aru jun khan nature pora ase etu khan ke bhitor connections ase koikena dibo, aru environment pora manu khan ke behaviour aru swasth કેneka hoy etu bhi dekhaibo.

English: If you keep your mind and body busy , you will be around a long time.
Nagamese: Tumi mind aru body busy raikile, tumi dighol jinda thakibo.

English: I just haven't died yet.
Nagamese: Moi aji tak mora nai.

English: The secret is smiling and having a good day.
Nagamese: Secret homai hasibo aru bhal din thakibo.

English: Food won't help you live longer.
Nagamese: Khana khanile tumi besi najiba. Jibon'ot khana dher important nai.

English: Food is least important thing in life.
Nagamese: Celebration khan jibon shuru karne time toh lagei.

English: Celebrations seem to be and is initial part of life .
Nagamese: Jinda thakibo di kushi kushi toh tension nalobo.

English: The secret to a long live is not to worry.
Nagamese: Tension dur koribo karne rasta'ot ulaikena manu khan ke hello kobo.

English: The best way to avoid anxiety is to go out in the street and say hello to people.
Nagamese: Mui laga main kam dost aru poroshi khan logote joma hobo.

English: My main hobby is getting together with friends and neighbours.
Nagamese: Din din notun manu logote kotha patibo.

English: Talking each day with the new people.
Nagamese: Tumi bhal puwa khan logote kotha patile, etu toh jibon bisi din jibole karne rasta ase.

English: Talking each day with the people you love, that's the secret to a long life.
Nagamese: Poroshi khan logote kotha barta aru chaa pibo.

English: Chatting and drinking tea with my neighbours.
Nagamese: Dheere dheere aru aram se thakibo, joldi nahakile tumi bisi din jibibo.

English: Slow down, and relax, you live much longer if you are not in a hurry.
Nagamese: Din din alag alag kam koribo.

English: Do many different things everyday.
Nagamese: Din din mui nijeke kobo, mui nijeke bhal pau. Mui dhuniya ase.

English: Everyday I say to myself I love myself.
Nagamese: Mui dhon aru maal wala ase. Mui pisat napharu mui teneibo.

English: I am healthy.
Nagamese: Mui dhuniya ase.

English: I am wealthy
Nagamese: Mui dhon aru maal wala ase.

English: I don't chase   I attract.
Nagamese: Mui pisat napharu mui teneibo.

English: I attract .
Nagamese: Mui teneibo.

English: Dancing in singing with your grand children is the best thing in life.
Nagamese: Nachibo aru gana gaibo apon laga nati nati khan logote etu toh jibon laga dher bhal kam ase.

English: The most important thing in life is to keep smiling.
Nagamese: Jibon'ot dher important toh hansi te hansi te thakibo.

English: There is no secret to it.the trick is just a live.
Nagamese: Eku secret nai etu'ot.

English: They are very proud of their traditions and local culture.
Nagamese: Taikhan laga purana riti-riwaj aro culture nimite bohut ghamand kare.

English: They celebrate all the time even little thing.
Nagamese: Taikhan khanaharu sob time celebrate kore, chutu kura khan o.

English: They are passionate about everything's day do.
Nagamese: Taikhan sob kaam te mon di ase.

English: They have an important purpose in life or survival.
Nagamese: Jibon toh thaka important karon ase nahole bachi thakibo lage.

English: They are always coming and going to sing karaoke, visit the neighbours, or play games.
Nagamese: Taikhan toh ahijai thake gan gabole karaoke te, porosi logot bhet koribo nimite, nahole games khelibo nimite.

English: What secrets to long life do the Japanese hold?
Nagamese: Ki dhoron secrets Japanese manu khan use kore dirgho jibon bachi thakibo nimite?

English: Locals eat a white variety of foods ,specially vegetables.
Nagamese: Etha karone taikhan sob white dhoron khana bostu khai, specially sobji.

English: They eat at least five servings of fruits and vegetables everyday.
Nagamese: Taikhan atleast panch baar fruits aru sobji khai ek ek din te.

English: Grains are the foundations of their diet.
Nagamese: Grains taikhan khanar buniyad ase.

English: The rarely eat sugar.
Nagamese: Taikhan toh komti sugar khai.

English: Reducing the risk of heart attack.
Nagamese: Heart attack hobo dikh komti hoi.

English: Strengthening the immune system.
Nagamese: Immune system toh takat dibo.

English: Dry fruits which contain vitamins and antioxidant and give you energy.
Nagamese: Suka phal khan juntu te vitamins aru antioxidant ase aru tumik takat dibo.

English: Yoga is  comes from India.
Nagamese: Yoga toh India pora ahise.

English: A balloon filled with helium.
Nagamese: Euta balloon juntu helium pora bhorai ase.

English: A person jumping of a diving board.
Nagamese: Euta manu diving board pora jomp marise.

English: The person falls towards the water due to the gravity.
Nagamese: Gravity thaka karone manu pani phale niche porise.

English: A person riding a bicycle
Nagamese: Euta manu bicycle solai ase.

English: The balloon floates upward because helium is less dense then air.
Nagamese: Balloon toh upar phale uri jabo kelemane helium toh hawa pora komti dence ase.

English: Reverse of string using stack.
Nagamese: String ulta koribo nimite stack use kore.

English: Searching in a single link list.
Nagamese: Single link list te khujibo.

English: Insertion in a single linked list.
Nagamese: Single linked list ot bhitor insertion.

English: Insertion in double linked list.
Nagamese: Double linked list ot insertion.

English: Creation of a single linked list.
Nagamese: Single linked list to bonuwa.

English: Array implementation of queue.
Nagamese: Queue to array te implement koribo.

English: Array implementations of stack.
Nagamese: Stack to array te implement koribo.

English: Link list implementations of stack.
Nagamese: Stack to link list te implement koribo.

English: Writing a recursive function.
Nagamese: Recursive function likhibo.

English: Summation of number from 1 to n.
Nagamese: 1 pora n tak number to plus koribo.

English: Displaying number from 1 to n.
Nagamese: 1 pora n tak number dekhuwa.

English: Display and summations of series.
Nagamese: Series to dekhuwa aru plus koribo.

English: Non recursive for binary tree.
Nagamese: Binary tree nimite non-recursive.

Nagamese: Memory te binary tree keneka represent kore.

English: Passing structure members as arguments.
Nagamese: Structure member khan argument hisaabe dibo.

English: Selection sort by exchanging data.
Nagamese: Selection sort data exchange kori kene.

Nagamese: Polynomial arithmetic linked list logote.

English: Flow of control in recursive function.
Nagamese: Recursive function ot flow of control.

English: Link representations of binary tree.
Nagamese: Binary tree nimite link representation.

English: Family is the place where you learn your first lesson in life.
Nagamese: Family to etu jindagi te shika aru bhal path huwa jaga ase.

English: Good values and good morals are always taught in a family.
Nagamese: Bhal niti aru bhal kam family te hikhai.

English: In the family, we are prepared to respect our elders and love younger ones.
Nagamese: Family te loku dangor ke keneka respect dibo lage aru sot ke ke keneka morom dibo lage hikai.

English: We learn lessons consistently from our family, about honesty, dependability, kindness and so on.
Nagamese: Ami family pura hosa, biswas aru doya keneka koribo lage sikhi.

English: My family is the best family for me. I live in a nuclear family of four members.
Nagamese: Moi laga family moi nimite sobse bhal ase. Moi chota family te ase, ami chari jon ase.

English: My father is a teacher.
Nagamese: Baba ekjon teacher ase.

English: He is the man who heads and leads our family.
Nagamese: Baba family laga mukhiya ase.

English: My mother is a housewife as well as a beautician.
Nagamese: Ama laga ma ekjon housewife aru beautician bhi ase.

English: I love my family because they are the jewels of my life.
Nagamese: Moi ama laga family ke morom kore kelemane etu ama laga zindegi laga kimti chiz ase.

English: Christmas is one of the most famous and light-hearted festivals which is celebrated across the world by billions of people.
Nagamese: Christmas ekta dangor festival ase, etu duniya pura billions manu celebrate kore.

English: The greatest wealth is our own health.
Nagamese: Sobse dangor dolot to ama laga health ase.

English: A healthy person can earn lots of money however an unhealthy person cannot because of a lack of motivation, interest, and concentration level.
Nagamese: Ekta bhal manu to paisa kamae pare, hoilebi ekta bimar manu to napare kelemane ta te mon aru khushi nithake.

English: Wealth matters, but, is not as important as health.
Nagamese: Paisa dorkar ase, hoilebi health itna dorkar nai.

English: Rapid technological advances have reduced the dependency on natural resources or the factors in proportion to it.
Nagamese: Technology dinodin unnoti huwa pora natural chiz upor te nirvor kamti hoise.

English: If we look at the global scenario, the modern world is moving exceptionally fast.
Nagamese: Duniya ke dikile, etu nua duniya bohut joldi aage bohi ase.

English: The government spends less money on infrastructure and laboratories.
Nagamese: Sorkar to infrastructure aru laboratory nimite kamti paisa kharcha kore.

English: The parents supported the case for adequate education for the children irrespective of their sex.
Nagamese: Parents khan bhi potiara dise ki chokra aru chokri khan ke bhal porhai dibo lage.

English: It is useful in producing a sensation of solidarity and a feeling of equivalent obligation among boys and girls.
Nagamese: Etu pora chokra aru chokri khan ekta jote thakibo aru ek dosra ke madad koribo nimite.

English: When young boys and girls come closer to each other, they take more care in understanding each other.
Nagamese: Jitia chokra aru chokri khan dosti kore, taikhan ek dosra ke bujibo nimite dhyan thakhe.

English: There are two basic purposes behind education.
Nagamese: Education dikena laga duto main maqsad ase.

English: The first is to free people from ignorance, superstition, bad habits, and many wrong ideas.
Nagamese: Pehla kaam toh janta ke agyan, andhvishwas, bura aadat, aru bisi ulta sidha idea pora mukt koribo lage.

English: Secondly, to provide the citizens of a country with some skill or special kind of knowledge that would enable them to earn a decent living.
Nagamese: Dusra, desh ke nagrik khan ke etu nisina skill dibo lageh, juntu pora taikhan dhang se jibo nimite paisa kamaibo pare.

English: In a highly populated country like India education is a must for both the purposes mentioned.
Nagamese: India nisina bisi population thaka desh te education duto kaam nimite dorkar ase.

English: First, there must be a hundred per cent literacy if the so-called democracy that the constitution guarantees for its citizens is to have any true meaning.
Nagamese: Pehla, ekdom hundred percent literacy thakibo lageh, juntu democracy constitution pora guarantee aise etu sotti mani thakibo lageh.

English: Educated people, must learn to produce things that are in daily demand.
Nagamese: Padha likha jana manu khan, taikhan jinis bonabo sikibo lageh, juntu din din dorkar lage.

English: He has continuously tampered with nature which led to a threat to the sustenance of mankind.
Nagamese: Tai toh hamesha nature ke disturb kori thakishe, etu pora manu jati ke khatra hoi ase jee pai thakibo nimite.

English: The constant more in the world population is the main reason for environmental pollution.
Nagamese: Duniya te manu bisi hoikena environment ke pollution kori ase.

English: To prevent these hazards from endangering human, animal, and plant life measures should be taken before the situation goes out of control.
Nagamese: Etu khatra khan pora manu, janwar, aru gas biacha ke bachabo nimite plan bonabo lageh situation control pora bahar huwa age.

English: Cutting down trees should be made punishable by law.
Nagamese: Gas kaatu toh law pora saja dibo lageh.

English: There is truth in the common saying: “Cleanliness is next to godliness.”
Nagamese: Etu kotha sotti ase: "Safai bhagwaan ke next ase."

English: Cleanliness comes out of a taste for decency.
Nagamese: Safai toh bhal lagibo lage dekhne nimite.

English: The disease is the handmaid of dirt.
Nagamese: Bimari toh gandagi ke dashi ase.

English: Cleanliness of the mind is as necessary as that of the body for self-respect.
Nagamese: Mon ke safai koribo toh dorkar ase jism nisina, izzat nimite.

English: The effects of cleanliness are great.
Nagamese: Safai ke natija bisi ase.

English: Child labour has been quite a problem down the ages.
Nagamese: Soru manu loga kaam toh ekta problem ase bohut zamana pora.

English: Young boys and girls can be paid lesser wages and they do not form unions to realize demands for higher pay.
Nagamese: Soru laki aru laki khan ke kamti paisa di kena kaam karaibo pare aru taikhan union naw bonaikena bisi paisa nimite demand koribo napare.

English: Though child labour is a cruel practice it saves many families from starvation.
Nagamese: Soru manu loga kaam toh ekta beya system hoile bhi etu pora bhukha pora bachai deye.

English: So simply banning the use of child labour one could not solve the problem.
Nagamese: Etu nisina hukum lagai dibo pora problem solve nai hoi.

English: In recent times the government of India has become aware of the evils of the system.
Nagamese: Aajibot India sarkar etu system ke beya boli jani jaise.

English: A hobby is voluntary work done in leisure with pleasure.
Nagamese: Hobby toh nijor khushi pora time pass koribo nimite ase.

English: But my favourite hobby is gardening.
Nagamese: Kintu moi laga favourite hobby toh gardening ase.

English: I started it when I was only ten.
Nagamese: Moi etu ke dukh bochor agey te shuru kurishe.

English: I have a small plot of land beside our house.
Nagamese: Moi laga ghor usorte ekdom chutu jaga ase.

English: There I cultivate gardening.
Nagamese: Titia moi gardening kora.

English: I spend one hour every day gardening.
Nagamese: Moi din-din ek ghanta gardening te time pass kure.

English: Back from the morning walk, I go to my garden with a spade and a waterful bucket.
Nagamese: Morning walk pora ahikena, moi mor garden te spade(mati khunda dangi) aru pani thaka bucket loi kene ja.

English: I face some problems in cultivating gardening.
Nagamese: Gardening koribole time te, moi olop problem face kure.

English: Ambition is a goal or objective to achieve in life.
Nagamese: Ambition matlab toh jindagi te achieve kuribole nemati ase.

English: In order to succeed in life, one must have a goal.
Nagamese: Jindagi te pass hoibole nematey, ekjon manu laga ekta aim thakibo lagey.

English: An aimless man is like a ship without a compass.
Nagamese: Ekjon aim nathaka manu ekta ship nisena ase, kun to compass nathake.

English: So, I have to select an ambition in my life.
Nagamese: Etu karone, moi mor jindagi te ekta ambition select kuribole lagey.

English: Very soon I shall be a citizen of my country.
Nagamese: Bahut jaldi te moi mor desh laga citizen hobole ase.

English: I shall have some duties to society and my country.
Nagamese: Moi laga society aru mor desh nematey duties thakibo.

English: Any noble work needs money.
Nagamese: Kun bhi bhal kaam kuribole nematey poisa dorkar ase.

English: I shall spend a large part of my profit on spreading education and treatment for the poor and sick villagers.
Nagamese: Moi mor profit laga dangi hissa toh education spread kuribole aru gorib aru bemar thaka gao wala khan ke treatment koribole nematey kharch kuribo.

English: I shall start night schools for the adults to make them literate. I shall set up a library.
