Nagamese: Moi raat school shuru kuribo juntu manu khan ke likha pora shikibo nematey.

English: I shall open training centres for young boys and girls to provide them with jobs.
Nagamese: Moi ekta library banaibo. Moi training centre khulibo chutu lora aru suwali khan ke jobs dibole nematey.

English: We can regain lost money and lost health but lost time is gone forever.
Nagamese: Ami poisa aru health piche lobi parey kintu time ekbar gele toh ami aru napai.

English: We should remember that we cannot recall the time that is gone.
Nagamese: Ami yaad rakhibo lagey ki ami time ke piche nalobi parey juntu gele.

English: We should avail ourselves of every opportunity.
Nagamese: Ami mora khan sob opportunity uthaibo lagey.

English: Idle time is said to be a thief of time.
Nagamese: Bekaam thakile time bisi jaai bole.

English: Gandhiji always used to keep a watch to watch his time.
Nagamese: Gandhiji hamesha time ke dhyan di thakise.

English: Trees are of great importance in our everyday life.
Nagamese: Gass khan amikhan laga din haru life te bisi important ase.

English: Both the urban and rural people gain advantages from growing more trees.
Nagamese: Urban aru rural manu khan duio gass besi ruluwa pora faida uthai.

English: We must care for trees for our own sake.
Nagamese: Ami khan gass laga dhyan rakhibo lage amikhan nijor karone.

English: More and more areas are brought under forest cover and people are taught “Plant trees and save a life.”
Nagamese: Aru besi jagah forest bhitor te anibo lage aru manu khan ke sikhai dibo lage "Gass rulu aru life bachao."

English: Morning walk and early rising go hand in hand.
Nagamese: Hokal poka aru morning walk ek logote jaai.

English: It is good exercise after a long night’s rest and provides us with fresh oxygen from the cool morning air.
Nagamese: Eta dhunia exercise ase rati bisi homoi homai piche aru amikhan ke taja oxygen dibo thanda homoi te.

English: A morning walk enables a man to have closer contact with nature.
Nagamese: Morning walk pora manu nature logote aru karibhi jor hoi.

English: Even the doctors advise their patients to have a morning walk daily, as a remedy for various types of physical ailments, especially diabetes.
Nagamese: Doctor khan bhi patient khan ke morning walk koribo lagi bole, kunba physical bimari nemite, specially diabetes nemite.

English: A morning walk is advantageous as an independent exercise.
Nagamese: Morning walk bisi faida ase.

English: If anyone wants to ensure proper care of his or her health, he or she can undertake a morning walk as it is very simple as well as beneficial.
Nagamese: Jodi kunba nijor deh ke bhal rakhibo lage, taile morning walk koribo pare kenki etu bisi easy aru faida bhi ase.

English: Science is a great boon to human civilization.
Nagamese: Science manu jati nimite bisi dhunia blessing ase.

English: All signs of Progress in civilization have been made possible by science.
Nagamese: Jotok progress ase civilization te, sob science pora hoi ase.

English: Science has made our life easy and comfortable.
Nagamese: Science amikhan laga life easy aru aaram banaide.

English: It has given us electric fans, and lights. fans cool us, lights remove darkness. Lift, washing machine, etc. save our labour.
Nagamese: Science amikhan plastic fan, aru light dise. Fan pora thanda kore, light pora andhar harai diye. Lift, washing machine, ityadi pora amikhan laga kaam kamti kore.

English: The computer has taken the excess load off our brains.
Nagamese: Computer amikhan laga dimag pora bisi load kamti koride.

English: Science has given us life-saving medicine.
Nagamese: Science amikhan ke life bachuwa medicine dise.

English: Surgery can do something miraculous.
Nagamese: Surgery bisi acharaj kaam koribo pare.

English: Space flight is another wonder of science.
Nagamese: Space flight bhi science laga aru taajub wali chiz ase.

English: The man who had once lived in the cave has now landed on the science of the moon is a blessing to us.
Nagamese: Juntu manu ekzomai cave te thakisele, etia science pora moon te land kurishe amikhan logote ashirbad ase.

English: Science has given us speed but has taken away our emotions.
Nagamese: Science amikhan ke speed dise, kintu amikhan laga feeling sorkari loise.

English: The introduction of the mobile phone has destroyed the art of letter writing.
Nagamese: Mobile phone ahikena letter likha art to pura nosto kori dise.

English: It has given us the frightful nuclear weapons that can destroy the whole world.
Nagamese: Eitu amikhan ke bhayanak nuclear hathiyar dise, juntu pura world ke nosto kori dibo pare.

English: We can use fire for cooking our food or burning other’s houses.
Nagamese: Ami agun laga pora khana banaibo pare ya dusra manu ghar bhi jalai dibo pare.

English: Any unwanted loud sound which causes stress and irritation can be termed noise pollution.
Nagamese: Kunu bhi unwanted dangor awaz juntu stress aru irritation dibo pare, etu ke noise pollution koise.

English: It is chasing us in almost every step.
Nagamese: Eitu amikhan ke almost sob step te picha kori ase.

English: We are almost deafened by the blaring mikes or the record players which are often played in full volume.
Nagamese: Ami almost behosh hoi jaise dangor mikes ya record players pora juntu pura volume te bajai ase.

English: It may cause impairment of hearing and can cause sleep disruption.
Nagamese: Eitu hearing komti kori dibo pare aru nendro bhi disturb kori dibo pare.

English: It may lead to an abnormal increase in heartbeat and heart palpitation.
Nagamese: Eitu heartbeat aru heart palpitation bhi barhai dibo pare.

English: People must also be made aware of the dangers of noise pollution.
Nagamese: Manu khan bhi noise pollution laga danger ke janibo lage.

English: Television is one of the many wonders of modern science and technology.
Nagamese: Television modern science aru technology laga ekta dangor chiz ase.

English: Previously the radio helped us hear things from far and near and spread information and knowledge from one corner of the globe to another.
Nagamese: Age te radio amikhan ke dur aru najor jaga pora awaz hunibo te madad kurishe aru knowledge aru khobor ekta kone pora dusra kone tak phulai dise.

English: But all this was done through sound only.
Nagamese: Kintu sob awaz pora hoise.

English: Today we can watch games, shows, and song and dance programs from all corners of the world while sitting in our own homes.
Nagamese: Ajikali ami khel, show, aru gana nach program sob kone pora dekhibo pare ghor te bosi thakikena.

English: But as in all things, too much televiewing may prove harmful.
Nagamese: Kintu jese sob chiz te, besi television dekhibo pora nuksan bhi hoi pare.

English: TV provides visual images but the televiewer has a limited choice of programs.
Nagamese: TV dekhai kintu televiewer laga program komti thake.

English: But as for the book, a reader’s imagination plays a vital role.
Nagamese: Kintu kitab laga hisab te, ekta parhne wala laga dimag bhi dorkar ase.

English: He can freely read a book which is a personal activity and it cannot be shared with others at the same time.
Nagamese: Taikhan kitab porhibo pare, eitu ekta nijor kam ase aru dusra logote share koribo napare ek samay te.

English: When we read books, we have to use our intelligence and imagination.
Nagamese: Jab ami kitab parhe, ami dimag aru imagination use koribo lage.

English: Kerala has the highest number of constituencies as all 20 seats went poll on Friday
Nagamese: Kerala te sobse besi constituency ase kelemane kunki pura 20 ta seat shukrobar dina vote hoishe.

English: An essay is nothing but a piece of content which is written from the perception of writer or author.
Nagamese: Essay ekta content nisina he ase, kun writer ba author nijor point of view pora likhi ase.

English: First of all, Education teaches the ability to read and write.
Nagamese: First time toh, Education pora likhiboley aru pobole janibo dey.

English: Consequently, Education makes people literate.
Nagamese: Etu karoneh, Education pora manu literate hoi.

English: This is due to the skills of a high paying job that Education provides.
Nagamese: Etu toh high paying job nimiteh Education pora skill dey.

English: Uneducated people are probably at a huge disadvantage when it comes to jobs.
Nagamese: Juntu manu poba likha nai, taikhan nimiteh job pawo toh bohut mushkil ase.

English: It seems like many poor people improve their lives with the help of Education.
Nagamese: Dikhiboley ase ki bohut grib manu khan Education madad pora nijor jibon bhal koreh.

English: Education makes an individual a better user of technology.
Nagamese: Education pora ekta manu technology bhal use koriboley janey.

English: Hence, without Education, it would probably be difficult to handle modern machines.
Nagamese: Etukarneh, Education nathakileh, etu bohut muskil hoi modern mashin khan handle kuriboleh.

English: Educated people also realize the value of time much more.
Nagamese: Pobha manu khan time laga value besi janey.

English: To educated people, time is equal to money.
Nagamese: Eduacted manu khan nimiteh, time aru poisa ekkekena ase.

English: Hence, educated people are quite likely to convince people to their point of view.
Nagamese: Etukarne, educated manu khan khan kunuba manu khan ke nijor side te anibo pareh.

English: Education is a ray of light in the darkness.
Nagamese: Education andhar te ekta ujala nisina ase.

English: It certainly is a hope for a good life.
Nagamese: Etu asha dey ki jibon bhal hobo boley.

English: To deny this right is evil.
Nagamese: Etu hakk nahibo boley kowa toh ekta burra baat ase.

English: Education is a basic right of every Human on this Planet.
Nagamese: Education toh sob manu laga ekta hakk ase etu prithibi te.

English: Uneducated youth is the worst thing for Humanity.
Nagamese: Poba likha nalaka lora kuwa khan toh humanity nimiteh bohut kharab baat ase.

English: Above all, the governments of all countries must ensure to spread Education.
Nagamese: Sobse upar te toh, sob desh laga sarkar khan toh Education dey boley dyaan diboleh lagey.

English: Education helps in Employment by providing necessary skills.
Nagamese: Education pora Employment te madad korey kunki dorkar thaka skills dey.

English: These skills are important for doing a high paying job.
Nagamese: Etu skills khan toh dorkar ase high paying job kuriboleh.

English: Education helps society by spreading knowledge.
Nagamese: Education pora gyan bilai kene society ke help kore.

English: This certainly is one excellent contribution to Education.
Nagamese: I tu education nimite ekta dhuniya contribution ase.

English: The Continental GT650 has a very refined engine and excellent build quality.
Nagamese: Continental GT650 khan bohut bhal engine ase aru build quality bhi bohut bhal ase.

English: This tastefully customised Royal Enfield Continental GT 650 might be the best-looking Continental GT 650 we have ever seen.
Nagamese: I tastefully customise Royal Enfield Continental GT 650 khan dikhibole bohut dhuniya ase, ami etiya tak jiman bhi Continental GT 650 dikhise, etu tai khan bhitorote ekdom dhuniya ase.

English: The Royal Enfield Continental GT 650 is a 648cc cafe racer
Nagamese: Royal Enfield Continental GT 650 hoile ekta 648cc cafe racer ase.

English: The Royal Enfield Continental GT 650, launched in 2018, strikes a balance between form and function, offering a nostalgic cafe racer experience with modern upgrades.
Nagamese: Royal Enfield Continental GT 650, 2018 te launch hoise, etu form aru function majote balance bohut bhal kore, purana cafe racer experience dikhai kene modern upgrade bhi ase.

English: Rajiv Bajaj confirmed that the first Bajaj CNG motorcycle will be launched in June this year, in a recent media interaction.
Nagamese: Rajiv Bajaj pora confirm korise ki first Bajaj CNG motorcycle khan June mahina te launch hobo, etu kotha tai media logote baat koribole time te koise.

English: The automobile vehicle manufacturer is already in news for developing a range of CNG-powered motorcycles aimed at mileage-conscious customers.
Nagamese: Automobile vehicle bana wala khan etiya news te ase CNG-powered motorcycle series bana bole nimite, juntu mileage ke dhyan di kene banaise.

English: As far as the technical details of the CNG-powered bike are concerned, they are still under wraps.
Nagamese: CNG-powered bike ni technical details kotha koi bole hoile, etu etiya tak lukai kene rakhi dise.

English: It is expected that the prices of these bikes will be higher than their corresponding petrol versions.
Nagamese: Asha ase ki i bike khan dam petrol bike pora besi hobo.

English: As far as the technical details of the CNG-powered bike are concerned, they are still under wraps.
Nagamese: CNG-powered bike ni technical details kotha koi bole hoile, etu etiya tak lukai kene rakhi dise.

English: But the speculations suggest that the project is internally known as the Bruzer E101 which is rumored to use a 110cc engine.
Nagamese: Kintu khan manu khan kotha hisab se, i project khan bhitorete Bruzer E101 naam se jana jae aru koi ase ki etu 110cc engine use koribo.

English: When it comes to the manufacturing setup, this upcoming CNG-powered bike is likely to be manufactured at the brand’s Aurangabad and Pant Nagar plants.
Nagamese: Manufacture koribole kotha hoi bole hoile, i CNG-powered bike khan brand ni Aurangabad aru Pant Nagar plant te banaibo bole ase.

English: Besides this, the bike maker is planning to have a production plan which can build 2 lakh units per annum.
Nagamese: Etu alawa, bike bana wala khan production plan bhi ase junte pora 2 lakh unit ek saal te banaibo pare.

English: Earlier, the company had highlighted the salient features of the upcoming CNG motorcycle.
Nagamese: Age te, company pora CNG motorcycle ni important features bhi highlight korise.

English: This includes reduced tailpipe emissions, a 50-65 percent cost reduction and also 50 percent reduction in CO2 emissions reportedly.
Nagamese: Etu bhitorete reduced tailpipe emissions, 50-65 percent cost komti aru 50 percent CO2 emissions komti ase bole koi ase.

English: A front disc brake and commuter stance of the motorcycle hinting at a 110-125 cc offering is also revealed in recent spy shots.
Nagamese: Ekta front disc brake aru commuter stance bhi dikhaishe photo te, etu 110-125 cc offering hoi bole lagishe.

English: It is noteworthy here that the operating cost will be a huge factor with the CNG bike as it is targeting mileage-conscious consumers.
Nagamese: Etu dhyan dibole kotha ase ki operating cost ekta dangor kotha hobo CNG bike te, kile ki manu khan mileage mangta ase.

English: It will also be offered with a technology to run on both petrol and CNG.
Nagamese: Etu petrol aru CNG te bhi cholibo dibo.

English: Yamaha India has ended the past fiscal on a positive note with a YoY and MoM improvement in sales both in domestic and global markets.
Nagamese: Yamaha India pora pichela saal khan bhal se sesh korise, YoY aru MoM sales bhi bhal ase aro domestic aru global market te bhi.

English: World of Warcraft, Diablo and Overwatch fans who were looking forward to getting together at BlizzCon in November may be disappointed to learn that Blizzard has canceled this year's edition of the event.
Nagamese: World of Warcraft, Diablo aru Overwatch manu khan jun khan November mahina te BlizzCon te jum hoi bole asha kori thakise, etu janikena dukh lagibo ki etu saal event to Blizzard cancel koridise.

English: The Electronic Entertainment Expo, better known as E3, is officially dead.
Nagamese: Electronic Entertainment Expo, junke E3 naam se bhi jana jae, officially morigise.

English: If you are not 100% satisfied with your vehicle protection plan purchase, you can get your money back - no questions asked!
Nagamese: Jodi tumi apuni laga gari protection plan kinikena 100% satisfied nai huwa, tumi apuni laga paisa wapas pabole paribo- koi question nai puchega!

English: Bodo, is an ethnolinguistic group native to the state of Assam in India.
Nagamese: Bodo, ekta ethnolinguistic group ase jun Assam state, India te paida huwa manu khan ase.

English: They are a part of the greater Bodo-Kachari family of ethnolinguistic groups and are spread across northeastern India.
Nagamese: Etu khan Bodo-Kachari family of ethnolinguistic groups laga bhag ase aru northeastern India te fela huwa ase.

English: They are concentrated mainly in the Bodoland Territorial Region of Assam, though Boros inhabit all other districts of Assam and Meghalaya.
Nagamese: Etu khan Bodoland Territorial Region of Assam te beshi miktar te ase, hala ki Boros khan Assam aru Meghalaya laga dusra district te bhi thake.

English: Boros were listed under both "Boro" and "Borokachari" in The Constitution (Scheduled Tribes) Order, 1950 and are continued to be called so in the Census of India documents.
Nagamese: Boros khan ke "Boro" aru "Borokachari" naam se The Constitution (Scheduled Tribes) Order, 1950 te likha thakise aru etu khan ke Census of India documents te bhi etia bhi etu naam se bulai.

English: Boros speak the Boro language, a Boro-Garo language of the Tibeto-Burman family, which is recognised as one of twenty-two Scheduled languages of India.
Nagamese: Boros khan Boro bhasa bole, ekta Boro-Garo bhasa jun Tibeto-Burman family se ase, jun ke India laga biwi scheduled bhasa khan majot ekta hisab se pachana jae.

English: Over two-thirds of the people are bilingual, speaking Assamese as second language.
Nagamese: Dusra teen hissa manu khan bilingual ase, Assamese ke dusra bhasa hisab se bole.

English: The Boro along with other cognate groups of Bodo-Kachari peoples are prehistoric settlers who are believed to have migrated at least 3,000 years ago.
Nagamese: Boro khan Bodo-Kachari manu khan laga dusra jati khan logote prehistoric settlers ase jun khan ke kamti bhi 3,000 saal age te migrate koribole biswas ase.

English: Boros are mostly settled farmers, who have traditional irrigation.
Nagamese: Boros khan beshi hissa settled kheti kora khan ase, jun khan laga traditional irrigation ase.

English: Boro is the self-designation or autonym of the community.
Nagamese: Boro toh community laga nijor naam ase.

English: Traditionally, Boros practised Bathouism, which is the worshiping of supreme God, known as Obonglaoree.
Nagamese: Traditionally, Boros khan Bathouism practice kore, jun to supreme God, junke Obonglaoree naam se jana jae, taake puja korikena ase.

English: The shijou tree (in the genus Euphorbia) is taken as the symbol of Bathou and worshiped.
Nagamese: Shijou ghas (Euphorbia genus te) ke Bathou laga symbol hisab se loi aru puja kore.

English: It is also claimed as the supreme god.
Nagamese: Etu ke supreme bhagwan hisab se bhi dabi kore.

English: In the Boro language, Ba means five and thou means deep.
Nagamese: Boro bhasa te, Ba matlab panch aru thou matlab gahrai.

English: Since Boros believe in the five mighty elements of God – land, water, air, fire, and ether – the number five has become significant in the Bathou culture, which is similar to the five elements of other Asian religions.
Nagamese: Kelemane Boros khan bhagwan laga panch taqatwar chij - jameen, pani, hawa, aag, aru ether - te biswas kora, etu nimite panch number Bathou culture te important hoi jaise, jun dusra Asian dharm khan laga panch taqat logote mile.

English: According to Bathouism, before the creation of the universe there was simply a great void, in which the supreme being 'Aham Guru', Anan Binan Gosai or Obonglaoree existed formlessly.
Nagamese: Bathouism hisab se, universe bana se age te khali ekta dher dangor khali jagah thakise, jun te supreme being 'Aham Guru', Anan Binan Gosai aru Obonglaoree bina aakar te thakise.

English: Aham Guru became tired of living a formless existence and desired to live in flesh and blood.
Nagamese: Aham Guru bina aakar se jibon jibon thakise aru body te jibon jibon bole mon jaise.

English: He descended on this great void with all human characteristics and created the universe
Nagamese: Uu etu dher dangor khali jagah te olai ahise aru universe banaishe.

English: Television is one of the many wonders of modern science and technology.
Nagamese: Television toh modern science aru technology pora ekta dangor chamatkar hoi.

English: It was invented in England by the Scottish scientist J.N. Baird in 1928 and the British Broadcasting Corporation was the first to broadcast television images in 1929.
Nagamese: I para England te, Scottish scientist J.N. Baird 1928 te banaishe, aru British Broadcasting Corporation para 1929 te first television image dikhaishe.

English: Previously the radio helped us hear things from far and near and spread information and knowledge from one corner of the globe to another.
Nagamese: Age te radio para amikhan ke dur aru najor jaga pora kotha hunai aru information aru knowledge dunia eku kona pora aru eku kona te pathaishe.

English: But all this was done through sound only. But television combined visual images with sound.
Nagamese: Kintu etu sob kotha hobole pora hoishe. Hoilebi television para dikhibole image aru kotha ekta logote milaishe.

English: Today we can watch games, shows, and song and dance programs from all corners of the world while sitting in our own homes.
Nagamese: Ajikali amikhan game, show, aru nach gana program dunia sob kona pora nijor ghor te bikhithaki sabo pare.

English: TV can be used for educating the masses, for bringing to us the latest pieces of information audio-visually, and can provide us with all kinds of entertainment even in colour.
Nagamese: TV ke lokhikhan ke hikhai dibole nimite use koribo pare, amikhan logote notun information audio-visually anibo pare, aru amikhan ke sob prakar entertainment dibo pare, etu bhi colour te.

English: But as in all things, too much televiewing may prove harmful.
Nagamese: Kintu sob chiz te dhyan rakhile bhal, besi TV sabo dikdar dibo pare.

English: TV provides visual images but the televiewer has a limited choice of programs.
Nagamese: TV para dikhibole image di, kintu TV sabole manuh khan ke program select koribole nimite kamti option thake.

English: He has to adjust himself to the scheduled programs of a particular television channel. But as for the book, a reader’s imagination plays a vital role.
Nagamese: Tai khan ke toh juntu channel te programme schedule thake, titu hesab te nijorke adjust koribo lage.

English: He can freely read a book which is a personal activity and it cannot be shared with others at the same time.
Nagamese: Kintu kitaab toh, ekjon porha wala ke mon dikhai di,etu bhal hoi.

English: In many cases, the habit of watching TV has an adverse effect on the study habits of the young.
Nagamese: Tai ekta kitaab ke aaram se porhibo pare juntu tai nijor kaam hoi aru etu ekta time te dusra ke logote share nakoribo .

English: When we read books, we have to use our intelligence and imagination.
Nagamese: Besi time, TV subole adat pora young manuh khan ke porha subole dikdar hoi.

English: But in most cases, TV watching is a passive thing. It may dull our imagination and intelligence.
Nagamese: Jitia amikhan kitaab pore, amikhan ke nijor dimag aru mon ke use koribole lage.

English: The Newspaper is the mirror of the world.
Nagamese: Kintu besi time, TV subole toh eku nokora nisina hoi.

English: A newspaper is a regular source of important news from home and abroad.
Nagamese: Etu pora amikhan ke dimag aru mon dubele kori dibo pare.

English: Modern life cannot be imagined without newspapers.
Nagamese: Newspaper toh dunia ke aaina nisina.

English: It represents the current and living history of the world.
Nagamese: Ekta newspaper toh ghar aru desh bahar pora important khobor regularly di thake.

English: The main function of a daily paper is to publish news of general interest while the others mostly contain literary pieces and articles on important topics.
Nagamese: Ajikali life toh newspaper nathakile kalpana nokoribole para.

English: So, the newspaper is one of the most powerful organs for the dissemination of news and views among the public.
Nagamese: Etu dunia ke current aru jinda history represent kore.

English: It plays a very important role in educating people and guiding them along the right path.
Nagamese: Daily paper ke main kaam toh general interest wala khobor chapai dibole hoi aru baki khan toh besi literary aru important topic uporte likha article thake.

English: If it wants to it can fight social evil successfully. A newspaper can also do us much harm.
Nagamese: Titia, newspaper toh khobor aru bichar lokhikhan majote pathai dibole nimite sobse dangor jinish hoi.

English: Used wrongly it can create hatred and enmity between man and man, section and section, nation and nation.
Nagamese: Etu lokhikhan ke hikhai dibole te aru thik rasta te chalaibole te dhyan di.

English: Sometimes it publishes baseless reports or stories to create deliberately confusion in the minds of gullible people.
Nagamese: Kileman beleti khan emanei baseless khobor bonai dibo ya confuse kori diboley bhal bishaar nuthaka manu khan ke.

English: A newspaper is as powerful as any potent weapon.
Nagamese: Ekta newspaper to powerful weapon nishena ase.

English: It can be used for both good and evil. Much depends on the outlook and motive of the people who are at the helm of the paper.
Nagamese: Etuk bhal aru bekhar kaam karne bhi use koribo pare. Etu to depend kori thake newspaper choloa manu khan ki bhabona aru ki niyot ase.

English: It shapes Public opinion. It can mislead people with false and fabricated news.
Nagamese: Etu public bhabona to bonaye. Etu manu ke burha aru jukha khobor dikhai ke mislead bhi koribo pare.

English: The newspaper should give impartial and correct pieces of information. It must not feed false news.
Nagamese: Newspaper khan impartial aru thik thik khobor diboley lage. Etu burha khobor nidibo lage.

English: Like Mahatma Gandhi, African leader Nelson Mandela battled against the oppression of the South African people by whites.
Nagamese: Mahatma Gandhi nishena, African leader Nelson Mandela bhi South Africa manu khan ke gora manu khan pora dabai dikene thaka ke against te fight korise.

English: He received a legal lawyer education.
Nagamese: Tai legal lawyer education bhi loishe.

English: Instead of living comfortably and in luxury as a successful lawyer, he did the opposite.
Nagamese: Aaram aru shanti te jibon to katibolaga jagah te, tai ekjon successful lawyer hoikena bhi, tai etu ulta korise.

English: Mandela was sentenced to life in jail by the reactionary South African government in 1962.
Nagamese: Mandela ke life imprisonment (jail) dise South African government 1962 te.

English: Mandela was finally released from jail on February 11, 1990, after 27 years and 9 months of imprisonment, as a result of other African National Congress leaders’ ongoing fight for freedom.
Nagamese: Mandela ke jail pora February 11, 1990 te ulai dise, 27 saal aru 9 mahina ke baad te, African National Congress leader khan fight korikena freedom ke anibolagey.

English: Brilliant scientists have come and gone, but perhaps none have accomplished anything under the circumstances that Stephen Hawking has faced.
Nagamese: Bohut dangor dangor scientist khan ahise aru gusi jaise, kintu Stephen Hawking jiman korsi, etu situation te koi bhi koribole para nai.

English: Doctors only gave Hawking two more years to live.
Nagamese: Doctor khan toh Hawking ke matro dui saal jibon ase koisile.

English: Despite this bleak prognosis, Hawking not only completed his course at Oxford, but also went on to complete his PhD thesis, marry, and have three children.
Nagamese: Etu dukh dikhai diya bhabhisyot thaka piche bhi, Hawking na keval Oxford te aponar course pura korise, kintu aponar PhD thesis bhi pura korise, biya korise, aru tinita bacha bhi ase.

English: He was elected a Fellow of the Royal Society in 1974, a rare honour for any scientist.
Nagamese: Tai 1974 te Royal Society te Fellow hisab te elect hoisile, etu kunu scientist nimite bohut samman laga kura ase.

English: Hawking contracted pneumonia again during a research trip to Switzerland, and his windpipe was operated on.
Nagamese: Hawking ke Switzerland te research koribole jua time te pneumonia wapas hoisile, aru taikhan taar hawa jua noli ke operate korisile.

English: This resulted in a total loss of speech.
Nagamese: Etu nimite tai kotha kobo napara hoi jaise.

English: ‘A Brief History of Time’ and ‘The Universe in a Nutshell’ are two books that popularised his epoch-making theories about the Creation and the Universe.
Nagamese: 'A Brief History of Time' aru 'The Universe in a Nutshell' etu duita kitab ase, juntu taar duniya hilaidiya theory khan, dunia kenekoi bonise aru Universe ke popular banaishe.

English: Academic honours have been bestowed upon Hawking, who is widely regarded as the greatest physicist and mathematician since Einstein.
Nagamese: Hawking ke academic award bhi deya hoise, tai Einstein pichet te bohut dangor physicist aru mathematician hisab te jana jai.

English: Stephen Hawking has demonstrated how the human intellect and spirit can overcome the most severe physical handicaps.
Nagamese: Stephen Hawking etu dikhaise manuhe kiman takat ase nijor dimag aru atma dwara, physical problem thakilebi.

English: Machines are at the heart of modern civilization.
Nagamese: Machine khan modorn civilization or important chij ase.

English: Machines require a constant supply of energy to function day in and day out, or they will come to a halt.
Nagamese: Machine chalabolagele energy toh hamesha lagiboi  din rati nathakile machine bahdha hoi jabo.

English: Our transportation systems—diesel-powered railways, steamships, automobiles, and aeroplanes—constantly require natural fuel to function.
Nagamese: Ami khan or transportation system jene diesel railway, steamship, gari aru aeroplane khan chalabolagele hamesha fuel toh লাগিবই.

English: Not only that but as the world’s population grows, so will the demand for energy.
Nagamese: Etu nahoi ekela, dunia te manu bachibo besi hole energy toh besi lagiboi.

English: That is why new and unconventional energy sources such as nuclear and solar energy must be developed.
Nagamese: Etukarne notun aru alag energy source jene nuclear aru solar energy bonabolagei.

English: But the point is that research into the development of new energy sources must be expanded and accelerated.
Nagamese: Kotha toh hol notun energy source usorote research toh bohuot besi kori bole lagibo.

English: Tobacco was discovered in America for the first time.
Nagamese: Tobacco America te poila bar pahise.

English: Its widespread cultivation with the assistance of negro-slaves made it one of the most important export items in the American economy.
Nagamese: Negro gulam khan logote sakh barikena etu America or economy te dangi export item hoise.

English: Tobacco was also grown in other parts of the world, including Burma and Cuba.
Nagamese: Tobacco dunia or aru jagah tebi jene Burma aru Cuba te.

English: Cigarettes made of tobacco rolled in tissue paper aided in the spread of a very harmful habit among the West’s so-called advanced countries.
Nagamese: Cigarette jun toh tissue paper te tobacco bhorti kori banaise etu harmful habit Запад or sobse dangi desh khan majote pheilise.

English: It then spread to various other countries around the world.
Nagamese: Pichete etu dunia or aru desh khan pheilise.

English: Attractive packaging and expert advertising aided in spreading the addiction to smoking cigarettes even among the poorest segments of the population.
Nagamese: Loba packaging aru expert advertising dwara cigarrate khabo addiction toh garib manu khan majotebi pheilise.

English: However, it has recently been discovered that smoking causes a variety of serious physical ailments, that include from coughing, sore throat, laryngitis, and asthma to the fatal disease cancer.
Nagamese: Kintu ajikali etu ulai dise ki cigarette khale bohu dharanor bemar hoi, jene khahi, throat dukhibo, laryngitis, aru asthma pora suru kori fatal bemar cancer tak.

English: Furthermore, even people who are unintentionally exposed to cigarette smoke while in the company of a smoker can develop any of these diseases.
Nagamese: Aru besi kotha hol jun manu cigarette nkhaibi, jun khan cigarette khane manu logote thake taikhan kebi etu bemar hobo pare.

English: This demonstrates how hazardous the habit of smoking cigarettes is.
Nagamese: Etu dikhai ki cigarette khabo habit kiman beya ase.

English: It has been discovered that quitting smoking can cure a variety of diseases.
Nagamese: Etu ulai dise ki cigarette khabo bandha korile bohu dharanor bemar thik hobo pare.

English: As a result, we should all avoid the bad habit of smoking cigarettes.
Nagamese: Etukarne ami sob cigarette khabo habit pora dur thakibo lage.

English: The computer is one of modern science’s marvels.
Nagamese: Computer modorn science or ekta ajab chij ase.

English: Its origins can be traced back to the mechanical calculating machine invented in 1834 by Charles Babbage, an English mathematician.
Nagamese: Etu jun banaise Charles Babbage naam or manu toh 1834 te teo english mathematician ase.

English: Because of the lack of engineering skill at the time, the machine was unable to fully realise Babbage’s vision.
Nagamese: Tetiya engineering kaam karne wala manuh khan laga skill nathakeneh, etu machine toh Babbage laga sapna pura hobo nimite pura puri nohoi.

English: Computers today are electronic.
Nagamese: Ajikali computer khan sob electronic hoi.

English: A computer can operate at high speeds because it is powered by a small current of electricity.
Nagamese: Computer toh high speed te kaam kori parey kelemane etu current electricity laga power pora choley.

English: It can perform calculations on very large numbers in a very short period of time using stored information and data.
Nagamese: Etu toh time nadhorikina data aaru information use kori keneh lamba lamba number khan bhi calculate kori dibo parey.

English: A computer can add, subtract, multiply, and divide large numbers in seconds.
Nagamese: Computer ekta lamba number khan bhi seconds te plus, minus, multiply aaru divide kori dibo parey.

English: Even an expert mathematician would take much longer to complete such calculations.
Nagamese: Ekta expert math te bisi homoy lagibo etu hisab kitab khan milaikena ulale.

English: Computers are now widely used in all advanced countries for data processing, which includes fact compilation, correlation, and selection.
Nagamese: Ajikali computer toh sob advanced desh te data processing nimite kaam te dhori ase, juntu te fact khan miluwa, selection koruwa bhi hoi.

English: The use of computers is increasing in all fields of research, industry, and business.
Nagamese: Computer laga use toh din din besi hoi ase research, industry aaru business khan te.

English: Computers have numerous advantages.
Nagamese: Computer laga faida toh dher ase.

English: They save time, speed up work, save labour, and reduce costs in the long run.
Nagamese: Taikhan homoy bachai dey, kaam joldi korey, manuh laga kamti korey aaru paisa bhi bachai dey bisi din nimite.

English: They are used in educational institutions, industries, research laboratories, government departments, and so on in all advanced countries.
Nagamese: Taikhan toh school, industry, research laboratory, government department aaru etu sob advanced desh khan te use korey.

English: The term “environment” refers to a healthy natural balance of air, water, animals, plants, and other natural resources.
Nagamese: Environment koile toh hawa, pani, janwar, gas aaru dusra natural chiz khan thik thakibo lage.

English: However, today’s environment is being poisoned in a variety of ways all over the world.
Nagamese: Kintu, ajikali environment toh alag alag pora kharab hoi ase duniate. Environment kharab huwa laga karon toh dunia te manuh bishi huwa pora hoi ase.

English: The main cause of environmental pollution is the world’s growing population. More people equals more industry.
Nagamese: Manuh bishi koile industry bhi bishi thakibo.

English: For also released into river waters; trees are cut down for fuel and other commercial purposes, or to acquire land for house construction.
Nagamese: Eku factories khan pora dukhua pani nadi te berai dey; gas khan toh jolai dey fuel nimite aaru business nimite, naile ghar bonuwa nimite.

English: As a result, the supply of oxygen provided by trees decreases.
Nagamese: Titia pora gas khan pora oxygen ulaikena diya toh kamti hoi jai.

English: Animals and birds lose their habitat when trees are cut down, destroying the ecological balance.
Nagamese: Janwar aaru sidiya khan jaga nathakibo gas khan katikena, environment bi bigri jai.

English: Humans are attacked with a variety of diseases such as asthma, hypertension, various types of heart disease, bronchitis, pleurisy, and so on.
Nagamese: Manuh khan toh alag alag bimari pora attack korey jese asthma, hypertension, heart laga bimari, bronchitis, pleurisy aaru etu sob.

English: The hooting of cars and buses causes sound or noise pollution, particularly in cities, and listening to music over loudspeakers exposes people to another type of environmental pollution.
Nagamese: Gari aaru bus laga horn bajale pora awaj hobo, noise pollution bohut besi hoi, city khan te sune, aaru music loudspeaker te bajale bhi manu khan environment pollution te fase.

English: To keep these hazards from endangering human, animal, and plant life, action should be taken before the situation worsens.
Nagamese: Eitu khatra khan pora bachibo nimite manuh, janwar aaru gas khan ke, action lobi lagey situation toh bisi kharab huwa agey te.

English: There should be more trees planted. Scientific anti-pollution methods should be developed.
Nagamese: Gass bisi bon kuribo lage. Science usor pora anti-pollution metods bhi banabo lage.

English: Science has done wonders for modern civilization. It has made human life simpler and more comfortable.
Nagamese: Science para modern zamana nimite dangor dangor kaam kori dise. Science para manu life to ekdom easy aru aaram banaidi dise.

English: The man had to rely solely on the mercy of nature in ancient times, and in society, manpower was the only source for doing any laborious work.
Nagamese: Purana zamana te manu khan nature usor te depend kori thakibo lagise, aru society te manu bal usor te depend kori thakibo lagise kaam koribo nimite.

English: Man invented new appliances and applied new techniques to do things in all walks of life as time passed.
Nagamese: Manu khan nawa nawa chiz aru techniques invent kurishe time jowa hisab te.

English: Man has now entered the nuclear age. He is now capable of deciphering many of space’s mysteries.
Nagamese: Manu khan etiya nuclear age te aahise. Etiya manu space ke bhi bujhi bole nimite capable ase.

English: Science has advanced human society to unimaginable heights in some Western countries.
Nagamese: Science para manu society ke bisi upor tak anidiya western desho te.

English: Even a small needle cannot be manufactured today without the use of science.
Nagamese: Eku chutu sui bhi science nalagaikena bonabo napare.

English: It saves both time and labour. Railways, trams, buses, ships, and aeroplanes have made travel more convenient and faster.
Nagamese: Science para time aru labour bachai dise. Railway, trams, buses, ships, aru aeroplanes para travel kora bisi easy aru joldi hoi dise.

English: Again, radios, wireless, telephones, fax, television, and computers aid in the rapid spread of knowledge.
Nagamese: Aru radios, wireless, telephones, fax, television, aru computers para knowledge joldi phulai dise.

English: Science has performed miracles in the field of medicine, though its work is not yet finished.
Nagamese: Science para medicine field te bhi miracle kurishe, hala ki etiya tak kaam finish huoa nai.

English: However, scientific achievements are not without their drawbacks.
Nagamese: Kintu, scientific achievements usor te drawbacks bhi ase.

English: The value of blood as a life-saving component cannot be overstated.
Nagamese: Blood ke life bachabo nimite bisi important ase koi kena bisi koi bole dorkar nai.

English: People with diseases like leukaemia and chronic anaemia need regular blood transfusions to survive.
Nagamese: Juntu manu khan ke leukaemia aru chronic anaemia ase, taikhan ke blood transfusion regular koribo lage bachibo nimite.

English: Also, those who have lost a lot of blood due to bleeding from wounds and injuries from accidents and other acts of violence need blood to replenish the lost blood.
Nagamese: Aru juntu manu khan ke accident usor pora blood bisi bohi jaise taikhan nimite bhi blood dorkar ase.

English: these urgent needs require a stock of blood in the blood banks of government hospitals from where people who need blood can get it immediately at a critical moment.
Nagamese: Etu urgent dorkar ke pura koribo nimite hospitals te blood bank banaibo lage aru kun manu ke blood dorkar ase taikhan ke blood dibo lage.

English: Doctors assure us that the amount of donated blood is replenished soon and the donation does not have any harmful effect on the donor’s physical condition.
Nagamese: Doctors para koise ki donated blood joldi pura hoi jai aru donation kora manu ke eku taklif nuhui.

English: One only has to remember that such a blood donation which has no harmful effect on the blood donor himself can save a precious life while hanging precariously in need of the precious fluid.
Nagamese: Kun manu khan blood donate kore taikhan ke eku taklif nuhui aru bisi kimti life bachai dibo pare.

English: Blood donation camps are organised by social organisations, political parties, and other charitable organisations.
Nagamese: Blood donation camps social organisations, political parties, aru aru bhi charitable organisations para organise kore.

English: If such organisations are genuinely interested in meeting a valuable social need, every right-thinking citizen should come forward and donate his blood, remembering that his blood could save a life.
Nagamese: Jodi etu organisations khan sotti pora social dorkar pura koribo nimite interested ase, sob manu khan ke age awibo lage aru blood donate koribo lage, etu yaad rakhibo lage ki blood para kunba ke bachai dibo pare.

English: it is impossible to imagine modern life without newspapers.
Nagamese: Newspaper nalagaikena modern life imagine koribo bhi napare.

English: A newspaper is a reliable source of important news from both the United States and around the world.
Nagamese: Ekta newspaper khan United States aro dunia ke news dibole ekta bhal source ase.

English: It represents the world’s current and living history. Newspapers are classified into daily, weekly, biweekly, and monthly publications.
Nagamese: Imi dunia current history represent kore.

English: A daily paper’s main function is to publish news of general interest, whereas the others mostly contain literary pieces and articles on important topics.
Nagamese: Newspaper khan daily, weekly, biweekly, aro monthly publications hisab te divide kora ase.

English: Every newspaper nowadays has some special sections dealing with politics, everyday problems, unusual news, business, sports, editorial pages, feature pages, and so on.
Nagamese: Ekta daily paper or main kaam to general interest news publish kora, hoilebi dusra khan mostly literary pieces aro important topics or articles ase.

English: As a result, the newspaper is one of the most powerful organs for spreading news and opinions to the general public.
Nagamese: Ajikali sob newspaper te politics, everyday problems, unusual news, business, sports, editorial pages, aro feature pages nisena special sections thake.

English: Many will support guilty pleas for some heinous crime, such as premeditated murder.
Nagamese: Eitu karone, newspaper to news aro opinions general public ke dibole ekta dangor source ase.

English: In the past, such a punishment was a death sentence on the basis that a person who destroys another person’s life or destroys a woman’s life by rape is unfit to live and deserves the death penalty.
Nagamese: Koikhan manuhe guilty pleas support koribo kunba dangerous crime nimite, nisena premeditated murder.

English: Furthermore, capital punishment serves as a deterrent to others who, in the absence of such severe punishment, may commit heinous and inhuman crimes.
Nagamese: Aru besi, capital punishment dusra ke ekta sikhona dibo juntu manu eku bhi punishment nathakile bhi dangerous aru inhuman crimes koribo pare.

English: However, some people believe that the state should not follow a “tooth for a tooth and an eye for an eye” policy represented by capital punishment.
Nagamese: Hoilebi, koikhan manishe bhabe state ekta “tooth for a tooth and an eye for an eye” policy follow nakoribo lage capital punishment te.

English: A criminal is a destroyer of human lives, no matter how ignominious those lives may be as a result of their cruel actions.
Nagamese: Ekta criminal manu or life destroyer ase, kiman humiliating life taikhan or cruel actions pora dekhai.

English: Furthermore, the death penalty serves as a deterrent to others who might otherwise commit heinous and inhumane crimes without fear of such severe punishment.
Nagamese: Aru besi, death penalty dusra ke ekta sikhona dibo juntu manu eku bhi punishment nathakile bhi dangerous aru inhumane crimes koribo pare.

English: However, some argue that the state’s “tooth for a tooth and eye for an eye” policy should not include the death penalty.
Nagamese: Hoilebi, koikhan manishe bhabe state or “tooth for a tooth and eye for an eye” policy te death penalty nathakibo lage.

English: A criminal, a destroyer of human life, regardless of how humiliating that life is demonstrated by their cruel actions.
Nagamese: Ekta criminal manu or life destroyer ase, kiman humiliating life taikhan or cruel actions pora dekhai.

English: Life imprisonment, penal servitude and, when necessary, psychiatric treatment of persons guilty of violent and inhumane crimes are recommended as alternatives to capital punishment.
Nagamese: Life imprisonment, penal servitude aru, jetiya dorkar, psychiatric treatment kunta manuhe violent aru inhumane crimes kore taikhan nimite, etu capital punishment or jagah te use koribo pare.

English: The 15th of August is celebrated as India’s Independence Day.
Nagamese: August 15 tarik to India's Independence Day hisab te celebrate kore.

English: Since 1947, India has been commemorating this day annually to honor its freedom from foreign rule.
Nagamese: 1947 pora, India eitu din har sal te celebrate kore taikhan or freedom foreign rule pora honor koribole.

English: On this momentous day, the British relinquished control over India and handed over the governance to the national government of independent India.
Nagamese: Eitu important din te, British khan India opor te control chadi dise aru governance to national government of independent India ke dise.

English: It signifies the triumphs of Indian patriots and nationalists who fought valiantly and made sacrifices for the liberation of their homeland.
Nagamese: Eitu Indian patriots aru nationalists or jeet dekhai kun manu dangor himmat pora ladi aru sacrifice korise taikhan or homeland ke mukti dibole.

English: Every year, the people of our country celebrate this day with immense energy and enthusiasm, recognizing its significance in India’s history.
Nagamese: Har saal, amikhan or desh or manuhe eitu din dangor energy aru enthusiasm pora celebrate kore, India's history te etu or importance mani bole.

English: The main event takes place at the Red Fort in the capital city of Delhi.
Nagamese: Main event to Delhi or capital city te Red Fort te hoi.

English: The Prime Minister of India raises the national flag at the Red Fort on this auspicious day.
Nagamese: India Prime Minister etu shubh dinot Red Fort te national flag utola.

English: The inaugural celebration of Independence Day was led by our first and most memorable Prime Minister, Pandit Jawaharlal Nehru, on August 15, 1947.
Nagamese: Independence Day shuru toh amikhan laga first Prime Minister, Pandit Jawaharlal Nehru, August 15, 1947 te korise.

English: He unfurled the tricolor National Flag at the Red Fort in the national capital, New Delhi for the first time.
Nagamese: Tai Red Fort, New Delhi te tricolor National Flag first time utaise.

English: Independence Day celebrations hold great importance.
Nagamese: ndependence Day toh bohut dangi kotha ase.

English: Government offices proudly hoist flags atop their buildings, and illuminated decorations create a captivating sight with electric bulbs.
Nagamese: Sarkar office khan te flag uthai, aru electric bulb khan jolai kene decorations bohut sundor dikhai.

English: Just like every year, this year too, Independence Day was celebrated with exceptional energy and enthusiasm in my town.
Nagamese: Jetiya har saal hoi, etu saal bhi Independence Day amikhan laga town te bhi dhamaka laga kori bole celebrate korise.

English: The program took place at the University Hall, with the Block Development Officer as the Chief Guest.
Nagamese: Program toh University Hall te hoise, Block Development Officer chief guest thakise.

English: The national flag was hoisted at 8 a.m. at the University Hall, performed by the Block Development Officer.
Nagamese: National flag toh sokal 8 baje te University Hall te uthaise, Block Development Officer dwara.

English: All the respected dignitaries and attendees saluted the flag while singing the National Anthem.
Nagamese: Sob dangi manu aru juntu ahi thakise sob flag ke salute marise National Anthem gaaute gaaute.

English: Following the flag hoisting, a cultural program commenced with the participation of students from various universities and schools.
Nagamese: Flag utoar pichete, cultural program start hoise alag alag University aru school pora students khan ahikene.

English: Patriotic poems and songs were recited by the students.
Nagamese: Patriotic poem aru gaan students khan porhise.

English: School students marched in a parade through the town’s central avenues, accompanied by a band playing the National Anthem.
Nagamese: School students khan parade korikene town laga main rasta pora jaise, band party bhi National Anthem bajaishe.

English: People also enjoyed flying kites, symbolizing the spirit of independence.
Nagamese: Manu khan kite urai bhi maja marise, etu independence laga nishan ase.

English: Similar celebrations are held in each city and town, schools, colleges, and so on.
Nagamese: Eitu nisena celebrations har ekta city aru town te hoi, school, college aru baki jaga te bhi.

English: Flying kites, symbolizing independence, brings joy to the people.
Nagamese: Kite urai kene independence dikhai manu khan ke bohut khushi lage.

English: Overall, there is a remarkable sense of enthusiasm among the populace.
Nagamese: Sobi mili kene, manu khan bhari josh dikhaise.

English: As dusk sets in, people engage in kite flying, setting the kites free to symbolize India’s freedom.
Nagamese: Jetiya rati hoi, manu khan kite urai, India laga azadi dikhai bole.

English: Independence Day serves as a reminder of the patriots who fought and endured hardships to secure our freedom.
Nagamese: Independence Day amikhan ke yaad dilai kun manu khan desh ke bachai bole bohut dukh paise.

English: It holds extraordinary significance in the lives of all Indian citizens.
Nagamese: Eitu toh India manu khan zindagi te bohut dangi kotha ase.

English: On this day, people also take a solemn vow to safeguard the nation’s freedom.
Nagamese: Eitu din te, manu khan sapath bhi loi desh laga azadi bachai bole.

English: Undoubtedly, Independence Day is one of the most pivotal days in the history of free India.
Nagamese: Koi xondeh nai, Independence Day toh amikhan laga azad India itihas te ekdom dorkari din ase.

English: It is celebrated with great fervor in every town, and city, and even by Indians residing in foreign countries.
Nagamese: Iko toh sob town, city, aru bidesh te thaka Indian khan bhi dhun pora celebrate kore.

English: NASA has discovered the eighth planet of a star system, similar to our solar system.
Nagamese: NASA para ekta nawa planet thuk kheji uliyaise, ekta star system te, etu amikhan laga solar system nisina ase.

English: The star, Kepler-90  is over 2,500 light years away and larger and hotter than our sun.
Nagamese: Etu star, Kepler-90, 2,500 light years dhoor te ase aru etu amikhan laga suraj pora dangor aru garam bhi ase.

English: It is the first star known to have as many planets as our solar system.
Nagamese: Etu toh ek nambar star ase juntu laga etna planet ase amikhan laga solar system nisina.

English: The Kepler-90 star system is much more compact than our solar system.
Nagamese: Kepler-90 star system toh amikhan laga solar system pora bhi beshi compact ase.

English: The outermost planet orbits the star at about the same distance as the earth orbits the sun.
Nagamese: Sobse bahar planet toh star ke etna dhoor pora ghume jetna dhoor pora earth para suraj ke ghume.

English: its inner planets are small and rocky while the outer ones are larger and made up of gas.
Nagamese: Etu laga bhitri planet khan toh chutu aru pathuria ase jabole bahar planet khan dangar ase aru gas pora banaise.

English: The newly discovered planet, Kepler -90i, is the third celestial object in the star system.
Nagamese: Etu naya khoja planet, Kepler-90i, star system te teen nambar celestial chiz ase.

English: It is probably rocky and similar to our earth, but because of its closeness to its home star has an average surface temperature of about 400° C.
Nagamese: Etu toh hoile bi pathuria aru amikhan laga earth nisina hobo, kintu etu ghore laga star laga olop dhakai te thakise nimite etu laga surface laga temperature 400° C ase.

English: It moves around the star once every 14.4 days.
Nagamese: Etu para star ke pura ghumibo nimite 14.4 din lage.

English: The discovery was made based on data provided by the Kepler space telescope.
Nagamese: Etu khojte Kepler space telescope diya dorkar khan laga data uporte vitti kori kena banaisile.

English: It appears to be well-preserved and in one piece.
Nagamese: I toh bhal lagise, ekdom thik ase.

English: Experts are now trying to examine the wreck and find out why the sub sank. They think it was probably an accident and not because of enemy fire.
Nagamese: Etiya expert khan wreck toh sai ase aru submarine kenekoi dubise huli ase. Tai khan bhabe eto accident hoise, dushman or fire nahoi.

English: The sub was on a mission to occupy German New Guinea, one of the German colonies in the Pacific.
Nagamese: Submarine toh German New Guinea, juntu German colony ase Pacific te, tai jagah lo bolai mission te asile.

English: In the past, there were 13 government - funded searches for the sub but, until now,  none of them proved successful.
Nagamese: agote, 13 bar government paisa di kene submarine hulisile, kintu kunutai saphul nuhole.

English: When the submarine was found navy officials held a commemorative service for the crew that the Australian navy had lost.
Nagamese: Jitia submarine paishe, navy officer khan ekta yadgar service korishe Australian navy or homai diya manu khan nimite.

English: Families were contacted and told that the oldest naval mystery in Australian history had finally been solved.
Nagamese: Family khan ke phone kori kene koise ki Australian history te purana naval mystery toh saphul hoigol.

English: The World Health Organisation has added the term "gaming disorder" to its
Nagamese: World Health Organisation "gaming disorder" koile eku word International Classification of Diseases te palai diase.

English: International Classification of Diseases. It refers to people who are addicted to video and other games and cannot stop.
Nagamese: Eto manu khan nimite ase juntu video aru dusra game khelikene nalagebole

English: It is the first update in the WHO's catalogue in almost three decades.
Nagamese: Etu WHO toh puraana catalogue te almost tin desak pichedei first update ase.

English: According to the WHO, gaming becomes a disorder if you are unable to control how long you play and when to stop.
Nagamese: WHO hisaab te, gaming ekta disorder hoi jaboh jodi tumi control nakoribo pareh kitia khelibo aru kitia bandh koribo lageh.

English: In order for a person to be regarded as having a gaming disorder, the behaviour must be going on for at least one year, either constantly or in phases.
Nagamese: Ekjon manu gaming disorder ase bolei mani bole nimite, etu behaviour ek saal toh jaroor dhori thakibo lageh, hamesha hoilebi ya phases te.

English: Gamers put their addiction above their family life, meeting with friends and going to school.
Nagamese: Gamer khan etu addiction ke family life, bandhu logote milibo aru school jabo upor te besi importance deh.

English: On one side studies have shown that playing video games may help with problems like depression and dementia.
Nagamese: Ekta side te studies dekhai diseh ki video game khelile depression aru dementia nisina problems te help koreh.

English: However, gaming is highly addictive and many people play for a longer time than is healthy.
Nagamese: Kilebi, gaming toh bohut nesha banaidiya ase aru dher manu healthy thaka time pora besi time khele.

English: As a result, people get fired for not going to work or miss school classes for a longer period of time.
Nagamese: Eitu nisina pora, manu khan kaam te najawa nimite ya school class miss kora nimite nokri pora ulai deh.

English: Many continue with their addiction, even if they see and realize the negative consequences it leads to.
Nagamese: Dher manu etu addiction logote continue koreh, etu pora negative result ulalebi.

English: Tetris, one of the world's most popular games is thirty years old.
Nagamese: Tetris, dunia te bohut popular games khan majot te ekta ase aru etu tihs saal purana ase.

English: A Soviet scientist, Alexey Pajitnov, invented it in 1984.
Nagamese: Ekjon Soviet scientist, Alexey Pajitnov, 1984 te etu invent korisile.

English: A few years later, it made its way to the west where it was played on the legendary Commodore 64, the first Apple computers and the IBM-PC.
Nagamese: Kichu saal pichede, etu west te ahisile juntu te legendary Commodore 64, first Apple computers aru IBM-PC te khelisile.

English: Within a short time, it became popular around the world.
Nagamese: Thora time bhitorede, etu dunia bhari te popular hoisile.

English: In 1989 it was released to the Gameboy console.
Nagamese: 1989 te etu Gameboy console te release kurisile.

English: Tetris is the best-selling video game of all times, with 150 million copies sold since its first release.
Nagamese: Tetris sobse besi bika video game ase, etu first release huwa pora 150 million copies bikise.

English: Tetris is very easy to play.
Nagamese: Tetris khelibo nimite bohut asaan ase.

English: The aim is to stack differently coloured and shaped blocks that fall down into a pit.
Nagamese: Etu aim toh alag alag rong aru shape thaka blocks khan ekta pit te giraibo aru stack koribo lageh.

English: You can rotate them in every direction and you get points when you have created a complete row of blocks, which then disappears.
Nagamese: Tumi etu khan ke kunu direction te ghuraibo pareh aru tumi point pabo jitia tumi block khan pora ekta pura row banaibo, juntu toh disappear hoijabo.

English: The goal of the game is to make horizontal lines full of blocks without any gaps between them.
Nagamese: Etu game toh goal horizontal lines ke block khan pora pura koribo lageh kunu gap nathakibo lageh.

English: Pajitnov created the game as a welcome distraction from everyday work, but Soviet authorities quickly banned the Tetris.
Nagamese: Pajitnov etu game ke banisile kaam pora mind divert koribo nimite, kilebi Soviet authorities Tetris ke ban korisile.

English: Unlike many other software developers, the Tetris did not make the Soviet scientist rich.
Nagamese: Dusra software developers khan nisina nohoi, Tetris Soviet scientist ke dhoni banaidiya nai.

English: Private business was banned during the Communist era and Pajitnov had to give up the rights to the government.
Nagamese: Communist time-te private business koribo nimite bandh kori dise etu nimite Pajitnov government ke rights dibo lagise.

English: Tetris is still popular today, mostly because it takes your mind off other things.
Nagamese: Tetris titia bhi popular ase, kelemane etu tumar mind ke dusra kura khan thaka dur kori diye.

English: You do not have to wait to see results like in other games.
Nagamese: Tumi result dikhibo nimite wait koribo nalage dusra games khan nisina.

English: Tetris becomes addictive from the minute you start playing it.
Nagamese: Tetris addictive hoi diye jitia tumi shuru kore.

English: Even though its educational value is near to zero people still keep coming back to play.
Nagamese: Etu educational value ekdum kumti homan bhi manu khan ahibo continue kore khelibo nimite.

English: Game developer Ubisoft have announced that it is releasing an ultimate version of Tetris in honour of the game's 30th birthday.
Nagamese: Game developer Ubisoft ene announce korise ultimate version Tetris laga release koribo nimite game laga 30th birthday te.

English: Pokémon Go is a new reality game that has created a hype around the world.
Nagamese: Pokémon Go eku notun reality game ase etu hype banaise dunia olopare.

English: It is unique because it combines the real world with a virtual one.
Nagamese: Etu unique ase kelemane etu real dunia ke virtual dunia ekta te milai diye.

English: Pokémon Go uses a mobile phone’s GPS device to capture and fight against mysterious creatures wherever a person goes.
Nagamese: Pokémon Go mobile phone laga GPS use kore capture koribo nimite aaru fight koribo nimite mysterious creatures manu jitia jabo titia.

English: In the first few weeks after the game’s release it was downloaded by over a hundred million people.
Nagamese: Shuru te few weeks pichete game release kora pichete etu hundred million manu khan download korise.

English: A user starts Pokémon Go by downloading the app on their mobile phone and choosing an avatar who accompanies them throughout the game.
Nagamese: User khan Pokémon Go shuru kore download kori kene app mobile phone te aaru avatar ekta choose kore etu tanga te thakibo etu game khelibo homan.

English: The player’s location is determined by the phone’s  GPS signal.
Nagamese: Player laga location GPS signal dwara thik kore.

English: A two-dimensional map turns into a virtual one.
Nagamese: Dui dimensional map virtual ekta hoi jaye.

English: As you move around so does the real-world map .
Nagamese: Jitia tumi here othere jabo titia real-world map bhi jabo

English: When you see a Pokémon you can throw a so-called Pokéball to capture it.
Nagamese: Jitia tumi Pokémon dikhe tumi Pokéball mari capture koribo pare.

English: Depending on where in the world you are you get to see different types of Pokémons and other objects.
Nagamese: Depend kore tumi dunia te koitah ase tumi alag alag Pokémons aaru dusra chiz khan dikhibo.

English: By going to different locations you can increase your playing level.
Nagamese: Alag alag location te jaikene tumi playing level barabo pare.

English: The  game is free to play but you must buy PokéCoins to  unlock special items .
Nagamese: Etu khelibo nimite free ase kintu tumi PokéCoins kinibo lage special items unlock koribo nimite

English: Pokémon Go may turn out to be one of the most successful mobile games of all times.
Nagamese: Pokémon Go ekta dangor mobile game hobo pare sob time te.

English: Shortly after Nintendo, the game’s owner, released the app, the company’s value on stock markets skyrocketed.
Nagamese: Nintendo, game laga owner, app release kora pichete company laga value stock markets te ekdom upor te jabo.

English: Nintendo also created the original Pokémon in the middle of the 1990s.
Nagamese: Nintendo company toh 1990s laga bagite original Pokémon bhi banaishe.

English: In that game players were able to catch , train and fight against little monsters in a virtual world.
Nagamese: Etu game te, players khan virtual dunia te chutu chutu monsters khan ke dhori bole, train kori bole aru fight kurishe.

English: Especially those who have played the original version are now the ones who are attracted to Pokémon Go.
Nagamese: Juntu manu khan etu original version khelishe etiya etu Pokémon Go te attract hoishe.

English: While the app has turned into a social phenomenon there has also been negative news.
Nagamese: Etu app social phenomenon banaishe logote negative news bhi ahishe.

English: There have been reports of people being lured into places and robbed or players accidentally running into cars and getting hit while following a Pokémon.
Nagamese: Khabar ase ki manu khan ke lura kori bole ekta jaga te loi jai aru chori kore ya players khan Pokémon pichete jaishe aru accident hoi bole gari logote takkar marishe.

English: The new law also prohibits blind and double amputee climbers from trying to reach the top peaks.
Nagamese: Nai kanoon te andha aru duita hath nai manu khan Everest top te jabo nemili.

English: More than 200 people have died in an attempt to reach Mount Everest, the world's highest mountain, since 1920.
Nagamese: 1920 pora 200 manu pora bhi besi morishe Everest top te pojibo nemite, etu dunia te subo dangor pahar ase.

English: The majority of deaths have occurred within the last 40 years.
Nagamese: Besi moroni khan pichla 40 saal bhitor te hoishe.

English: Recently, an 85-year-old mountaineer died in an attempt to be the oldest human to reach the top of Mount Everest. Two Europeans died while making a solo climb last spring.
Nagamese: Pichla time te, ekta 85 saal manu morishe Everest te subo nemite aru etu manu subo purana hobo nemite top te.

English: Although mountaineers die for a number of reasons, almost every fifth death is caused by acute mountain sickness.
Nagamese: Dui ta European manu morishe jababole time te pichla spring season te.

English: Authorities have announced that they will check medical certificates of climbers to see if they are physically capable of such a demanding task.
Nagamese: Mountaineers khan besi reason pora more, lekin almost har ek fifth moroni acute mountain sickness pora hoi.

English: In addition to more safety, Nepalese authorities hope that the new law will createmore jobs for mountain guides in the country.
Nagamese: Authority khan janaishe ki tai khan climbers khan ke medical certificate check kurishe.

English: The government will also give Everest climbing certificates to high altitude guides and workers hired by foreign climbers.
Nagamese: Najaanishe taikhan physically capable ase ki nai etu kam koribo nemite.

English: Local citizens have welcomed the new law, but some officials fear that banning physically handicapped people from climbing could get them into conflict with human rights organisations.
Nagamese: Safety logote, Nepalese authority khan asha kurishe ki etu kanoon pora mountain guide khan nimite aru besi chakri hobo.

English: According to statistics, 4,800 climbers have reached the top of Mount Everest since Tenzing Norgay and Sir Edmund Hillary's historic climb in 1953..
Nagamese: Government Everest climbing certificate bhi dibo juntu guide aru workers foreign climber khan loi ase.

English: The Himalayas are mountains that are located in southern Asia.
Nagamese: Local manu khan etu nawa kanoon ke welcome kurishe, lekin kunba officer khan dhorai ase ki physically handicapped manu khan ke bandh kore debole pora conflict hobo pare human rights organisation logote.

English: They are a part of in Nepal, India, Pakistan, Tibet and Bhutan..
Nagamese: Statistics hisab se, 4,800 climbers khan Everest te subishe Tenzing Norgay aru Sir Edmund Hillary climb kurishe 1953 te..

English: the mountains are the highest in the world, reaching almost 9,000 meters above sea level.
Nagamese: 14 pahar toh 8,000 meters dangor ase, titu bhitor te K2, Nanga Parbat aru Mount Everest ase, 8,848 meter dunia te subo ucha pahar ase.

English: The Himalayas separate the Indian subcontinent from the inner part of Asia. The word Himalaya means “home of snow”.
Nagamese: Etu Nepal, India, Pakistan, Tibet aru Bhutan te ase..

English: The Himalayas extend over 1,500 miles (2,400 km) from the Indus Valley in the west to the Brahmaputra Valley in the east.
Nagamese: Himalaya porbot toh Indus Valley para suru kori, west te thaki Brahmaputra Valley, east ta, 1,500 miles (2,400 km) dighol ase.

English: They are between 100 and 250 kilometers wide.
Nagamese: Iku toh 100 aru 250 kilometers width ase.

English: Many of the mountain peaks are sacred to the people who live in the surrounding areas.
Nagamese: Etu porbot khan, asapas te thaka manuh khan nimete bohut dhorom mana jaga ase.

English: Hindu and Buddhist pilgrims go there and pray to God.
Nagamese: Hindu aru Buddhist jatrar khan ahi kene bhagaban ke prarthana kore.

English: The Himalayas belong to the youngest mountain ranges in the world.
Nagamese: Himalaya toh prithibite thaka porbot majot te sobse notun porbot ase.

English: They were formed when the Indian subcontinent, which was originally a part of the southern landmass, drifted to the north and crashed into Asia.
Nagamese: Etu porbot toh India desh, juntu age south side thakise, uttar dike ahi Asia logot takraise khanar pora hoise.

English: This movement started about 70 million years ago and has been going on up to the present day.
Nagamese: Eku judai prai 70 million bosor agot suru hoise aru aji bhi chali ase.

English: The Himalayas are still becoming taller, moving at about 7 cm per year. Earthquakes and volcanoes are evidence that the area is still very active.
Nagamese: Himalaya aji bhi dhire dhire dangor hoise ase, ek bosor te prai 7 cm kore uthi ase.

English: Glaciers and permanent snow fields cover the higher regions of the Himalayas.
Nagamese: kompon aru agni thaka porbot khan pora proman hoi ki etu jaga aji bhi jinda ase.

English: They are the source of streams that flow into the two big rivers of the region.
Nagamese: borof aru hamesha thanda thaka jaga khan ycha jaga khan ke dhaki ase abarun kori ase.

English: The Indus flows to the west and through Pakistan into the Arabia Sea.
Nagamese: Etu khan para pani ulai kene etu jaga dukhon nodi te jai.

English: The Ganges and Brahmaputra flow to the east and get together in Bangladesh.
Nagamese: Indus nodi pahsim dike bohi Pakistan para hoi kene Arabia sea te jai.

English: They form  the largest delta in the world.
Nagamese: Ganges aru Brahmaputra nodi purab dike bohi Bangladesh te log hoi.

English: Almost every type of climate can be found in the different altitudes of the mountain range.
Nagamese: Duio mili kene sobse dangor delta bonal.

English: The lower slopes in the south are home to tropical plants and tea.
Nagamese: eyate biswas rakone, sob rakam climate porbot te ucha jaga hisap te mile.

English: Trees grow up to a height of 4000 meters.
Nagamese: south side te taltikha jaga khan te jangal aru cha gach ase.

English: Wheat and other cereals are grown in higher regions.
Nagamese: Gach khan 4000 meters tak ucha hoi.

English: The Himalayas influence the climate in both India and Tibet.
Nagamese: Gehu aru dusra chawal usa jaga khan te boi.

English: They form a barrier for monsoon winds that blow from the Indian Ocean over India.
Nagamese: Himalaya porbot toh India aru Tibet dukhon jaga climate te asar kore.

English: On the front side of the mountains it rains heavily while dry air blows across the plains of Tibet.
Nagamese: Etu porbot toh monsoon hawa ke India oporedi bharatiya mahasagar para aha ke roke.

English: The Himalayas are very sparsely populated because of the harsh climate.
Nagamese: Porbot ke samne te bohut pani die, jabole dry hawa Tibet te bohi.

English: Most people live on the lower Indian slopes.
Nagamese: Beshi manu toh India laga tala slope te thake.

English: Many people earn their living as sherpas, guiding tourists and mountaineers to the peaks of the mountain range.
Nagamese: Anek manu sherpa hisab te kaam koreh, tourist aru pahar chora manu khan ke pahar usum te rasta dekhai diye.

English: Most of the passes that cross the Himalayas are over 5,000 high.
Nagamese: Himalaya par koribole rasta khan 5,000 usum uporte ase.

English: They are covered with snow during the winter period and almost impossible to pass.
Nagamese: Etu khan burf pora dhaki thake winter time te aru par koribole ekdom napare.

English: Mountain climbing has become the main tourist activity in the Himalaya Mountains.
Nagamese: Pahar choribo toh Himalaya pahar usum te tourist khan laga dangor kaam hoise.

English: It started towards the end of the 19th century when many mountaineers started climbing the peaks.
Nagamese: Etu 19th century shesh te shuru hoishe jetiya anek pahar chora manu khan usum chora shuru kurishe.

English: In 1953 Edmund Hillary and Sherpa Tenzing Norgay were the first to reach Mount Everest, the world’s highest mountain.
Nagamese: 1953 te Edmund Hillary aru Sherpa Tenzing Norgay first manu thakishe Mount Everest, dunia laga usum pahar, te pouchibole.

English: India is an Asian subcontinent that reaches into the Indian Ocean.
Nagamese: India ekta Asian subcontinent ase jun Indian Ocean bhitor te jaise.

English: With over one billion people it is the second most populous country in the world.
Nagamese: Ek billion uporte manu thakileh etu dunia te second number te beshi manu thaka desh ase.

English: India is a land full of contrast, from the snow-capped peaks of the Himalaya Mountains in the north to the tropical forests of southern India.
Nagamese: India ekta desh ase jun bhinna bhinna dikhai diye, jine Himalaya pahar laga burf pora dhaka usum north te aru southern India laga jungle khan.

English: Although most people speak English there are many dialects and languages next to Hindi, India’s national language.
Nagamese: Hala ki beshi manu toh English te kotha koreh, khite anek bhasa aru boli ase Hindi sobko India laga national bhasa hisab te jane.

English: Over half a century after it had become independent from Great Britain, India has become one of Asia’s leading powers.
Nagamese: Adha century uporte hoishe Great Britain pora azaad huwa pichete bhi, India Asia laga sobko dangor desh khan majote ekta hoise.

English: However the gap between rich and poor is still present and many millions of Indians live in extreme poverty.
Nagamese: Kilebi ameer aru gorib manu majote gap toh abi bhi ase aru lakh lakh Indian manu khan toh ekdom gorib te jibon jiyekena ase.

English: Japan has agreed to help India build its first bullet train.
Nagamese: Japan raji hoishe India ke taikhan laga first bullet train bonabole madad koribole.

English: An Indian version of Japan's high-speed train, the Shinkansen, will travel on a route between Mumbai and Ahmedabad, reducing the travelling time from 7 hours to two hours.
Nagamese: Japan laga high-speed train, Shinkansen, jine ekta Indian version toh Mumbai aru Ahmedabad majote cholibo, aru ja bole time 7 ghanta pora kaamti hoi dui ghanta koridibo.

English: The joint venture between Japan and India is the start of a government program that will improve India's railways and  speed up travelling.
Nagamese: Japan aru India ek logote bonai diya kaam toh ekta government program laga shuruwat ase jun India laga railways ke thik koribo aru ja bole time bhi komti koribo.

English: Japan has promised to  give India 12 billion dollars in loans to finance the train and provide the necessary technology.
Nagamese: Japan dhar dibole raji hoishe India ke 12 billion dollars train bonabole aru darkar huwa technology dibole.

English: With the signing of the agreement Japan has beaten China, which also wants to become India's partner in building the subcontinent's high speed rail system.
Nagamese: Agreement sign kora pichete Japan toh China ke haraidise, kelemane China bhi India laga partner bonibo mangishe subcontinent laga high speed rail system bonabole.

English: China is currently helping India in other ways.
Nagamese: China toh India ke dusra tarika te madad kori ase.

English: They are training Indian railway engineers and  setting up a railway university .
Nagamese: Taikhan Indian railway engineer khan ke training di ase aru railway university bhi banaidi ase.

English: According to experts Japan won the deal to provide high-speed trains, because the Shinkansen is fast and reliable.
Nagamese: Expert khanekora hisap thakile Japan para high-speed train bana bole nimite deal paise, kelemane Shinkansen to bisi dighol aru biswas koribo para ase.

English: Other foreign companies are competing to sell  technology for India's transportation system.
Nagamese: Dusra desh khaneka company para India transportation system nimite technology bikri koribo lagi competition kuri ase.

English: France and Spain are conducting surveys to examine how high-speed trains would affect passenger travel in India.
Nagamese: France aru Spain para survey kuri ase high-speed train para India manu khanikhan ke ki dukh dibo.

English: Apart from the bullet train agreement , Japan and India have announced that they will cooperate in defence and other areas.
Nagamese: Bullet train agreement para alaga Japan aru India para janaise ki taikhan para defence aru dusra jaga te bhi milikena kam koribo.

English: Construction of the bullet train will begin  in 2017 and be completed five years later.
Nagamese: Bullet train bana kam 2017 te suru hobo aru paanch saal piche te khatam hobo.

English: Over the next decade, the Indian government plans to spend over 100 billion dollars to modernize a railway network, that dates back to the 19th century when the British built the major lines.
Nagamese: Agami dossa saal bhitor te, India sarkar para 100 billion dollar bhi bisi kharcha koribo railway khan ke nawa bana bole nimite, juntu to 19th century te British para banaishe.

English: Researchers at the John Hopkins University in Baltimore have announced that they are close to developing a new blood test that could detect common cancer forms.
Nagamese: Researchers John Hopkins University Baltimore para janaise ki taikhan para eku nawa blood test bana bole nimite ekdom najuk ase juntu para cancer forms khan ke dhoribo pare.

English: The new test, called  Cancer SEEK ,  would cost about $500 and be able to identify early forms of cancer cells in the body.
Nagamese: Nawa test, juntu ke Cancer SEEK bole, taake nimite $500 dorkar hobo aru body bhitor cancer cell khaniku jaldi te chinibo paribo.

English: According to the scientists, the test concentrates on the most frequent types of cancers including lung, breast, colon,  and stomach cancer.
Nagamese: Scientist khanikhan hisap thakile, itu test para dhyan dibo cancer khanikhan juntu bisi hoi ase, jene ki lung, breast, colon, aru stomach cancer.

English: After testing about  1000 people diagnosed with these cancer forms the new blood test found signs of cancer in 70% of them.
Nagamese: 1000 manu khanikhan ke test kura piche te juntu khan ke cancer hoi ase, itu nawa blood test para 70% manu khanite cancer thaka sign paishe.

English: Tumours release tiny amounts of altered DNA into the bloodstream.
Nagamese: Tumours para thora thora DNA bloodstream te chari dibo.

English: The test looks for mutations in 16 genes that frequently come up in cancer.
Nagamese: Itu test para 16 genes te dhyan dibo juntu cancer te dikhai diye.

English: Although the first tests have had promising results there are still problems that have to be dealt with.
Nagamese: Jabole first test para bhal result dikhaise taahile bhi dukh ase juntu thek koribo lage.
