English: There is still a high rate of false alarms, where cancer is shown in patients who are not diagnosed with any disease.
Nagamese: Etu te bisi chance ase jhuta alarm bajibo, juntu manu khan ke cancer nai taake dikhaibo.

English: Another problem is that the test sometimes shows signs of cancer but cannot pinpoint where exactly in the body they appear.
Nagamese: Dusra dukh itu ase ki itu test para cancer sign toh dikhaibo lekin kuntu jaga te ase bole exact dikhaibo napare.

English: Doctors state that the new blood test could be a major breakthrough for cancer patients and can extend their lives.
Nagamese: Doctor khanikhan janaise ki itu nawa blood test para cancer thaka manu khanikhan nimite bisi bhal hobo aru taikhan jibon bhi bisi din tak bachi thakibo pare.

English: The earlier a cancer is found and located the better the chances are for it to be treated.
Nagamese: Jitna jaldi cancer ke paibo aru kuntu jaga te ase bole janibo utna bhal asse taake thek koribo nimite.

English: The ultimate goal is to find cancer at early stages of the illness, at a time when patients can be treated successfully.
Nagamese: Last goal itu ase jaldi te cancer ke paibo, time thakute jitia manu khan ke bhal se thek koribo pari bole.

English: In some forms of cancer, for example, pancreatic cancer, symptoms show up in the late stages of the illness.
Nagamese: Kuntu cancer form khanite, misal nimite, pancreatic cancer, symptoms piche te dikhai diye.

English: As a result, four out of five patients die shortly after it has been diagnosed.
Nagamese: Etu nimite, chaar jon bhitor ekjon manu mori jay bole pata lage pichete.

English: Blood is an important fluid that keeps us alive.
Nagamese: Blood itu bisi dorkari pani ase juntu para amikhan bachi thake.

English: We cannot live without it.
Nagamese: Ami etu nathakile najibo.

English: The heart pumps blood to all parts of the body and brings them oxygen and food. At the same time blood carries all the substances we don’t need away from us.
Nagamese: Heart toh body para blood pump kore aru oxygen, khana loi jai. Etu time te blood toh dorkar nalaga bostu khan bhi loi anibo.

English: Blood fights infections, keeps our body temperature the same and carries chemicals that control body functions. vessels so that we don’t bleed to death.
Nagamese: Blood toh infections logot fight kore, body temperature ek rakhe aru chemicals boi boi body toh control kore.

English: Finally, blood has substances that repair broken blood
Nagamese: Last te, blood te eneka bostu asey juntu pora ketia blood vessel bhangile bhi blood najai.

English: Blood is a mixture of fluid and solid matter.
Nagamese: Blood toh pani aru nimati bostu mili thake.

English: Plasma is the liquid part of our blood. It makes up about 50 – 60 % of it.
Nagamese: Plasma toh blood pani part. Eku mane 50-60% etu thake.

English: Plasma consists mostly of water but many other substances are in it.
Nagamese: Plasma beshi pani asey kintu aru বস্তু bhi asey.

English: It contains dissolved food, chemicals that control our growth and do other jobs, proteins, minerals and waste products.
Nagamese: Etu bhitor te khana, chemicals juntu pora ami dangar hoi aru aru kaam bhi kore, proteins, minerals aru waste বস্তু thake.

English: Red blood cells look like flat round discs.
Nagamese: Red blood cells toh chapta round disc nisena dikhe.

English: They contain haemoglobin, a protein that carries oxygen to the body and gives blood its red colour.
Nagamese: Etu bhitor te haemoglobin thake, ekta protein juntu oxygen body te boi kene loi jai aru blood ke ronga colour diye.

English: Each drop of blood has about 300 million of these red cells.
Nagamese: Ekdam chutu blood te bhi 300 million red cells thake.

English: White blood cells, also called leukocytes, fight infections and harmful substances that invade the body.
Nagamese: White blood cells, jukhu leukocytes bhi koi, infections aru kharap বস্তু logot fight kore juntu body te dhuke.

English: Most of these cells are round and colourless.
Nagamese: Beshi cells toh round aru colour nadikhe.

English: They have different sizes and shapes.
Nagamese: Etu khan different size aru shape te asey.

English: White blood cells are not as numerous as red ones.
Nagamese: White blood cells toh red cells nisena besi nai.

English: For every 700 red blood cells there is only one white blood cell.
Nagamese: Jetiya 700 red blood cells thake, tetiya ekta white blood cell thake.

English: Platelets are tiny bodies that are much smaller than red blood cells.
Nagamese: Platelets toh ekdom chutu body asey jukhu red blood cells pora bhi dukhun chutu asey.

English: They stick to the edges of a cut and form blood clots to stop bleeding.
Nagamese: Etu khan kata jaga te lagi jai aru blood clot bole aru blood jua bandh kore.

English: The blood of a normal adult has about 2 trillion platelets.
Nagamese: Normal manu body te 2 trillion platelets thake.

English: The circulatory system carries blood to all parts of your body.
Nagamese: Circulatory system pora blood para body ke sob jaga te boi boi loi jai.

English: The heart pumps blood through big blood vessels called arteries and veins.
Nagamese: Dil toh blood ke arteries aur veins naam ka bada vessels dwara pump kore.

English: In our body there are also millions of small blood vessels called capillaries.
Nagamese: Amaar body te millions chhota blood vessels bhi ase, kebole capillaries.

English: Oxygen, food and other substances pass through the thin walls of these capillaries into the tissue.
Nagamese: Oxygen, khana aur dusra chij etu capillaries ke patla deewar se tissue te jae.

English: When you inhale air oxygen passes through your lungs and and is picked up by haemoglobin which transports it to your whole body.
Nagamese: Tum jab hawa andhar loi, oxygen tumar lungs se cross hoi aur haemoglobin naam ka chij loi, etu tumar pura body te transport kore.

English: It is released into cells which produce energy.
Nagamese: Itu cells te release hoi aur energy banae.

English: In return cells produce carbon dioxide which enters your blood stream and is transported back to your lungs where it is exhaled.
Nagamese: Badla te cells carbon dioxide banae, etu tumar blood stream te enter hoi aur tumar lungs te wapas transport hoi jaha se tum exhale kore.

English: Food also reaches your body by means of blood.
Nagamese: Khana bhi blood se tumar body tak poche.

English: It is digested in your stomach and important substances like fat, sugar, proteins, vitamins and minerals are separated.
Nagamese: Itu tumar stomach te digest hoi aur important chij jaise fat, sugar, proteins, vitamins aur minerals separate hoi.

English: These nutrients enter your blood stream and are moved to the cells and muscles where they are needed in order to give you energy or fuel.
Nagamese: Eitu nutrients tumar blood stream te enter hoi aur cells aur muscles tak move hoi jaha unko energy dene ke liye darkar ase.

English: The work of the muscles and other tissue creates heat.
Nagamese: Muscles aur tissue ka kaam heat create kore.

English: Blood is the transporting system which carries heat throughout your body and warms you.
Nagamese: Blood transporting system ase, etu heat pura body te carry kore aur tumko garam rakhe.

English: The things that you don’t need are transported to your intestines and kidneys and leave your body again.
Nagamese: Jo chij tumko nehi darkar, etu tumar intestines aur kidneys tak transport hoi aur body se bahar nikal jae.

English: White blood cells play an important role in your immune system.
Nagamese: White blood cells tumar immune system te important role play kore.

English: When harmful substances invade your body an alarm goes off and white blood cells are activated
Nagamese: Jab harmful chij tumar body te ghus jae, alarm baje aur white blood cells activate hoi.

English: Then they work to destroy the invaders.
Nagamese: Tab unlog invaders ko destroy karne ke liye kaam kore.

English: They fight off viruses, harmful bacteria and begin anti-body production.
Nagamese: Unlog viruses, harmful bacteria se lare aur anti-body production start kore.

English: Blood also carries hormones to places where they are needed.
Nagamese: Blood hormones bhi places tak carry kore jaha unko darkar ase.

English: When a hormone reaches a part of the body it controls growth, how the body uses food and other things.
Nagamese: Jab ek hormone body ka part tak poche, etu growth control kore, body kaise khana aur dusra chij use kore.

English: You would bleed to death from a small cut if your blood didn’t clot.
Nagamese: Agar tumar blood clot nehi hoi toh tum ek chhota cut se bhi bleed to death hoijibo.

English: When a blood vessel breaks platelets rush to the damaged area and stick to one another , forming a plug.
Nagamese: Jitia khoon najor dhorile, platelets khan damage hoijoa jaga te joldi jai ar eklogot lage, plug nisina bonai dei.

English: Blood cells come from bone marrow.
Nagamese: Khoon or cells khan bone marrow pora ahhe.

English: They begin as stem cells and then develop into red or white blood cells, or platelets.
Nagamese: Etu stem cells hisape shuru hoi, tar pisot red or white blood cells, or platelets hoi.

English: They don’t live forever and must be replaced by new ones.
Nagamese: Etu khan hamesha thaki najai, aru notun pora bonabo lage.

English: Red blood cells live an average of 120 days before wearing out.
Nagamese: Red blood cells khan jibon 120 din tak thake wear out huijoa age te.

English: Then they are captured and destroyed in the liver and spleen.
Nagamese: Titia etu khan ke dhori loi aru liver aru spleen te nosto kori dei.

English: Platelets live only for about 10 days.
Nagamese: Platelets khan 10 din tak he thake.

English: The amount of blood in your body depends on your size, weight and the altitude at which you live.
Nagamese: Tumikhan or body te kitiman khoon thakibo etu tumi khan or size, weight aru altitude, tumi kun jaga te thake etu upor te depend kore.

English: An adult who weighs 80 kg has about 5 litres of blood, a 40 kg child about half the amount.
Nagamese: Ekjon burha manu juntu 80 kg ase taikhan logot 5 litres khoon thake, ekjon 40 kg bacha manu logot adha thake.

English: People who live in high areas where the air is thinner need more blood to deliver more oxygen to the body.
Nagamese: Juntu manu dhong jaga te thake hawa patla thakile taikhan ke besi khoon lage oxygen dibo nimite.

English: If an adult suddenly loses a litre or more of blood he may die unless the blood in his body can be replaced.
Nagamese: Jodi ekjon burha manu ek litre pora besi khoon harai dile taikhan mori jabo pare jodi taikhan or body te khoon nahomile.

English: Over the years blood transfusions have saved countless lives.
Nagamese: Pichla sal te khoon transfusion pora olop bhi manu khan ke bachai dise.

English: Transfusions can also help patients who cannot produce enough blood cells to survive.
Nagamese: Transfusions pora bhi kun manu khan enough blood cells bonabo napare taikhan ke bhi help kore bachibo nimite.

English: They also help during operations when patients lose some blood.
Nagamese: Etu operation time te bhi help kore kun manu khan olop khoon harai dise.

English: Blood banks collect blood from donors and put it in sterile bags.
Nagamese: Blood banks manu khan pora donor pora khoon collect kore aru sterile bags te rakhe.

English: It is cooled down and can be stored for up to 50 days.
Nagamese: Etu thanda kore aru 50 din tak store kori pare.

English: Laboratory workers screen blood for infectious diseases like AIDS and hepatitis.
Nagamese: Laboratory workers khan khoon ke infectious diseases khan ke screen kore jineka AIDS aru hepatitis.

English: Only clean and safe blood can be given to patients.
Nagamese: Only safa aru bhal khoon manu khan ke dibo pare.

English: When a person suffers from anaemia there are not enough red blood cells to supply the body with the oxygen he needs.
Nagamese: Jitia ekjon manu anaemia pora duk pai, taikhan logot enough red blood cells nai etu nimite taikhan ke oxygen supply dibo nimite.

English: Leukaemia is a kind of cancer of the bone marrow, in which not enough or abnormal white blood cells are produced.
Nagamese: Leukaemia ek rakam cancer ase bone marrow or, etu te enough or abnormal white blood cells bona najai.

English: Without white blood cells diseases can enter your body without being controlled.
Nagamese: White blood cell nahothakle, bemar bur body bhitor te ahikena control nathakibo.

English: When your body does not have enough platelets blood cannot clot well.
Nagamese: Body te platelets komti hole khoon bhal se joma nahobo.

English: Even small injuries can lead to a loss of blood because bleeding doesn’t stop.
Nagamese: Saan saan injury pora bhi khoon jaabo pare kelemane khoon beruwa bandh nohoi.

English: The human heart is a muscle that lies left of the chest .
Nagamese: Manuh khanika heart ekta muscle ase, etu chest laga left side te thake.

English: It has about the size of a fist .
Nagamese: Etu ekta mutthi jiman dangor ase.

English: The heart is like a pump that sends blood around your body.
Nagamese: Heart ekta pump nisena ase, etu khoon pura body te pathai diye.

English: The blood gives you the oxygen you need .
Nagamese: Khoone tumikhan ke oxygen diye jun tumikhan ke darkar ase.

English: An average heart pumps about 70 millilitres of blood into your body with every beat .
Nagamese: Ekta normal heart pura minute te 70 millilitres khoon pump kore.

English: That’s about 5 litres every minute or about 7200 litres every day.
Nagamese: Minute te 5 litres or din pora 7200 litres pump kore.

English: The heart is made up of 4 chambers .
Nagamese: Heart chata chamber pora bana ase.

English: There are two chambers on each side of the heart.
Nagamese: Heart laga har side te dui ta chamber ase.

English: The top two chambers are called the atria.
Nagamese: Uporte thaka dui ta chamber ke atria bole.

English: They fill with blood.
Nagamese: Taikhan khoon pora bhorti hoi.

English: The two chambers at the bottom of the heart are called ventricles.
Nagamese: Tole te thaka dui ta chamber ke ventricles bole.

English: Their job is to squirt out the blood.
Nagamese: Ta laga kaam khoon daka mari kena ber kora.

English: In the middle of the heart there is a thick muscle called the septum that separates the left and the right side.
Nagamese: Heart laga maj te ekta mota muscle ase jun ke septum bole, etu left aur right side ke alag kore.

English: The atria and ventricles work together.
Nagamese: Atria aur ventricles ek logote kam kore.

English: The atria fill with blood and when they are full they let the blood into the ventricles.
Nagamese: Atria khoon pora bhorti hoi aur jitia taikhan bhorti hoi, taikhan ventricles ke khoon diye.

English: When these ventricles pump the blood out of the heart, the atria fill up again and the whole process starts again.
Nagamese: Jitia eitu ventricles khoon ke heart pora pump kore, atria pher bhorti hoi jabo aur pura process pher shuru hoi jabo.

English: There are valves in the heart that make sure that the blood is pumped in the right direction.
Nagamese: Heart bhitor te valves ase etu sure kore khoon thik disha te pump huwa.

English: When the heart contracts , it pumps blood from its chambers into the body.
Nagamese: Jetiya heart toh contract kore, etu manu body bhitor te chamber pora blood pump kore.

English: Then the heart muscle relaxes and lets the chambers fill up.
Nagamese: Tar pisot heart muscle toh relax hoi aaru chamber ke fill up hobo dei.

English: Every cell in your body needs oxygen to live.
Nagamese: Tumi body t thaka prottek cell ke jinda thakibo nimite  oxygen lage.

English: The role of the heart is to send blood that is full of oxygen to all the cells in your body.
Nagamese: Heart or kaam to holo oxygen thaka  blood tumi body or sob cell  te pathai dibo lage

English: The passageways that carry this blood are called blood vessels.
Nagamese: Juntu rastate etu blood jai etu ke blood vessels bole.

English: Arteries carry oxygen-rich blood to the cells that need it.
Nagamese: Artery khan oxygen thaka  blood cell ke pathai juntu ke etu lage.

English: The largest artery is the aorta. Veins carry blood back to the heart again.
Nagamese: Sobse dangor artery toh aorta ase. Vein khan blood ke heart te ulta pathai diye.

English: It is pumped into your lungs where it picks up oxygen and then goes back to your heart, from where it is pumped back again into your body.
Nagamese: Etu blood lungs te pump kore jotha pora etu oxygen loi aaru phir heart te jai, jotha pora etu phir tumi body te pump hoi jai.

English: It only takes about 20 seconds to pump blood to every cell of your body.
Nagamese: Tumi body or prottek cell te blood pump koribo nimite matro 20 seconds lage.

English: Even though your heart is inside you, you can see it work by feeling your pulse.
Nagamese: Heart tumi bhitor te thakile bhi, tumi etu kaam kori thaka feel koribo pare tumi pulse feel kore

English: You can find your pulse in many parts of your body.
Nagamese: Tumi tumi pulse to tumi body or alag alag jagah te pabo pare.

English: It’s best to press your finger on the inside of your hand, just below the thumb.
Nagamese: Sobse bhal hoi tumi hath bhitor side te anguli dhuri bole, just thumb nise.

English: You can feel a small beat under your skin.
Nagamese: Tumi eti chhoto dhuk dhuk feel koribo tumi skin nise.

English: This is caused by the contraction of your heart.
Nagamese: Eto heart contraction kora karon hoi.

English: When you rest or sleep your heart does not beat that fast—about 60 to 80 beats a minute.
Nagamese: Jetiya tumi rest kore ba ghumai  tumi heart toh etu joldi beat nokore — moramoti 60 pora 80 beats priti minite.

English: When you run around a lot your heart pumps more blood into your body—maybe up to 200 times a minute.
Nagamese: Jetiya tumi douriye khele  tumi heart toh besi blood pump kore  tumi body te— bodh hoi 200 bar protiminite.

English: As people grow older their heart rates change.
Nagamese: Jetiya manuh  dhire dhire dangor hoi, taikhan heart rate toh change hoi.

English: A newborn baby has a heart rate of about 130, a three-year old has about 100 and an eight-year old’s heart beats about 90 times a minute.
Nagamese: Eti notun jonma baby or heart rate toh about 130 thake, tin bosor old or heart 100 thake aaru asht bosor old manu  heart toh 90bar proti minite beat kore.

English: An adult has a heart rate of about 70 to 80.
Nagamese: Eti adult manu or heart rate toh about 70 pora 80 thake.

English: The older you get the slower your heart beats.
Nagamese: Jetiya tumi burha hoi tumi heart beats toh dhire hoi jai.

English: Your heart is probably the most important part of your body.
Nagamese: Tumi heart toh boda tumi body or important part ase.

English: If it stops pumping blood you cannot survive .
Nagamese: Jodi khoon pump koribo bandh kori diye, tumi bachibo naparibo.

English: The greatest danger for your heart is a heart attack.
Nagamese: Tumi laga dil (heart) nimite sobse danger to heart attack he ase.

English: It happens suddenly and is like an explosion
Nagamese: Etu ekaebekhe hoi, aru etu ekta explosion nisina ase.

English: When the heart stops pumping blood, muscles must die because they cannot get any oxygen.
Nagamese: Jitia dil khoon pump koribo bandh kore, muscles moribo lagey kelemane ke etu khan oxygen napai.

English: In some cases blood is not able to flow through the blood vessels because they are clogged up.
Nagamese: Kikuman time-ot, khoon khoon noli bhitor-thi jabo napare kelemane ke etu jam (clogged) hoi jai.

English: So the heart does not get enough blood to work.
Nagamese: Etileke dil kam koribo nimite pura khoon napai.

English: Chest pain may be a possible warning sign of an upcoming heart attack.
Nagamese: Chest dukhile tumi hosiyaar hobo parey ke tumi nimite heart attack ahi ase.

English: According to the World Health Organization over a billion adults around the world suffer from high blood pressure.
Nagamese: World Health Organization hisab-thi, duniate ek billion manu high blood pressure-thi duk paiche.

English: It is now taking steps to fight against what is called a silent killer.
Nagamese: Etia etu step loi ase ekta silent killer opor-thi juj koribo nimite.

English: High blood pressure can lead to strokes and a heart attack.
Nagamese: High blood pressure-thi stroke aru heart attack hobo parey.

English: Almost 10 million people die of these two diseases every year.
Nagamese: Almost 10 million manu etu dui ta bemar nimite mori jai har saal.

English: They are the number one causes of death globally.
Nagamese: Etu khan sobse dangor bemar ase duniate manu moribo nimite.

English: High blood pressure also leads to kidney failure, blindness and other illnesses.
Nagamese: High blood pressure-thi kidney kharab hobo parey, dikhibo napare aru dusra bemar bhi hobo parey.

English: It often goes hand in hand with diabetes and obesity, which increases heath risks even further.
Nagamese: Etu majority time-ot diabetes aru obesity logote ahe, etu khan bemar bhi aru besi danger hoi diye.

English: As the WHO reports, people in developing countries in Africa suffer more from high blood pressure than people in developed countries.
Nagamese: Jitia WHO report kore, Africa develop nahua deshot manu khan besi high blood pressure-thi duk pai thake develop hua desh opor-thi.

English: The reason is that health care in the developed world is much better organized and people go to the doctor soon enough.
Nagamese: Etu nimite ke develop hua desh-ot health care besi bhal ase aru manu khan jaldi doctor usorte jai.

English: High blood pressure should never be ignored and always taken seriously.
Nagamese: High blood pressure ke ketiabi ignore nakoribo aru hamesha serious lobi.

English: Although people may live with high blood pressure for years without even knowing it, it is important to check your blood pressure regularly and take the necessary steps.
Nagamese: Hoile bhi manu khan high blood pressure-thi bohut saal juwan parey najanikena, etu important ase ke tumi laga khoon pressure har time check kori thakibi aru sob step lobi juntu dorkar ase.

English: It is a warning sign from your body that something is not OK.
Nagamese: Etu tumi laga shareer-thi ekta warning ase ke etu theek nahoi ase.

English: The WHO says that you can reduce your blood pressure by changing your living habits.
Nagamese: WHO ko hisab te, tumi khan nijor jibon juwar to change kori kene blood pressure komti koribo pare.

English: Eating a balanced diet, exercising regularly, avoiding alcohol and nicotine are some things that you can do without consulting a doctor.
Nagamese: Balanced diet khawa, regularly exercise kora, aru alcohol aro nicotine pura puri napiba.

English: The body is an amazing object; the more you ask of it, the more it can do.
Nagamese: Etu khan sob doctor logote kotha nokorikena kori bole pare.

English: If you are inactive most of the time, your body will quickly become tired when it should be active.
Nagamese: Body ta ekdom dangor chiz ase; jitna tumi body ke koribo di bi, body heto koribo pare.

English: But if you are active many times each day or week, your body will get used to the hard work and it will become easier to do.
Nagamese: Jodi tumi beshi time kam nakori kene thake, tumi body joldi thaki jabole, etu time te active thakibo lage.

English: Children who want to play active games with their friends should exercise regularly so they have the energy to play as long as they want.
Nagamese: Kintu jodi tumi din aro hafta te beshi bar active thake, tumi body heto kam te aadat hoi jabo aru koribo nimite asaan hoi jabo.

English: Many young people want to be physically active because it helps them look and feel good.
Nagamese: Jun bacha khan friend logote active games khelibo lage, etu khan regularly exercise koribo lage, etu dwara energy thakibo aru jitna time tak khelibo mon ase, heto time tak khelibo pare.

English: Activity burns more calories than just sitting , so it is important for keeping a healthy weight .
Nagamese: Beshi jawan manu khan physically active thakibo lage, kelemane etu khan dikhibo nimite aro feel koribo nimite bhi bhal lage.

English: Exercise also makes your muscles and bones stronger and keeps your body in a good condition .
Nagamese: Active thakile beshi calories jole tumi khan bosi thakile to, etu nimite healthy weight maintain koribo nimite important ase.

English: Physical activity also helps people stay healthy.
Nagamese: Exercise kori kene tumi khan nijor muscles aro haddi khan bhi strong banai aru tumi khan nijor body ke bhi bhal condition te rakhe.

English: Exercise strengthens the immune system , and helps the body fight off illness .
Nagamese: Physical activity dwara manu khan healthy bhi thake.

English: Daily exercise also helps people sleep better.
Nagamese: Exercise kori kena immune system bhi strong hoi, aru body ke bimari logote laribo nimite bhi madad kore.

English: Active students don’t complain about being tired.
Nagamese: Din din exercise kori kene manu khan bhal nind bhi pai.

English: They have a good night’s sleep and have the energy to work hard and have fun every day.
Nagamese: Jun student khan active ase, etu khan nalage moi thaki ase.

English: Strength is the force that muscles use. People used to think only boys could be strong, but that is not true.
Nagamese: Etu khan bhal nind pai aru energy bhi thake kam koribo nimite aru maja koribo nimite bhi every day.

English: All people who want to be active and healthy must be strong. Strength training keeps your bones strong.
Nagamese: Strength to muscles dwara force use kora ase.

English: People who have not been very active in the past should begin slowly.
Nagamese: Manu khan age te bhabona kori thakise sirf laki khan he strong hobo pare, kintu etu sotti nohoi.

English: Being strong does not always mean that you have big muscles .
Nagamese: Juntu manu khan active aro healthy thakibo lage etu khan strong homelaga.

English: Muscle size is often determined by your genes-very few people will look like bodybuilders when they get stronger.
Nagamese: Strength training kori kene tumi khan nijor haddi khan strong banai.

English: Age also helps find out muscle size.
Nagamese: Juntu manu khan age te active nathakise etu khan slow slow suru koribo lage.

English: Children of elementary school age will become stronger by doing exercises, but their muscles probably will not grow much bigger .
Nagamese: Strong asena mane nohoi tumi dangor muscles banaibo.

English: Boys may get bigger muscles if they do strength training because their hormones may rise .
Nagamese: Muscle size to tumi khan genes dwara bhi hoi-- bisi manu khan bodybuilders nisena nadikhe strong hobo time te.

English: Girls can also become stronger but they won’t get bigger muscles because their balance of hormones is different from that of males .
Nagamese: Age dwara bhi muscle size to pata lage.

English: To strengthen your muscles , you must make them work harder than usual.
Nagamese: Tumikhan laga muscles ta strong koribo nimite, tumi etu khan ke aadat thaka pora besi kaam koribo dibi.

English: Lifting weights in a gym is not the only way to increase strength .
Nagamese: Gym te weights uthaile he takot bahibo koile ekla hoi jua nohoi.

English: You can build strength just by helping to do things at home like lifting or bringing grocery bags from the car to the kitchen.
Nagamese: Tumi ghor te kaam kori kene bhi takot bahabo pare, jine grocery bag khan gari pora kitchen te anibo.

English: To increase strength , try moving a heavier object than normal.
Nagamese: Takot bahabo nimite, normal thaka pora dhikan bosta uthaibo.

English: This could be a heavier weight or a heavier grocery bag .
Nagamese: Etu dhikan weight bhi hobo pare ya dikan grocery bag bhi hobo pare.

English: After a while it will be easy to move this heavier object.
Nagamese: Thora din piche te etu dhikan bosta uthabo nimite ekdom easy hoi jabo.

English: Anytime an object becomes easy for you to move, you must increase the weight again if you want to continue to get stronger.
Nagamese: Jetiya bhi bosta uthabo nimite easy hoi jai, tumi weight aru bhi bahabo lage jodi tumi aru bhi strong hobo mon ase.

English: Remember, it takes time to become strong.
Nagamese: Yaad rakhibi, strong hobo nimite time lage.

English: But you should increase weights slowly, otherwise your muscles may get hurt.
Nagamese: Kintu tumi weight slow slow kori kene he bahabi, nohoile tumi laga muscles dukhibo pare.

English: Exercises like push-ups and sit-ups will also build strength .
Nagamese: Push-ups aru sit-ups kori kene bhi takot bahabo pare.

English: When you do these exercises, you move the weight of your own body.
Nagamese: Jitia tumi etu exercise khan kore, tumi tumi laga body laga weight here move kore.

English: Although you cannot increase your own weight, you can continue to build strength by always doing more sit-ups or push-ups .
Nagamese: Hala ki tumi tumi laga weight toh bahabo napare, tumi takot bahai thakibo pare sit-ups or push-ups besi kori kene.

English: You must also do the right exercises.
Nagamese: Tumi thik exercise bhi koribo lage.

English: Lifting weights with your arms will not make your leg muscles stronger.
Nagamese: Hat pora weight uthaile tumi laga leg muscles strong nohoi.

English: If you want stronger legs, you must do leg exercises.
Nagamese: Jodi tumi strong legs lage, tumi leg exercise koribo lage.

English: For the best fitness, it is a good idea to work on strengthening all your muscles by doing many different exercises.
Nagamese: Bhal fitness nimite, etu bhal idea ase ki tumi tumi laga muscles khan ke exercise kori kene strong banabi.

English: The lungs bring oxygen to the blood, and the heart pumps the blood through blood vessels to the muscles .
Nagamese: Lungs pora blood te oxygen jai, aru heart pora blood vessels pora muscles tak blood pump hoi jai.

English: Cardiovascular fitness is the body’s ability to bring oxygen to the muscles for a longer time.
Nagamese: Cardiovascular fitness toh body oxygen muscles te besi time tak pathibo paribo ability ase.
